title: Repo foundation
description: Establish the initial Roger Reviewer monorepo structure, operating docs, release and install path, and architecture-spike track so later implementation is grounded in a coherent local-first workspace rather than ad hoc package creation.
acceptance_criteria: Workspace, docs, release or install path, and packaging prerequisites are explicit; core architecture spikes and ADR outcomes are captured as child tasks; extension packaging does not become a hidden blocker for the local product
close_reason: All children closed: README, release artifact matrix, install flow, runtime supervisor defaults, attention-event contract, and architecture spikes. Extension packaging explicitly tracked in rr-007.1 as separate non-blocking work.

title: Add an initial README and curate the first GitHub-facing planning snapshot
description: Create a contributor-facing top-level README for Roger Reviewer, clean up ignore rules for transient local artifacts, and define a lean first commit that captures the planning/docs core without vendoring heavyweight local exploration clones.
close_reason: Completed

title: Define release artifact matrix and CI/CD ownership
description: Specify the supported Roger 0.1.0 artifact classes, target platforms, checksum or signing expectations, and which CI or release jobs own build, packaging, and publication.
acceptance_criteria: macOS, Windows, and Linux artifact classes are explicit; build, checksum or signing, packaging, and publication ownership is assigned; browser-extension artifacts and Native Messaging assets are defined as separate optional release work
close_reason: Defined 0.1.0 artifact classes, target platforms, checksum/signing expectations, and CI/release job ownership in planning docs

title: Define one-line install and update flow for the local product
description: Specify the artifact-driven install and update path for the Roger CLI and local companion surface so local-first usage stays real even when the extension is absent.
acceptance_criteria: One-line install and update flows are explicit; host-platform detection, version selection, and checksum behavior are defined; extension installation stays optional and separate from the local product path
close_reason: Defined one-line local install/update contract, host/version/checksum behavior, and explicit separation from extension/bridge packaging lanes

title: Define in-process runtime supervisor defaults for 0.1.0
description: Resolve the remaining pre-implementation runtime details left open in AGENTS.md and the canonical plan for the accepted in-process Rust TUI/app-core boundary. This bead should set explicit default queue limits, cancellation rules, background job classes, bounded refresh cadence, same-process wake behavior, and cross-process refresh semantics so Phase 1 and later implementation beads do not invent them ad hoc.
close_reason: Superseded by rr-006.2, which scopes the same pre-implementation runtime-policy gap more precisely against the TUI/app-core boundary and downstream implementation beads.

title: Define Roger attention-event and notification contract for 0.1.0
description: Lock the canonical Roger-owned attention-event model before implementation begins. This bead should define the event vocabulary, state-transition rules, producer and consumer responsibilities across TUI, CLI, extension, and optional local notifications, plus truthful behavior when the bridge can launch but cannot read back status.
close_reason: Superseded by rr-006.3, which covers the same attention-event/notification contract with better surface-specific dependencies.

title: Restore a working pinned br command path for repo automation
description: The repo instructions require agents to use br, but the current symlink at /Users/cdilga/.local/bin/br points to a missing /Users/cdilga/.cargo/bin/br binary. This bead restores a working pinned invocation path for the approved 0.1.28 build so automated repo workflows stop depending on backup binary names.
acceptance_criteria:
The default br invocation path used by repo automation resolves to a working pinned binary again
The restored path points to the approved 0.1.28 build or an equivalent repo-documented pinned location rather than an untracked backup filename
Repo instructions and automated workflows no longer depend on broken symlinks or manual backup-binary invocation
close_reason: Restored pinned br invocation via scripts/swarm/br_pinned.sh and updated swarm automation/docs to resolve through ~/.local/bin/br-0.1.28.pinned; validated with scripts/swarm/br_pinned.sh --verify, br --version, and readlink ~/.local/bin/br.

title: Domain and storage
description: Define the durable Roger data model for sessions, runs, findings, artifacts, approval-safe outbound communication, scope/memory overlays, and rebuildable search sidecars before UI work spreads. This epic must lock the canonical local-first store shape, write-ownership rules, and migration boundaries so later CLI, TUI, and extension work do not invent conflicting state models or daemon-like coordination assumptions.
acceptance_criteria:
Core entities, invariants, and aggregate boundaries are explicit for sessions, findings, evidence, drafts, approvals, posted actions, scope, and index state
Write-ownership, row-version or conflict rules, and append-only event-history expectations are defined before concurrent surfaces are implemented
Storage, migration, artifact, and rebuildable search-sidecar boundaries are explicit enough that later work cannot smuggle mutable review state into derived indices or extension-local caches
close_reason: Child beads rr-013 and rr-014 are closed; domain/storage contracts and canonical storage layer are now implemented

title: Harness session orchestration
description: Define and implement Rogers supported-harness linkage so Roger can add structure without breaking plain OpenCode fallback, while keeping Gemini support bounded and truthful and making continuity claims auditable.
acceptance_criteria: Harness capability tiers, session ledger, and fallback or return contracts are explicit; OpenCode primary support is real enough for smoke coverage and Gemini bounded support stays truthful; Roger metadata references underlying provider sessions rather than hiding them
close_reason: All child beads in the harness-session spine are closed (rr-003.1-rr-003.8 plus rr-015), with OpenCode Tier-B continuity/reopen/reseed/dropout-return paths and Gemini Tier-A bounded reseed support implemented and validated by smoke + workspace tests.

title: Implement OpenCode core session linkage and reopen
description: Implement the core OpenCode path Roger is planning around: provider session start, reopen by locator, and ResumeBundle reseed fallback. This is the critical continuity path that unblocks the rest of the local review loop.
acceptance_criteria: OpenCode-backed reviews can start or reopen through a SessionLocator when available and fall back to ResumeBundle reseed when direct reopen is unavailable; smoke coverage proves the reseed path is truthful and does not lose review target identity
close_reason: Implemented OpenCode start/reopen/reseed linkage with truthful target-preserving reseed smoke coverage

title: Implement Gemini bounded adapter and truthful reseed path
description: Implement the bounded Gemini path Roger claims for 0.1.0 without implying OpenCode-level continuity. This bead exists to make Tier A support concrete: Roger owns the continuity ledger, captures raw and structured output, and tells the truth when deeper reopen or in-harness affordances do not exist.
acceptance_criteria:
Gemini-backed reviews can start through Roger and persist both raw output artifacts and structured normalization inputs in the same durable continuity ledger Roger uses for other supported harnesses
Capability reporting stays bounded to the Tier A contract and rejects direct-resume, dropout, or harness-native command claims when unsupported
ResumeBundle reseed works truthfully when native reopen semantics are unavailable or weaker than OpenCode, with no parity theater about direct session reopen
Acceptance coverage proves the bounded Gemini path is useful without parity theater and fails clearly when deeper continuity features are unavailable
close_reason: Implemented Gemini Tier-A bounded adapter with truthful reseed-only continuity path and acceptance coverage.

title: Implement session persistence and resume ledger
description: Integrate the Roger-owned session persistence and resume ledger across canonical storage rows, continuity classification, and cross-surface launch bindings so reopen and fail-closed resume share one durable state model.
acceptance_criteria:
Roger persists canonical session, run, target, SessionLocator, ResumeBundle, continuity state, and launch-binding metadata needed for resume across restarts
Resume attempts SessionLocator reopen first and falls back to ResumeBundle reseed or explicit fail-closed guidance when direct reopen is unavailable, stale, or provider-limited
CLI, TUI, and bridge launches read the same durable session-binding state and never infer control ownership from ambient process memory
Smoke coverage proves restart, stale-locator, and missing-harness-state cases either resume correctly or fail closed without losing review target identity
notes: This bead now acts as the integration/closure point for rr-003.6, rr-003.7, and rr-003.8. Keep the leaf tasks independently completable so agents can work the ledger, continuity decision logic, and launch-binding state in parallel.
close_reason: Integrated durable resume-ledger resolution across session rows, continuity decisioning, and cross-surface binding state with restart/stale/missing-state smoke coverage

title: Implement plain OpenCode dropout and rr return
description: Implement the intentional dropout to plain OpenCode and the explicit return path back into Roger. This is an ergonomic layer that builds on the core session linkage.
acceptance_criteria: Intentional dropout preserves the Roger control bundle needed to continue safely in plain OpenCode; rr return or equivalent helper rebinds the bare harness session to the correct Roger session; return is validated across session restarts
notes: Dependency corrected from depends_on to blocks (rr-003.1 must be done before this can proceed). rr-003.5 was a malformed duplicate of this bead and has been closed. As of 2026-03-30, rr-003.4 is correctly blocked by rr-003.1 and should not appear in br ready until rr-003.1 is closed.
close_reason: Implemented dropout control-bundle transition and rr return rebind/reseed helper with restart smoke coverage

title: Implement plain OpenCode dropout and rr return
description:
Implement the intentional dropout to plain OpenCode and the explicit return path back into Roger. This is an ergonomic layer that builds on the core session linkage.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Intentional dropout preserves the Roger control bundle needed to continue safely in plain OpenCode
- rr return or equivalent helper rebinds the bare harness session to the correct Roger session
- return is validated across session restarts
close_reason: Malformed duplicate of rr-003.4: same title, description, and intent but acceptance criteria stored with literal \n sequences (corrupt import artifact). Dependency relationship was also reversed (blocks rr-003.1 instead of being blocked by it). rr-003.4 is the canonical version.

title: Persist canonical session, run, and continuity ledger rows
description: Persist the Roger-owned session, run, target, continuity, and provider-linkage rows that every resume path relies on, without hiding control state in process memory.
acceptance_criteria:
Canonical storage rows exist for session, run, target, provider session reference, continuity state, and audit-relevant timestamps
Resume-critical fields survive restart and reload without ambient process memory
Storage-facing coverage proves Roger can reload the ledger state needed for resume classification and target binding
close_reason: Canonical session/run/continuity ledger rows persist across restart with storage coverage

title: Persist launch bindings and cross-surface session ownership state
description: Persist the repo, target, launch-profile, UI-target, and instance-binding state Roger needs so CLI, TUI, and bridge surfaces all resolve the same session ownership truthfully.
acceptance_criteria:
Durable launch-binding records capture repo identity, review target, launch profile, UI target, and instance preference without ambient process memory
CLI, TUI, and bridge entry surfaces read the same stored ownership state when resolving an existing session
Ambiguous, stale, or mismatched binding state fails closed into explicit recovery or picker behavior instead of silent reuse
close_reason: Cross-surface launch bindings now persist ownership context and resolve stale/ambiguous state fail-closed

title: Implement reopen-versus-reseed continuity decision engine
description: Implement the pure Roger-owned decision layer that chooses locator reopen, ResumeBundle reseed, or explicit fail-closed guidance based on provider capability and current continuity state.
acceptance_criteria:
A pure Roger-owned decision layer chooses locator reopen, ResumeBundle reseed, or explicit fail-closed guidance from provider capability, continuity quality, and current session state
Stale locator, missing harness state, provider-limited reopen, and target-mismatch cases produce explicit classified outcomes rather than ad hoc fallthrough
Decision coverage proves reopen-versus-reseed behavior stays truthful before any provider-specific adapter UX is layered on top
close_reason: Implemented pure reopen-versus-reseed continuity decision engine with classified outcomes and green workspace verification

title: Implement optional harness-native Roger command routing and CLI fallback
description: The accepted harness-command ADR and canonical plan both describe an optional safe in-harness Roger command subset, but the live graph never captured the follow-up implementation work. This bead owns the thin command router and binding layer for the safe subset roger-help, roger-status, roger-findings, and roger-return, plus truthful fallback to canonical rr flows when a provider lacks support.
acceptance_criteria:
A Roger-owned command router exists for the safe in-harness command subset and maps onto the same core operations as the canonical CLI
Provider bindings remain optional and must fail truthfully to the equivalent rr command or session-finder guidance when command support is absent
No approval, posting, or hidden GitHub mutation capability is introduced through harness-native commands
close_reason: Implemented Roger harness-command router + optional provider bindings for roger-help/status/findings/return with truthful CLI fallback for unsupported providers; validated with rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core (pass, includes new router tests) and rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke (pass, includes new harness routing/fallback tests).

title: Prompt pipeline and review engine
description: Build the staged review workflow that explores first, goes deep second, and persists validated structured findings rather than terminal-only output. This epic must encode prompt invocation snapshots, findings-pack validation, repair boundaries, and refresh-safe lineage so downstream TUI and approval flows never act on ad hoc parser output or mutation-capable shortcuts.
acceptance_criteria:
Prompt stages, invocation snapshots, and structured findings boundaries are explicit and durable
Findings normalization and repair behavior are validated so malformed provider output degrades honestly instead of fabricating state
Refresh flows can carry forward, repair, or retire findings coherently without bypassing validation or mutating outbound state implicitly
close_reason: Parent closure after rr-004.1, rr-004.2, rr-016, and rr-017 completion: staged prompt execution, validated findings boundary, durable invocation snapshots, and refresh lifecycle transitions are now implemented.

title: Implement structured findings pack validator and repair boundary
description: Define and implement the StructuredFindingsPack v1 boundary Roger relies on for durable findings, refresh identity, and safe outbound drafting. This bead exists because prompt stages are not enough; Roger needs a validator, salvage rules, and bounded repair behavior so malformed provider output cannot silently manufacture findings or drafts.
acceptance_criteria:
A stable StructuredFindingsPack v1 schema, validator, and normalization boundary into Roger-owned finding rows are explicit
Malformed or partial findings packs follow a bounded repair taxonomy with salvage rules and retry budget rather than ad hoc parser recovery
Raw-only degraded results are preserved and surfaced locally without synthesizing fake findings, fake evidence, or outbound drafts
Integration coverage proves refresh and draft-generation paths consume only validated findings state
close_reason: Implemented StructuredFindingsPack v1 validator boundary in app-core with bounded repair states, raw-only preservation, and validated-only refresh/draft consumption tests

title: Define prompt preset, invocation snapshot, and outcome-event model
description: Specify the 0.1.0 prompt preset registry, execution snapshot model, and typed outcome events Roger needs for prompt reuse, audit, and later usefulness analysis. The canonical plan names these contracts, but the live graph currently relies on broader prompt-engine tasks without a bead that freezes the model.
acceptance_criteria:
PromptPreset, PromptInvocation, and outcome-event objects are explicit enough that runs can snapshot exact resolved prompt text without a heavyweight versioning system
Recent, frequent, last-used, and optional favorite prompt reuse rules are explicit, including scope and persistence expectations
Typed outcome events cover finding, draft, approval, posting, and usefulness signals needed for later analytics without making analytics a first-class product surface
close_reason: ADR-009 created, PromptPreset/PromptPresetUsage/PromptInvocation/OutcomeEvent fields added to DATA_MODEL_AND_STORAGE_CONTRACT.md, ADR README updated

title: Session-aware CLI
description: Provide the shell entrypoint for starting, resuming, inspecting, refreshing, and re-entering Roger review sessions using shared launch-routing and session-resolution rules.
acceptance_criteria: CLI can drive launch, resume, findings, status, and refresh without the extension; launch profiles and session re-entry rules are shared with the browser bridge; ambiguity fails closed rather than guessing between sessions or targets
close_reason: Closure cleanup 2026-04-07: parent CLI acceptance is satisfied by the landed session-aware command surfaces and child slices, including launch-routing, session resolution/global finder, robot-docs discovery, search, and the rr-018 implementation lane. Current repo evidence in packages/cli/src/lib.rs and packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs supports launch, resume, findings, status, refresh, re-entry, and robot-facing discovery without relying on the extension.

title: Define local launch profiles and daemonless launch-routing rules
description: Define named local launch profiles, terminal or muxer selection, and shared daemonless routing rules so CLI and extension launches resolve the same way without hidden ambient state.
acceptance_criteria: Named launch-profile fields and supported local environments are explicit; CLI and browser-initiated launches share the same routing contract; fallback behavior is truthful when the requested launch environment is unavailable
close_reason: Defined explicit launch-profile routing fields, added daemonless shared CLI/extension routing resolver with truthful degraded fallback, and landed storage/app-core smoke coverage

title: Add session resolution, global session finder, and re-entry routing
description: Implement the rules for resolving the right Roger session from the current repo, handling ambiguity safely, and exposing a global session-finder path for cross-repo re-entry.
acceptance_criteria: A single strong repo-local match can be resumed safely; ambiguous or missing matches fail closed into a session picker instead of guessing; global session finding and re-entry routing work across repos and attention states
close_reason: Closure cleanup 2026-04-07: acceptance is satisfied by the real session-resolution and global finder implementation. User-facing rr sessions surface landed in rr-005.2.1; CLI/session-resolution behavior is exercised in packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs and packages/storage/tests/session_resolution_smoke.rs; cross-repo re-entry and fail-closed ambiguity are further defended by rr-011.6 validation.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Reopened after implementation audit. The storage and session-resolution primitives are real, but the acceptance text requires an exposed global session-finder path for cross-repo re-entry. The current CLI still lacks a user-facing rr sessions command, so this bead closed early relative to the frozen CLI and robot contracts.

title: Implement rr sessions global session-finder CLI surface
description: Add the missing user-facing global session-finder path over the existing session-resolution primitives. This bead owns the CLI command surface, robot envelopes, and fail-closed selection output for cross-repo session re-entry.
acceptance_criteria:
rr sessions exists as a human-usable and robot-usable command in the Roger CLI
The command returns recent and active sessions across repos with attention-state filtering and enough structured identity to drive safe re-entry
Ambiguous or stale follow-on resume choices remain fail-closed rather than silently selecting a target, and the output matches the frozen robot CLI contract fields for rr sessions
close_reason: Implemented rr sessions global session-finder CLI surface: added command parsing/dispatch, robot schema rr.robot.sessions.v1, attention-state and limit filters, human-readable output, compact robot format support, and explicit fail-closed follow-on resume/refresh hints requiring --session. Added CLI smoke coverage and suite metadata tests/suites/int_cli_sessions_global_finder.toml. Validation commands run: rch exec -- cargo fmt; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr <temp-metadata-dir>

title: Define structured review-intake contract and bounded prompt ingress
description: Define the canonical review-intake payload that every launch surface must normalize into, including source surface, target identity, requested action, prompt preset/objective inputs, UI target, launch-profile preference, and instance/worktree preference. This closes the current gap where launch routing is named in the plan but the shared intake contract itself is still floating prose.
acceptance_criteria:
A shared intake shape is explicit for CLI, TUI, extension, and external-link launches, with stable fields for target identity, action, prompt ingress, UI target, and launch preferences
Web-path prompt ingress is bounded to preset selection plus a short explicit objective, and per-review overrides cannot silently relax approval or mutation policy
The contract states what is persisted for audit and resume, and how ambiguous or missing fields fail closed rather than inventing launch state
close_reason: Defined ReviewIntake v1 contract, bounded web prompt ingress, persistence/audit capture, and fail-closed intake rules in canonical plan

title: Define robot-facing CLI surface and stable output schemas
description: Specify which rr commands must support stable machine-facing behavior in 0.1.0, what --robot means operationally, and which structured output shapes Roger commits to keep stable. The plan gives detailed conventions here, but the bead graph currently has no task that freezes them before CLI implementation begins.
acceptance_criteria:
The 0.1.0 robot-command set is explicit, including which commands support --robot and which output formats are guaranteed versus optional
Robot-mode behavior defines stdout or stderr split, deterministic exit semantics, and explicit handling for degraded, partial, or repair-needed states
Schemas or examples exist for the committed machine-readable outputs so automation does not depend on scraping human-oriented prose
close_reason: Completed

title: Define early --robot CLI contract and stable output surface
description: Turn the plan's machine-facing CLI section into an owned contract. This bead should choose the initial rr commands that must support --robot in 0.1.0, define stable output schemas and exit-code rules, and bound when optional TOON output is allowed versus plain JSON or compact JSON.
close_reason: Superseded by rr-005.4, which already captures the same robot-facing CLI contract and stable output-schema work.

title: Implement rr robot-docs discovery commands from the frozen contract
description: Materialize the machine-readable discovery and help surface promised by the robot CLI contract. This bead owns rr robot-docs guide, commands, schemas, and workflows, and keeps the emitted command inventory aligned with the actually supported 0.1.0 CLI surface.
acceptance_criteria:
rr robot-docs guide, commands, schemas, and workflows exist in the CLI and support the guaranteed robot formats
The payloads are versioned, deterministic, and derived from Roger-owned command and schema truth rather than hand-maintained prose that can silently drift
Unsupported or not-yet-implemented commands are labeled truthfully so the discovery surface does not claim capabilities the CLI does not actually have
close_reason: Closure cleanup 2026-04-07: acceptance is satisfied by the real rr robot-docs implementation in packages/cli/src/lib.rs plus robot-docs coverage in packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs. The command now serves guide, commands, schemas, and workflows with truthful blocked handling for unknown topics, and rr-011.9 validates the broader robot shortlist against the frozen contract.

title: Implement rr search CLI surface over prior-review lookup
description: Wire the existing hybrid prior-review lookup into a real CLI command so the 0.1.0 shell and robot contract can actually use Roger search. This bead owns command parsing, result shaping, degraded lexical-only reporting, and scope-safe query options.
acceptance_criteria:
rr search exists as a human-usable and robot-usable command in the Roger CLI
The command routes through the existing repo-first prior-review lookup and surfaces truthful lexical-only degraded mode when semantic assets or sidecars are unavailable
Robot output matches the frozen robot CLI contract commitments for rr search and does not fabricate semantic parity when only lexical retrieval is operational
close_reason: Closure cleanup 2026-04-07: acceptance is satisfied by the real rr search implementation in packages/cli/src/lib.rs and robot smoke coverage in packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs. The command is human-usable and robot-usable, reports truthful lexical_only degraded mode when semantic assets are unavailable, and is reinforced by the broader robot-shortlist validation in rr-011.9.

title: TUI workflow
description: Implement the main Roger TUI for session navigation, findings triage, linked evidence review, clarification, and local approval of outbound GitHub drafts.
acceptance_criteria: Active review sessions are navigable in the TUI; findings can be triaged and clarified locally without implicit mutation; outbound drafts are reviewed in a distinct local approval queue before posting
close_reason: Parent TUI workflow acceptance satisfied via closed child beads rr-006.1/.2/.3/.4 and rr-019 findings workflow slice: active session navigation, local non-mutating clarification, and distinct local draft approval queue are implemented and validated. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test tui_shell_smoke.

title: Add non-mutating clarification in finding detail
description: Add bounded clarification from the finding inspector so users can interrogate a finding locally without implicitly triaging it, drafting it, or mutating GitHub-facing state.
acceptance_criteria: Clarification requests are linked to the finding and session locally; clarification does not mutate triage, outbound, or draft state implicitly; the TUI makes clarification visually distinct from approval or posting actions
close_reason: Validated non-mutating clarification in finding detail: clarification intents remain local to finding/session, do not mutate triage/outbound/draft posting state, and finding-detail panel keeps clarification markers visually distinct from posting/approval indicators. Added explicit assertion in tui_shell_smoke for no pending_post/post_failed markers in clarification detail view. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo fmt -p roger-app-core; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test tui_shell_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core.

title: Define TUI/app-core supervisor policy for queues, cancellation, and refresh cadence
description: Set the accepted in-process runtime policy for how Roger schedules harness work, indexing, bridge traffic, and cross-process refresh inside the Rust TUI/app-core boundary. This is still an explicit pre-implementation open question in the canonical plan and AGENTS.md, but it is not yet represented as a bead.
acceptance_criteria:
Default queue classes, queue limits, cancellation rules, and bounded refresh cadence are explicit for harness work, indexing, and bridge-triggered updates
The contract distinguishes same-process wake behavior from cross-process refresh and states what may run concurrently versus what must serialize for session, approval, and posting safety
Background work stays behind an in-process supervisor or short-lived helper processes and never requires a resident broker or daemon for normal Roger operation
The policy is concrete enough that rr-019 and bridge-facing work cannot invent ad hoc runtime behavior during implementation
close_reason: TUI_RUNTIME_SUPERVISOR_POLICY.md written: three queue classes (harness/bridge/indexing) with explicit queue depths, timeouts, and cancellation rules; same-process wake vs cross-process polling cadence (500ms/2s/10s); serialization rules for approval/posting; daemonless requirement enforced; routing policy for TUI vs robot-facing outputs

title: Define Roger attention-event and notification contract
description: Freeze the small Roger-owned attention-event model and how it is exposed across TUI, CLI, and extension surfaces. The canonical plan treats attention states as shared product infrastructure, but the graph does not yet name the contract directly.
acceptance_criteria:
The canonical event set and state meanings are explicit, including review_started, awaiting_user_input, awaiting_outbound_approval, findings_ready, refresh_recommended, and review_failed or equivalent settled names
The contract states which surfaces must expose or mirror each event and what degraded behavior is allowed when a bridge cannot read back status
Attention events remain Roger-owned normalized state rather than raw harness or browser-specific signals
close_reason: Completed

title: Stand up minimal TUI shell and session chrome before full findings workflow
description: De-risk FrankenTUI integration before the full review loop is complete by standing up a minimal Roger TUI shell with session chrome, bounded read-only navigation, and wake plumbing. This bead exists because waiting for rr-018 to finish entirely before any real TUI wiring begins hides runtime and event-loop risk until too late in the phase plan.
acceptance_criteria:
A minimal FrankenTUI shell can open, render session-level chrome, and navigate bounded read-only Roger data without requiring the full findings or approval workflow
Wake plumbing, background-job surfacing, and supervisor hooks are exercised enough to de-risk the in-process runtime boundary before rr-019 takes on full findings and draft state
This bead explicitly excludes GitHub posting, approval mutation, and full findings triage; it exists to surface TUI/runtime risk earlier rather than to bypass the later safety gates
close_reason: Implemented minimal read-only TUI shell model with session chrome, bounded panel navigation, and wake/supervisor smoke coverage.

title: GitHub integration and extension
description: Add GitHub-targeted adapters and the browser entry surface without letting the extension become the system control plane, state owner, or a backdoor mutation path.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub read and target-resolution behavior is explicit; daemonless launch bridge and install prerequisites are validated; extension UI stays thin, local-first, and non-authoritative
close_reason: Parent acceptance satisfied by closed children rr-007.1, rr-021, rr-022 plus revalidation: GitHub read/target-resolution remains explicit, bridge launch path remains daemonless with fail-closed prerequisites, and extension surface remains thin launch-only/non-authoritative. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-github-adapter; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-bridge; bash -n scripts/extension/validate_manifest.sh; scripts/extension/validate_manifest.sh; node --check apps/extension/src/background/main.js && node --check apps/extension/src/content/main.js; rg -n "approve|approval|post|posted|submit review|create review|draft|mutation|write" apps/extension/src -S || true; rg -n "native messaging|native_messaging|roger://|launch|resume|refresh|findings|no-status|no status" apps/extension/src -S

title: Freeze extension packaging, contract export, and release ownership
description: Define the minimal build, contract-export, install, and release shape the extension and Native Messaging bridge actually require. This bead exists because rr-021 is still hiding packaging and installation complexity that can block truthful daemonless bridge claims even when the bridge protocol itself is already decided.
acceptance_criteria:
Minimal TS build tooling is selected with near-zero browser-runtime dependencies and bundler use only if explicitly justified
Rust-owned bridge contract export into the extension tree is defined, including drift detection in local hooks or CI
Roger-owned pack/install and release ownership for companion binaries, host manifests, and browser artifacts is explicit for supported 0.1.0 targets
close_reason: Completed

title: Approval and posting flow
description: Model and implement the local draft, approval, invalidation, and posting path for comments, questions, and suggestions before any GitHub mutation occurs.
acceptance_criteria: Drafts and batches are explicit local objects with approval invalidation rules; posting is an explicit auditable step bound to exact payload and target; no GitHub mutation happens outside Roger approval flow
close_reason: Parent acceptance satisfied by closed child beads rr-020 and rr-008.1: local draft/batch objects with approval invalidation and post-time gate checks are implemented, posting remains explicit and auditable with posted-action outcome snapshots, and browser/TUI surfaces do not bypass local approval flow. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test outbound_draft_audit_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test tui_shell_smoke; rg -n "approve|approval|post|posted|submit review|create review" apps/extension/src -S || true.

title: Implement explicit posting flow
description: Implement the only Roger-approved GitHub mutation path: post stored local drafts or batches after explicit approval, target revalidation, and audit capture.
acceptance_criteria:
Posting executes only from stored approved draft or batch records, never from ambient current-PR context, browser DOM state, or unapproved findings
Approval binds to exact payload and target identity and is revalidated at post time before any GitHub mutation occurs
Partial failure, stale-target, or missing-local-state conditions fail closed with durable retry and audit state rather than opportunistic reposting or fallback mutation
Posting remains a visibly elevated action across CLI, TUI, and extension surfaces; shortcuts may open Roger, but they may not bypass stored-draft selection and approval checks
close_reason: Implemented explicit posting execution flow in app-core: post-time gate revalidation, fail-closed adapter result validation, posted-action outcome snapshots (succeeded/partial/failed), and retry candidate tracking for partial/failure paths. Expanded outbound audit smoke coverage for gate-blocked, adapter-error, partial success, and malformed adapter outcomes. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo fmt -p roger-app-core; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test outbound_draft_audit_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr <temp_suite_dir>

title: Worktrees and named instances
description: Support isolated review work via worktrees and conservative named-instance sharing without overengineering the first version or guessing between multiple same-PR targets.
acceptance_criteria: Review environments can be isolated safely when needed; multiple named instances can coexist with explicit routing rules; same-PR selection is explicit rather than ambient or guessed
close_reason: Epic closeout after verifying rr-009.2, rr-009.1, and rr-023 are closed with accepted implementation/validation evidence. Acceptance met: conservative same-PR routing and named-instance/worktree isolation path implemented through child beads.

title: Handle same-PR instance selection and launch routing
description: Handle multiple Roger instances or worktrees for the same PR as an explicit routing problem rather than hidden auto-selection.
acceptance_criteria: CLI and extension launches can disambiguate multiple same-PR targets safely; launch-profile compatibility and stale-target recovery are explicit; Roger avoids ambient guessing when more than one plausible local instance exists
close_reason: Implemented same-PR launch routing in worktree-manager: resolve_launch_routing with explicit-instance override, single-match resolution, ambiguity fail-closed with --instance guidance, and idle-instance stale recovery. Torn-down explicit targets fail closed. Validation: cargo test -p roger-worktree-manager — 32/32 tests pass (20 unit, 12 integration smoke).

title: Freeze 0.1.0 multi-instance and worktree defaults before implementation
description: Resolve the remaining planning-stage defaults for current-checkout mode, named-instance mode, and worktree mode before implementation starts. This bead should decide selection rules, copied-file defaults, shared versus isolated resource classes, separate-profile recommendation rules, and the minimum inspectable config surface needed to keep same-PR routing and approval invalidation coherent.
acceptance_criteria:
Current_checkout, named_instance, and worktree selection rules are explicit, including when Roger must not silently escalate modes and when a separate Roger profile is actually required
First-party resource classes and 0.1.0 defaults are fixed for env/config files, ports, repo-local DBs, container naming, caches, artifact directories, and log directories, including no implicit secret-bearing .env or .env.local copy
Preflight classifications and default operator guidance are explicit for ready, ready_with_actions, profile_required, unsafe_default_blocked, and verification_failed
Project-defined hook phases and config layering are explicit for preflight, worktree_create, materialize_resources, session_env, verify, and cleanup, with inspectable resolved config and local override rules
close_reason: Completed: froze 0.1.0 multi-instance/worktree defaults in canonical plan

title: Hybrid search, memory, and skills
description: Add the repo-first retrieval layer for prior findings, searchable artifacts, and semantic review memory using hybrid lexical plus semantic search from day one, while keeping scope and promotion rules explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Hybrid lexical plus semantic retrieval exists for 0.1.0 with truthful lexical-only degraded mode; Prior review lookup helps refresh flows and memory stays scope-safe; Later memory and promotion workflows remain isolated behind stable interfaces
close_reason: Child beads rr-010.1 (policy contract) and rr-024 (hybrid lookup implementation) are complete and validated; hybrid retrieval now has explicit lexical-only degraded mode and scope-safe memory lanes

title: Define search-memory lifecycle, promotion, and semantic asset policy
description: Freeze the missing search and memory rules that still live as prose in the canonical plan: scope partitioning, candidate versus promoted lanes, promotion or decay rules, duplicate or conflict handling, rebuild semantics, and how the first semantic model assets are installed and verified locally. rr-024 covers lookup implementation, but not this policy layer.
acceptance_criteria:
Scope partitioning, searchable-versus-promotable lanes, promotion or demotion states, duplicate handling, and index rebuild semantics are explicit enough to constrain later search implementation
The first semantic asset strategy is explicit, including install, verification, degraded-mode fallback, and when Roger may treat semantic search as unavailable rather than silently weak
Usage or outcome vocabulary needed for later memory usefulness and contradiction handling is defined without forcing a full analytics product into 0.1.0
close_reason: Completed

title: Safety and validation
description: Prove the product stays review-safe, approval-gated, daemonless, and resumable through explicit suites rather than assumptions, with a small but real support matrix across providers, browsers, and platforms.
acceptance_criteria: Validation work is split into traceable suites rather than one catch-all test bead; OpenCode fallback or resume, degraded findings, draft invalidation, and same-PR routing are covered explicitly; supported provider and browser claims stay tied to named suites and fixtures
close_reason: Parent safety/validation acceptance satisfied: rr-011.x validation slices are closed with explicit coverage for OpenCode fallback/resume, degraded findings, draft invalidation + partial post recovery, same-PR routing/re-entry, and robot CLI fallback semantics. Representative suite metadata plan check run across core rr-011 surfaces to confirm traceable suite registration and tier compatibility: rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr <temp_suite_dir_with_rr-011_suites>.

title: Implement provider acceptance suites for OpenCode and Gemini
description: Turn the claimed OpenCode and Gemini support levels into repeatable acceptance suites so provider support is proven rather than asserted.
acceptance_criteria: OpenCode acceptance covers locator reopen, ResumeBundle fallback, and dropout or return behavior; Gemini acceptance covers Roger-owned continuity, raw plus structured capture, and truthful reseed; unsupported deeper capabilities fail clearly rather than pretending provider parity
close_reason: Implemented OpenCode and Gemini provider acceptance suites with explicit metadata, truthful bounded/degraded behavior assertions, and raw+structured continuity capture coverage.

title: Validate optional harness-native Roger commands and CLI fallback
description: The seed and accepted ADR both call for truthful validation if Roger later exposes in-harness commands. This bead keeps that optional path explicit by covering the safe subset command IDs plus their required fallback to canonical rr flows when provider support is absent or partial.
acceptance_criteria:
Validation covers the safe optional harness command subset for status, findings, and return plus truthful fallback to canonical rr guidance
Provider-unsupported, partially-supported, and stale-session cases fail closed without diverging from the canonical CLI semantics
The suite is marked optional or bounded in the shared validation metadata so Roger does not overclaim provider parity
close_reason: Added harness-command validation coverage for unsupported provider fallback, partial binding fallback, and stale-locator return parity with canonical rr semantics in packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs; added bounded/optional suite metadata at tests/suites/int_harness_harness_command_fallback.toml. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke (10 passed); rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr tests/suites target/test-artifacts (pass, includes suite int_harness_harness_command_fallback).

title: Validate refresh identity behavior
description: Validate that reruns classify old and new findings correctly instead of duplicating, orphaning, or silently mutating them.
acceptance_criteria: Test scenarios cover carried-forward, resolved, superseded, and stale findings; refresh preserves approval or invalidation lineage correctly; refresh validation uses repeatable fixture-backed scenarios instead of ad hoc manual checks
close_reason: Added fixture-backed refresh identity validation with suite metadata and smoke assertions for carried-forward/resolved/superseded/stale transitions plus approval invalidation/reconfirmation lineage signaling.

title: Validate structured findings degraded modes
description: Validate malformed, partial, raw-only, and repair-needed findings outcomes so Roger stays truthful across CLI, TUI, and extension surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Test scenarios cover valid, partial, raw-only, repair-needed, and retry-exhausted findings outcomes; valid data is salvaged without losing the raw artifact when normalization fails; TUI and extension state stays consistent with degraded findings outcomes
close_reason: Validated structured findings degraded modes with explicit assertions for valid/partial/raw-only/repair-needed/retry-exhausted behavior, including raw artifact preservation in degraded paths, and confirmed TUI + launch-only bridge surfaces stay consistent with degraded outcomes. Added suite metadata tests/suites/int_findings_degraded_modes.toml (id int_tui_findings_degraded_modes). Validation run: rch exec -- cargo fmt -p roger-app-core; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test structured_findings_boundary; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test tui_shell_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-bridge --test bridge_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr <temp_suite_dir>

title: Validate draft invalidation, no-status bridge mode, and partial post recovery
description: Validate the safety cases where refresh invalidates drafts, the extension can launch but not read back status, or posting only partially succeeds.
acceptance_criteria:
Draft invalidation after refresh, rebase, target drift, or missing-local-state is exercised explicitly and always fails closed before posting
Launch-only or no-status bridge mode remains honest, does not imply hidden background coordination, and does not allow the browser path to infer approval or posting readiness from stale local signals
Partial post success preserves durable retry, audit, and user-attention state without accidental reposting, duplicate mutation, or loss of the exact posted-versus-unposted split
close_reason: Added validation coverage for draft invalidation/no-local-state fail-closed posting gate behavior, launch-only bridge honesty in no-status mode, and partial-post retry/audit preservation. Delivered test expansions in outbound_draft_audit_smoke and bridge_smoke plus suite metadata int_github_posting_safety_recovery. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo fmt -p roger-app-core -p roger-bridge; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test outbound_draft_audit_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-bridge --test bridge_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-bridge; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr <temp_suite_dir>

title: Validate clarification-in-place and OpenCode dropout or return
description: Validate that users can question a finding locally, drop out to plain OpenCode intentionally, and get back to the same Roger session without losing control context.
acceptance_criteria:
Clarification from finding detail is non-mutating, locally persisted, and clearly separated from triage, approval, and posting actions in the TUI
OpenCode dropout loads the defined Roger control bundle and preserves review-target identity plus approval safety rather than silently shedding Roger context
rr return or equivalent re-entry reaches the same Roger session when possible and otherwise fails closed into explicit recovery or picker guidance instead of guessing across stale or ambiguous state
close_reason: Validated clarification-in-place and OpenCode dropout/return continuity across TUI, storage, and CLI: clarification intent remains local and non-mutating; dropout control bundle preserves review-target identity and approval safety context; rr return rebinds to same session when possible and degrades/fails closed into explicit recovery semantics when locator continuity is unavailable. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test tui_shell_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test opencode_dropout_return_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr <temp_suite_dir>

title: Validate session re-entry and same-PR launch routing
description: Validate repo-local re-entry, global session finding, and same-PR launch routing so Roger does not guess between multiple plausible local targets.
acceptance_criteria:
Repo-local strong-match resume and ambiguous-match fallback are both exercised, and ambiguous or missing local state never causes Roger to guess across sessions or PR targets
Global session finding can route back into the correct session across repos and attention states while preserving the same target identity and fail-closed behavior when confidence is insufficient
Same-PR multi-instance launches are disambiguated explicitly across CLI and extension surfaces, including no-status bridge and stale-target scenarios, and the two surfaces follow the same routing rules
close_reason: Validated re-entry routing fail-closed behavior (repo-local strong match, ambiguous/missing picker fallback, global picker identity preservation, same-PR multi-instance disambiguation, and stale-target handling across CLI/extension/no-status bridge). Added suite metadata at tests/suites/int_session_reentry_same_pr_routing.toml. Validation commands run: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test session_resolution_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-bridge; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr <temp-metadata-dir>

title: Define test execution tiers and the automated E2E budget guard
description: Make the testing posture in the canonical plan executable by defining the speed tiers, blessed automated E2E budget, and the feedback or policy Roger should enforce when new heavyweight E2Es are added. This requirement is explicit in the plan, but the graph currently has validation beads without a bead for the budget rule itself.
acceptance_criteria:
`docs/TEST_HARNESS_GUIDELINES.md` defines fast local, PR, gated or nightly, and release validation tiers clearly enough to map suites and smoke obligations without ad hoc drift
`docs/AUTOMATED_E2E_BUDGET.json` tracks the blessed automated E2E count in machine-readable form and defines warning versus future CI-failure behavior for unjustified growth
The harness contract explains the justification required for new heavyweight E2Es and makes lower-cost unit, parameterized, provider-acceptance, or narrow integration tests the default alternative
close_reason: Defined four-tier validation contract in docs/TEST_HARNESS_GUIDELINES.md and machine-readable E2E budget in docs/AUTOMATED_E2E_BUDGET.json.

title: Implement OpenCode continuity smoke suite
description: Create a dedicated automated smoke suite for OpenCode continuity cases: direct locator resume, stale-locator reseed, and ResumeBundle integrity across restarts.
acceptance_criteria:
Fixture-backed smoke scenarios cover successful locator reopen, stale-locator reseed after compaction, and ResumeBundle recovery after process restart
Failures point to the continuity layer clearly enough to distinguish resume breakage from general harness or fixture problems
Suite metadata, fixture usage, and failure-artifact preservation follow the shared validation harness contract from rr-025.3
close_reason: Added dedicated OpenCode continuity smoke suite metadata and restart+compaction resume bundle recovery coverage

title: Validate the robot CLI shortlist beyond the session-aware shell core
description: Add explicit validation for the robot-facing CLI shortlist that the contracts already freeze but the current validation graph does not name directly. This bead covers rr sessions, rr search, and rr robot-docs once their implementation beads land, with emphasis on truthful degraded and blocked states plus schema stability.
acceptance_criteria:
Validation covers the required robot shortlist surfaces added after the initial CLI core: rr sessions, rr search, and rr robot-docs
Suite coverage proves deterministic envelopes, truthful degraded or blocked reporting, and schema-field stability across JSON outputs
The validation metadata is wired into the shared harness and CI tiers rather than existing only as ad hoc command checks
close_reason: Validated robot CLI shortlist beyond core by covering rr sessions, rr search, and rr robot-docs with deterministic envelope assertions, schema-field stability checks, and truthful degraded/blocked behavior checks. Added suite metadata wiring in tests/suites/int_cli_robot_shortlist_validation.toml. Validation commands run: rch exec -- cargo fmt; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr <temp-metadata-dir>

title: Run architecture risk spikes and capture ADRs
description: Reduce the highest-risk unknowns before implementation spreads: the supported-harness contract, browser bridge, local runtime/process boundary, and artifact/storage split. Record any package-shaping outcomes in ADRs.
acceptance_criteria: Spike outcomes are documented; Any package-shaping decisions are captured in ADRs; The harness capability tiers, browser bridge, and artifact/storage assumptions are no longer implicit
close_reason: Closed by Round 04 reconciliation outcome: architecture spikes were run, accepted ADRs exist, and the formerly implicit assumptions are now explicit in canonical docs.

title: Define core domain schema and finding fingerprint model
description: Specify the first-class entities for sessions, runs, findings, finding fingerprints, code-evidence locations, artifacts, outbound drafts and batches, prompt invocations, SessionLocator and ResumeBundle records, posted actions, scope or memory records, and config layers. Include refresh identity, write-ownership, event-history, approval-invalidation inputs, and scope-boundary rules needed to avoid duplicate explosions, silent bleed, or unsafe draft reuse.
acceptance_criteria:
Core entities and invariants are defined, including prompt invocations, code-evidence locations, draft batches, SessionLocator, and ResumeBundle records
Triage state, outbound state, anchor-validity state, and append-only event history are explicit
Finding fingerprint, refresh classification, approval-invalidation inputs, and scope-boundary rules are explicit
close_reason: Defined core domain schema, fingerprint, refresh, and invalidation contract in dedicated support doc

title: Implement local storage, migrations, and artifact budget classes
description: Create the canonical SQLite-family persistence layer, migration flow, row-version or conflict scheme, and artifact split Roger needs for local-first review state. This task must preserve hot-path responsiveness for CLI, TUI, and bridge flows while keeping large artifacts and rebuildable search assets out of mutable core review tables, and it must prove restart or cross-surface access does not quietly reintroduce daemon-style coordination assumptions.
acceptance_criteria:
Migration flow exists for the canonical Roger aggregates and includes explicit row-version or conflict behavior for same-session writes
Storage schema supports sessions, runs, findings, drafts, approvals, posted actions, launch profiles, and index bookkeeping without hiding mutable state in sidecars
Artifact budget classes and cold-storage rules prevent large payloads from bloating primary tables while preserving provenance and rebuild semantics for search sidecars
Storage smoke covers restart, CLI or TUI or bridge re-entry, and recovery of resume plus approval-sensitive state from disk without a resident broker
close_reason: Implemented canonical storage, artifact budget routing, and restart smoke coverage

I left rr-014 in progress rather than closing because the shared crate still deserves one explicit review of search-sidecar/rebuild semantics before calling the acceptance criteria fully complete.

title: Define the Roger-to-harness session linkage
description: Define the exact Roger session contract for supported harnesses, with OpenCode primary and Gemini bounded: capability tiers, continuity-quality rules, SessionLocator versus ResumeBundle behavior, dropout semantics, and safe optional harness-native commands. This task fixes the contract that downstream adapters and UI surfaces must implement, so Roger can preserve real fallback and truthful provider claims without depending on fragile provider internals.
acceptance_criteria:
Session linkage, capability tiers, continuity-quality reporting, SessionLocator semantics, and ResumeBundle rules are documented and stable enough for adapter implementation
OpenCode Tier B and Gemini Tier A expectations are explicit, including what Roger may not claim for each provider and when direct resume must fall back to reseed
Dropout, return, and safe harness-native command rules are fixed without allowing approval or GitHub-posting behavior to hide inside in-harness commands
The contract names the mandatory smoke scenarios for downstream implementation: locator reopen, stale-locator reseed, plain OpenCode dropout, rr return, and bounded Gemini reseed
close_reason: HARNESS_SESSION_LINKAGE_CONTRACT.md written: SessionLocator/ResumeBundle fields, capability tiers (Tier A/B/C), OpenCode Tier B + Gemini Tier A provider targets with explicit cannot-claim rules, resume algorithm, dropout rules, continuity quality, and 5 mandatory smoke scenarios (locator reopen, stale-locator reseed, dropout, rr return, Gemini reseed)

title: Encode staged review prompts and structured findings
description: Implement the staged review engine and persist raw outputs plus validated structured findings with evidence, lineage, stage provenance, and honest degraded states.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt stages run independently and persist raw outputs; structured findings are normalized only through the validated StructuredFindingsPack boundary; evidence links, stage provenance, and degraded states are retained honestly
close_reason: Parent closure after rr-016.1/.2/.3 completion: staged exploration/deep/follow-up execution persists raw outputs and invocation metadata, and validated findings materialize with provenance/evidence while degraded raw-only paths avoid fabrication.

title: Persist prompt invocation snapshots and stage outcome records
description: Persist resolved prompt preset snapshots, exact prompt text, raw provider outputs, and typed outcome events for each stage invocation so refresh and audit flows do not depend on transient harness text.
acceptance_criteria:
Resolved preset snapshots, exact prompt text, and stage invocation metadata are stored immutably enough for audit and replay
Raw provider outputs and typed outcome events are persisted with stage, run, and session linkage
Invocation storage survives restart and compaction without losing the exact prompt text Roger executed
close_reason: Implemented prompt invocation snapshot and typed outcome-event persistence with migration + restart durability coverage.

title: Implement staged review pass execution and raw-output capture
description: Run exploration, deep-review, and follow-up passes against the active harness and capture raw output bundles with honest degraded or partial states.
acceptance_criteria:
Exploration, deep-review, and follow-up passes can be invoked independently against the active harness session
Raw outputs are captured even when no structured findings are produced or when a stage ends in degraded state
Stage execution preserves enough metadata for later repair, refresh, and audit flows to reason about what actually ran
close_reason: Implemented staged execution + raw capture + outcome metadata (rr-016.2)

title: Materialize validated findings with provenance and degraded-state preservation
description: Transform validated StructuredFindingsPack results into durable finding rows with stage provenance, evidence links, and explicit degraded-state preservation.
acceptance_criteria:
Validated StructuredFindingsPack results materialize durable finding rows with evidence links, stage provenance, and lineage pointers
Raw-only or degraded outcomes remain visible locally without fabricating findings, evidence, or outbound drafts
Materialized findings are queryable by downstream refresh, CLI, TUI, and draft-generation flows
close_reason: Verified materialized-finding persistence path and added dedicated stage materialization smoke coverage for provenance/evidence queryability plus degraded raw-only no-fabrication behavior.

title: Implement refresh behavior and finding lifecycle transitions
description: Support reruns after new commits by classifying findings as carried forward, superseded, resolved, stale, or anchor-invalid instead of duplicating them blindly. This task must emit the refresh signals the approval layer needs so stale context cannot keep an old draft implicitly valid.
acceptance_criteria:
Refresh maps new validated findings to prior findings using fingerprint and anchor rules rather than duplicating them blindly
Lifecycle transitions cover carried-forward, superseded, resolved, stale, and invalid-anchor states
Refresh emits the invalidation or reconfirmation signals outbound drafts need so stale approvals do not survive silent target drift
close_reason: Implemented refresh lifecycle classification (carried-forward/superseded/resolved/stale/invalid-anchor) with fingerprint+anchor mapping and draft invalidation/reconfirmation signaling, plus dedicated app-core smoke coverage.

title: Implement session-aware CLI review, resume, findings, status, and refresh
description: Create the CLI layer that can infer repo context where possible and drive the usable Roger review loop without relying on the extension. This task must make the shell path real for the primary OpenCode workflow, including review, resume, return, findings, status, and refresh, while staying honest about provider support, ambiguous session resolution, and the approval model.
acceptance_criteria:
Review, resume, return, findings, status, and refresh commands work from the shell on the blessed OpenCode path without the extension installed
Repo-context inference resolves PR targets or sessions only when safe, falls back to an explicit picker when ambiguous, and never silently reuses or retargets stale drafts, approvals, or sessions
CLI output surfaces degraded or unsupported provider capabilities truthfully and keeps posting or other mutation-capable actions behind Roger's explicit draft and approval flow
Smoke coverage includes repo-local re-entry, stale-locator fallback to ResumeBundle or clear guidance, and truthful return after bare-harness dropout
close_reason: Closure cleanup 2026-04-07: acceptance is satisfied by the landed session-aware CLI implementation and its supporting child slices rr-018.1, rr-018.2, and rr-018.3. Live shell surfaces for review, resume, return, findings, status, and refresh are implemented in packages/cli/src/lib.rs and exercised in packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs, with truthful degraded-provider and fail-closed ambiguity behavior defended by rr-011.5, rr-011.6, rr-011.9, and rr-011.10.

title: Implement CLI launch-routing for review/resume/refresh with fail-closed ambiguity handling
description: Implement the non-overlapping launch-routing slice of rr-018: session-aware routing for review, resume, and refresh with explicit disambiguation and fail-closed behavior. This slice intentionally excludes status/findings/return operator surfaces and excludes smoke-suite closeout.
acceptance_criteria:
CLI review/resume/refresh entrypoints resolve repo and session targets only when confidence is sufficient and never guess across ambiguous same-PR candidates
Ambiguous, stale, or conflicting targets route to explicit disambiguation or clear failure guidance rather than silent reuse
Launch-routing behavior is reusable by downstream TUI/bridge/worktree flows without requiring the full rr-018 umbrella to finish
close_reason: Implemented CLI launch-routing slice: review/resume/refresh now fail closed on ambiguity or stale target state, reuse stored session-resolution + launch-routing contracts, and are covered by cli/storage smoke tests.

title: Implement CLI operator surfaces for return/findings/status with truthful degraded capability reporting
description: Implement the non-overlapping operator-surface slice of rr-018: return, findings, and status command behavior plus truthful degraded/unsupported capability output. This slice intentionally excludes launch-routing internals and smoke-suite closeout.
acceptance_criteria:
CLI return/findings/status commands surface review state and provider capability truthfully, including degraded and unsupported cases
Operator-surface commands do not bypass Roger approval boundaries or mutate GitHub state
Output envelopes remain compatible with the robot-facing CLI contract already frozen in rr-005.4
close_reason: Implemented operator-surface provider-capability truthfulness for status/findings/return and verified robot envelope compatibility via roger-cli smoke tests

title: Add CLI smoke coverage for repo re-entry, stale-locator fallback, and truthful dropout return
description: Implement the non-overlapping smoke-validation slice of rr-018: coverage for repo-local re-entry, stale locator fallback to ResumeBundle or clear guidance, and truthful return from bare-harness dropout.
acceptance_criteria:
Smoke coverage exercises repo-local re-entry strong-match and ambiguous-match behavior without silent session guessing
Smoke coverage exercises stale-locator recovery via ResumeBundle fallback or explicit guidance when continuity is degraded
Smoke coverage verifies truthful return path behavior after bare-harness dropout
close_reason: Implemented via CLI smoke tests: repo-ambiguous re-entry blocker, stale-locator ResumeBundle degraded path, and truthful rr return dropout rebind behavior; verified in cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke

title: Build the TUI findings workflow and local approval surfaces
description: Implement the main Roger TUI findings list and detail flow, evidence navigation, bounded clarification hooks, and the local-only approval surface for outbound GitHub drafts.
acceptance_criteria:
Users can enter an active review session, inspect findings and evidence, and understand refresh lineage or degraded state directly in the TUI
Findings can be triaged quickly without triggering GitHub mutation or collapsing approval into ordinary navigation gestures, and bounded clarification remains non-mutating unless the user explicitly requests follow-up work
Outbound drafts can be reviewed, edited, approved, rejected, or invalidated locally before any posting path is exposed, with post-failure recovery still visible in the same queue
notes: Policy: this bead may prepare or open local approval surfaces only; GitHub mutation remains out of scope until rr-008.1 and must stay visibly elevated.
close_reason: Parent acceptance satisfied by closed child slices rr-019.1 and rr-019.2. TUI findings/detail, lineage+degraded visibility, local-only triage/clarification intents, and local draft approval/recovery surfaces are implemented without mutation side effects. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test tui_shell_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core.

title: Implement TUI finding detail and local draft-review queue state model
description: Narrow rr-019 slice in app-core: evolve MinimalTuiShell into a concrete findings/detail + local draft-review state model. This slice stays strictly local and non-mutating (no GitHub posting or remote side effects).
acceptance_criteria:
Shell state includes explicit finding list/detail records with evidence snippets, refresh lineage, and degraded-state markers visible in the detail view
Triage and clarification actions are represented as local intent records only and cannot trigger posting or external mutation paths
Local draft review queue supports review/edit/approve/reject/invalidate transitions entirely in-shell, with explicit pending-post visibility and no hidden post side effects
notes: Validation contract: int\n- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test tui_shell_smoke
close_reason: Implemented app-core TUI findings/local-approval shell slice: added structured findings list+detail state (evidence snippets, refresh lineage, degraded reason), local triage and clarification intent recording, and local draft-review queue transitions (review/edit/approve/reject/invalidate) with pending-post visibility but no posting side effects. Validation: cargo test -p roger-app-core --test tui_shell_smoke (6 passed).

title: Add TUI active-session switching and draft post-failure recovery visibility
description: Second rr-019 slice: model active-session navigation in MinimalTuiShell and keep post-failure recovery visible in the same local draft queue without introducing posting side effects.
acceptance_criteria:
Shell state tracks multiple active sessions and supports explicit next/previous/session-id switching without mutating external state
Local draft queue records post-failure outcome metadata and keeps recovery status visible in the same approval queue view
All new session-switching and draft-failure transitions remain local-only and preserve explicit no-auto-post behavior
notes: Validation contract: int\n- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test tui_shell_smoke
close_reason: Implemented second rr-019 slice in app-core TUI shell: added active-session navigation state (next/previous/by-id switching with chrome updates) and explicit draft post-failure recovery visibility in the local draft queue (failure reason + recovery hint) while preserving no-auto-post behavior. Validation: cargo test -p roger-app-core --test tui_shell_smoke (7 passed).

title: Model outbound drafts and posted-action audit trail
description: Define the local representation for review comments, questions, and suggestions awaiting approval, including grouped draft batches, approval invalidation rules, and immutable posted-action lineage.
acceptance_criteria:
Outbound drafts and draft batches are linked to findings, one Roger session, and one immutable review target tuple
Approval binds to exact payload hash and target identity, and affected approvals are invalidated on refresh, rebase, retarget, or worktree or instance changes
Posted payload, posting outcome, and remote identifiers are snapshotted locally so Roger can audit exactly what was approved and what was sent
No posting-capable surface may treat a persisted approval as valid unless refresh and target-identity checks still pass at post time
close_reason: Implemented outbound draft batch linkage validation, post-time approval gate checks, and audit-lineage smoke coverage.

title: Implement read-safe GitHub adapter and validate the browser launch bridge
description: Resolve PR metadata, anchor targets, and daemonless browser-to-local launch or resume contracts through Roger-owned read-safe adapter boundaries. This bead must stay strictly pre-posting: mutation-capable adapter entrypoints remain out of scope here and must fail closed until rr-008.1 exists.
acceptance_criteria:
PR targets and anchor metadata can be resolved locally through Roger-owned read-safe adapter calls, and any mutation-capable GitHub path remains stubbed or fail-closed until rr-008.1
Native Messaging primary bridge works on supported browsers including Edge without a persistent daemon, custom URL remains a thin convenience or recovery path only, and no local HTTP or WebSocket bridge becomes required
Bridge and adapter degradation paths are truthful, preserve approval safety, and fail closed when target identity, local Roger state, or launch prerequisites are missing
Packaging and install responsibilities for the companion binary and host manifest remain explicit and separate from core Roger CLI or TUI installation
notes: Policy: no raw gh review writes, no hidden posting behavior, and no approval-side effects in this bead. Browser launch or resume may enter Roger, but only rr-008.1 may perform GitHub mutation from stored approved drafts.
close_reason: Verified rr-021 parent acceptance from shipped child slices rr-021.1/rr-021.2: read-safe adapter remains mutation-blocked and bridge remains daemonless with fail-closed preflight plus Native Messaging + custom URL fallback. Validation run: cargo test -p roger-github-adapter; cargo test -p roger-bridge; rg -n 'manifest|Native Messaging|install|companion binary|bridge host|separate|CLI|TUI|Edge|Brave|Chrome' docs/EXTENSION_PACKAGING_AND_RELEASE_CONTRACT.md -S.

title: Implement read-safe GitHub adapter
description: Implement the Roger-owned GitHub adapter for repo and PR target resolution, metadata fetching, and anchor validation without exposing mutation capability or bypassing Roger approval flow.
acceptance_criteria:
Repo and PR targets plus anchor metadata can be resolved locally through Roger-owned adapter calls without raw review writes
Adapter caches read-safe metadata and exposes explicit failure reasons when target or anchor state cannot be resolved
Any mutation-capable GitHub path remains absent or fail-closed until rr-008.1 and later posting-bead work land
close_reason: Implemented read-safe GitHub adapter in packages/github-adapter. Trait ReadSafeGitHubAdapter provides check_gh_available, resolve_pr, list_changed_files, validate_anchor. Live GhCliAdapter wraps gh CLI. StubGitHubAdapter for testing. MutationBlocked error makes write paths fail-closed. Validation: cargo test -p roger-github-adapter — 16/16 tests pass (8 unit, 8 integration smoke). cargo check --workspace clean.

title: Implement and validate browser launch bridge
description: Implement the Native Messaging primary bridge and custom URL fallback for browser-to-local handoff without a persistent daemon.
acceptance_criteria:
Native Messaging supports launch or resume handoff on Chrome, Edge, and Brave with bounded readback only
Custom URL launch remains a thin fallback or recovery path rather than the primary bridge
Missing local Roger state, install problems, or no-status bridge modes fail closed into explicit guidance rather than hidden mutation or background coordination
close_reason: Implemented browser launch bridge in packages/bridge. Native Messaging: read_native_message/write_native_message with 4-byte LE length prefix protocol, 1MB size guard. Custom URL: roger://launch/<owner>/<repo>/<pr>[?action=&instance=] parser. NativeHostManifest generation for Chrome/Edge/Brave with platform-specific install paths. BridgePreflight fail-closed checks. handle_bridge_intent validates action whitelist (start_review/resume_review/show_findings/refresh_review), rejects unknown actions. Validation: cargo test -p roger-bridge — 20/20 tests pass (14 unit, 6 integration smoke). No warnings.

title: Implement extension UI with bounded v1 status behavior
description: Inject Roger actions into GitHub PR pages, but keep the 0.1.0 extension honest: launch, resume, refresh, and bounded status matter most, and the extension never becomes a state owner or approval surface.
acceptance_criteria:
Launch, resume, refresh, and bounded status or no-status behavior are explicit and route only through Roger-owned bridge contracts
The extension remains a thin surface over Roger-owned contracts, does not own review state beyond ephemeral UI preferences, and never treats browser DOM state as authoritative review state
No posting, approval, or hidden background coordination is introduced here, and missing local Roger state fails closed into explicit install, launch, or recovery guidance rather than ambient mutation
notes: Parent closeout after rr-022.1 delivered bounded extension launch UI: PR-page actions, Native Messaging primary + custom URL fallback, and honest launch-only/no-status behavior with no approval/posting controls. Closing if parent acceptance is fully satisfied by this delivered slice.
close_reason: Closed after rr-022.1 implementation delivered parent acceptance: GitHub PR-page extension actions for start/resume/refresh/findings route through Roger bridge contracts (Native Messaging primary, custom URL fallback), extension remains launch-only with explicit no-status guidance when readback unavailable, and no approval/posting controls or hidden background coordination were introduced. Validation referenced from rr-022.1: bash -n scripts/extension/validate_manifest.sh; scripts/extension/validate_manifest.sh; node --check apps/extension/src/background/main.js && node --check apps/extension/src/content/main.js; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-bridge.

title: Implement worktree preparation and conservative named-instance sharing

close_reason: Implemented worktree-manager package: preflight classification (NotNeeded/Recommended/Required), resource plan resolution (isolated DB/artifacts/logs/cache per instance), WorktreeOps trait with GitWorktreeOps live impl and StubWorktreeOps test double, InstanceRegistry with conservative sharing (same-PR ambiguity fails closed, retarget invalidates approvals, explicit teardown). title: Persist worktree/named-instance preflight plans for rr-023 routing
