title: Map persona journeys and E2E contracts into owned proof and implementation beads
description: Turn PERSONA_JOURNEYS_AND_CHAOS_RECOVERY.md, REVIEW_FLOW_MATRIX.md, TESTING.md, and the six major E2E scenarios into explicit bead ownership. This epic exists because the current graph still describes provider and lifecycle work mostly by mechanism, while the persona docs describe operator-visible journeys, dropout/return chaos paths, and happy-path proof obligations that must also be represented directly.
acceptance_criteria: The graph contains explicit leaves that map PJ-01 through PJ-06 and E2E-01 through E2E-06 to owned flow families, invariants, proof lanes, and any missing follow-on implementation slices. Persona and chaos-recovery support claims stop living only in matrix docs and instead have named beads that can narrow or widen support truth honestly. The resulting graph makes it obvious which user-visible journeys are already owned by code/proof and which still need follow-on work.
notes:
Primary sources: PERSONA_JOURNEYS_AND_CHAOS_RECOVERY.md, REVIEW_FLOW_MATRIX.md, TESTING.md, TEST_EXECUTION_TIERS_AND_E2E_BUDGET.md, RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, and VALIDATION_INVARIANT_MATRIX.md.

Execution posture: this epic is mapping-first and process-first. Agents should use persona docs, flow families, invariant ownership, and E2E-budget rules to decide what proof already exists, what proof should stay cheap, and where a missing product or recovery slice needs its own follow-on bead. Do not silently absorb missing implementation into the persona-mapping work; create explicit follow-on beads when journeys expose real product gaps.

title: Implement executable E2E-02 cross-surface review continuity with recall
description: Turn budget slot E2E-02 into a real executable heavyweight proof for browser/bridge entry, terminal resume, TUI triage, and recall truth across one review. This bead exists because rr-ouyz found only cheaper integration and acceptance coverage plus docs, not an owned executable suite for the cross-surface continuity promise.
acceptance_criteria:
1. An executable suite and suite metadata exist for budget slot E2E-02.
2. The suite enters from a supported browser or bridge path, resumes the same review from another terminal or TUI surface, triages findings, and proves continuity state plus local draft state survive the surface handoff.
3. The suite asserts requested versus resolved query mode, retrieval_mode, scope and provenance buckets, candidate posture, and degraded lexical-only behavior when semantic retrieval is unavailable.
4. Closeout records the exact run command and narrows support wording if any branch remains owned only by cheaper suites.
notes: Validation contract: run the executable E2E in operator-stability or release-candidate conditions after the cheaper truth slices land. Do not close by metadata or docs alone; this bead is the missing executable-proof owner discovered by rr-ouyz.

title: Implement executable E2E-03 TUI-first review with memory-assisted triage
description: Turn budget slot E2E-03 into a real executable proof for the TUI-first operating model. rr-ouyz found that the journey is described in docs and partly defended by cheaper search and TUI suites, but there is no owned executable suite proving memory-assisted triage, continuity, and draft refinement together.
acceptance_criteria:
1. An executable suite and suite metadata exist for budget slot E2E-03.
2. The suite starts from CLI or TUI on a concrete review target, uses prior-review recall during TUI triage, records at least three distinct triage outcomes, and refines a local outbound draft or suggestion without direct GitHub posting.
3. The suite proves truthful memory posture in the TUI, including retrieval mode, scope bucket, provenance buckets, candidate versus promoted distinction, and lexical-only degraded behavior when semantics are unavailable.
4. Closeout records the exact run command and keeps any residual failure or recovery proof assigned to cheaper suites instead of overclaiming TUI-wide parity.
notes: Validation contract: executable E2E plus cheaper TUI/search integration anchors. Do not close by adding metadata or matrix prose alone; this bead exists because rr-ouyz found the major TUI-first journey had no executable proof owner.

title: Implement executable E2E-04 refresh and draft reconciliation after new commits
description: Turn budget slot E2E-04 into a real executable refresh-and-reconciliation proof. rr-ouyz found named unit, property, and integration owners for invalidation, reconciliation, and recovery, but no owned executable suite for the full new-commits journey that Roger still budgets as a heavyweight proof.
acceptance_criteria:
1. An executable suite and suite metadata exist for budget slot E2E-04.
2. The suite starts from an active review with triage and at least one local outbound draft, introduces new commits or equivalent target drift, runs Roger's normal reconciliation path, and proves finding lineage across new, carried-forward, and resolved or stale outcomes.
3. The suite proves draft approval is either revalidated or visibly reconfirmation-required before posting, with preserved audit history and truthful recovery state after refresh.
4. Closeout records the exact run command and keeps lower-cost invalidation and retry guarantees assigned to the cheaper suites that still own them.
notes: Validation contract: executable E2E after cheaper lifecycle and outbound recovery proofs land. This bead is the missing heavy-proof owner that rr-ouyz surfaced for the refresh journey; it must not close on docs or metadata alone.

title: Implement executable E2E-05 browser setup and first PR-page launch
description: Turn budget slot E2E-05 into a real executable first-use browser proof. rr-ouyz found supported-browser smoke and bridge integration coverage, but no owned executable suite for the full setup-plus-first-launch journey Roger budgets as a heavyweight E2E.
acceptance_criteria:
1. An executable suite and suite metadata exist for budget slot E2E-05.
2. The suite starts from an unconfigured or partially configured browser path, runs Roger's setup and doctor surfaces, proves truthful Native Messaging host registration or honest repair guidance, and launches from a supported GitHub PR page into the correct local Roger session.
3. The suite records which supported browser path was exercised and preserves launch or repair transcripts as proof artifacts.
4. Closeout records the exact run command and keeps lower-cost bridge-smoke and install-repair claims assigned to the cheaper suites that still own them.
notes: Validation contract: executable browser E2E after init, resolved-config, and bridge-contract truth land. This bead is the missing heavy-proof owner discovered by rr-ouyz; browser smoke alone is not enough to close it.

title: Implement executable E2E-06 bare-harness dropout and return continuity
description: Turn budget slot E2E-06 into a real executable proof for the OpenCode-first fallback story. rr-ouyz found acceptance and smoke anchors for dropout and return, but no owned executable suite proving deliberate harness dropout, safe continuation, and truthful return continuity under the stricter lifecycle and baseline rules.
acceptance_criteria:
1. An executable suite and suite metadata exist for budget slot E2E-06.
2. The suite starts a Roger review, intentionally drops out to the supported bare harness, proves that approval and posting state do not mutate implicitly during the dropout segment, and returns through Roger to the same session with truthful continuity.
3. The suite proves session baseline and continuity posture survive the dropout and return path without guessing from ambient shell state.
4. Closeout records the exact run command and keeps cheaper acceptance or smoke coverage assigned to the narrower fallback cases they still own.
notes: Validation contract: executable E2E after transactional lifecycle, stale-event rejection, OpenCode re-audit, and baseline continuity land. This bead is the missing heavy-proof owner discovered by rr-ouyz; existing acceptance and smoke anchors are not the same as this product-defining journey.

title: Readiness review: bead graph coverage versus the canonical plan
description: Audit whether the current bead graph fully covers the canonical plan and identify any missing or under-specified planning work that should be captured before implementation.
close_reason: Audited bead graph against canonical plan, created missing planning beads, and wired blockers for under-specified contracts.

title: Clean up redundant semantic_assets_verified in PriorReviewLookupQuery
description: The  field in  is redundant as the verification is now performed internally within the  function. This bead removes the redundant field from  and updates its usage.
close_reason: Removed redundant semantic_assets_verified from PriorReviewLookupQuery and updated prior_review_lookup flow to derive semantic readiness internally (asset verification + sidecar readiness + candidate derivation), with no external semantic_assets_verified input. Validation: rch exec -- cargo build --workspace; rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (pass).

title: Introduce SearchPlan, retrieval objectives, and fusion orchestration on top of planner truth
description:
Implement the first Roger-owned retrieval-control layer that lives above explicit planner intent and canonical recall truth. This bead owns a real SearchPlan or equivalent retrieval-objective object with Roger-specific fields for active scope set, session identity, anchor set, trust floor, candidate-versus-promoted allowances, degraded semantic flags, and strategy selection across lexical, semantic, prior-review, and candidate-audit lanes.

The point is to make retrieval composition deliberate and inspectable. SearchPlan should choose and justify strategy mix intentionally instead of letting the app fan out into ad hoc retrieval branches. This is the control-plane lift from planner truth into explicit retrieval objectives, not the later storage hot-path optimization or every UI projection.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Roger materializes a SearchPlan or equivalent retrieval-objective layer with explicit fields for scope, session baseline, trust floor, candidate visibility, degraded semantic posture, and retrieval strategy selection.
2. Retrieval composition across lexical, semantic, prior-review, and candidate-audit lanes is deliberate and explainable rather than implicit fan-out.
3. The SearchPlan contract preserves Roger-owned scope and authority rules: it cannot silently widen memory scope, trust posture, or candidate-versus-promoted allowances.
4. Validation contract: unit coverage for plan construction and strategy selection plus integration coverage showing planned retrieval objectives are inspectable and deterministic.
notes:
Primary sources: QMD_RETRIEVAL_LIFT_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md sections on SearchPlan, lexical retrieval, vector retrieval, and fusion, plus the Round 06 search-truth beads. This bead is intentionally large: it should settle the retrieval control plane that later uplift beads build on.

Execution answer: allow substantial implementation freedom in pursuit of better global retrieval quality, inspectability, and performance. Agents may reshape planner structures, pipeline composition, and ranking orchestration if that improves end-to-end retrieval truth and system behavior, but they must keep scope limits, degraded-mode semantics, and memory-authority boundaries explicit and Roger-owned.

title: Restore roger-cli lib-test compile gate for release/install command paths
description: The release shell tooling passes, but the current roger-cli lib-test target is broken at compile time when exercised directly. Local verification on 2026-04-02 found cargo test -p roger-cli --lib update_fails_closed_for_local_build_without_release_metadata and related bridge/install test filters fail to compile packages/cli/src/lib.rs because symbols such as DEFAULT_OPENCODE_BIN and now_ts are not resolving in the tested build. This bug exists because the earlier release beads validated script-level behavior and selected smoke tests, but did not hold the roger-cli lib-test target itself as a closeout gate. Acceptance: (1) the roger-cli lib-test target compiles again; (2) the release/install-related unit tests pass, including update_fails_closed_for_local_build_without_release_metadata, bridge_install_and_uninstall_manage_assets_with_checksums, and bridge_pack_extension_emits_checksum_asset_manifest; (3) an event-driven CI lane runs this target on pull_request so the regression cannot hide behind shell-only release tests again. Validation contract: int/unit layer via cargo test -p roger-cli --lib and CI evidence from a pull_request-triggered workflow or an existing PR lane updated to include this target.
close_reason: Acceptance verified in current state: lib test target compiles and required tests pass via rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --lib; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --lib update_fails_closed_for_local_build_without_release_metadata; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --lib bridge_install_and_uninstall_manage_assets_with_checksums; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --lib bridge_pack_extension_emits_checksum_asset_manifest. PR CI gate present in .github/workflows/validation-pr.yml with pull_request trigger and explicit cargo test -p roger-cli --lib step.

title: Round 06 provider-truth, worker-boundary, and Copilot admission wave
description:
Implement the missing follow-on program that the canonical plan, the provider-parity/Copilot side-plan, and the Round 06 reconciliation brief now treat as current scope. The older graph already covers the first implementation slice for OpenCode continuity, bounded providers, the bridge, posting data model, and validation scaffolding, but it does not yet containerize the newer proof groups that make those support claims honest under the stricter anti-gap rules.

This epic exists so no accepted current-scope concept remains stranded only in planning documents. It owns the bead expansion for the review-worker boundary, search-planner truth, build/help/provider-surface truth, transactional lifecycle hardening, bootstrap and provider-aware doctor surfaces, explicit outbound draft/approve/post productization, provider-claim re-audit, GitHub Copilot CLI admission, and the proof/audit hardening required before Roger widens support claims again.

Execution rule: child beads must stay proof-shaped. No child may close on adapter coverage or docs-only cleanup when the promise depends on live CLI exposure, verified provider/session binding, recovery behavior, or validation evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
The bead graph contains explicit child groups for worker boundary, search-planner truth, build/help truth, transactional lifecycle, bootstrap/doctor, outbound CLI productization, provider re-audit, Copilot admission, and proof/audit hardening.
Dependency structure matches the intended implementation order closely enough that agents can claim non-overlapping leaf work without inventing side plans.
No critical concept from the accepted provider-parity/Copilot transition remains represented only in docs without at least one bead owning implementation or validation responsibility.
New or widened provider claims remain gated behind their own proof beads rather than being inherited from older OpenCode/Gemini/CLI work.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 rr-x51h group epics after unique children were reparented.

title: G0: Implement the review-worker runtime and rr agent boundary
description:
Create the first real implementation lane for the manager-versus-worker boundary that the canonical plan and REVIEW_WORKER_RUNTIME_AND_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT now freeze. Roger already has review sessions, prompt execution, findings materialization, and bounded provider adapters, but the review worker still risks remaining implicit prompt glue unless the graph owns this seam directly.

This group owns the shift from ambient stage execution to explicit worker tasks, manager-owned tool/context access, and stable in-session transport semantics. It must keep the worker transport distinct from operator-facing CLI/robot surfaces, preserve Roger as the authority over findings, memory, approval, and posting, and leave MCP as an optional later adapter rather than the foundation.
acceptance_criteria:
The graph contains leaf work for canonical worker objects/persistence, rr agent transport and gateway behavior, and worker-boundary validation.
The group is explicit about what the worker may read, propose, and return, and what it may not mutate directly.
Dependencies make it possible to implement the worker boundary without smearing semantics back into the human CLI surface.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G0 worker-boundary leaf set under rr-x51h.1.*.

title: Materialize canonical worker objects and the ReviewTask or WorkerStageResult ledger
description:
Implement the Roger-owned runtime objects that the worker contract depends on: ReviewTask, WorkerContextPacket, WorkerInvocation, WorkerToolCallEvent, WorkerStageResult, and the persisted identity/binding fields that let Roger reject stale or cross-session results. This bead exists because the current graph has prompt execution, findings materialization, and session continuity, but it still does not have a first-class persisted model for bounded worker tasks inside a provider session.

The slice should stay transport-neutral. It owns the data model, persistence, and manager-side lifecycle semantics for worker tasks/results, including task nonce round-tripping, task strategy metadata, provenance, and result-state handling for findings, clarification requests, memory review requests, and follow-up proposals.
acceptance_criteria:
Canonical worker/task/result objects are persisted or otherwise materialized in Roger-owned state with stable schema and binding fields.
Task nonce, session/run/task identity, and result-envelope semantics are strict enough that stale or cross-session submissions fail closed.
The slice defines exactly what result states Roger can materialize from a worker response without giving the worker direct finding/memory/posting mutation power.
Validation contract: unit/schema coverage for round-trip serialization plus storage/app-core integration coverage for stale-binding and malformed-result rejection.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G0 worker-boundary leaf set under rr-x51h.1.*.

title: Implement the rr agent transport and manager-owned worker gateway
description:
Build the first concrete worker transport over the canonical worker operations using the dedicated rr agent namespace rather than overloading the human CLI or robot surfaces. This bead owns the in-session transport shape, command routing, nonce/session/run/task binding checks, and the manager-owned gateway operations that the worker may call for review context, memory lookup, finding detail, artifact excerpts, status, and result submission.

The goal is not to make rr agent a general automation surface. The goal is to give Roger-managed review workers a stable local transport that is clearly separate from rr --robot and from human operator commands, with explicit denial behavior when the worker asks for out-of-scope operations or loses task binding.
acceptance_criteria:
rr agent exists as a distinct machine-facing surface for bound in-session worker calls and is clearly separate from rr --robot.
The transport requires valid ReviewSession, ReviewRun, ReviewTask, and task_nonce binding and fails closed on mismatch or expired binding.
Manager-owned worker operations cover bounded context reads and result submission without exposing direct posting, approval, or unrestricted memory mutation.
Validation contract: integration coverage for successful worker reads/results plus negative-path coverage for stale binding, forbidden operations, and malformed submissions.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G0 worker-boundary leaf set under rr-x51h.1.*.

title: Add worker-boundary validation and bounded provider acceptance coverage
description:
Convert the new worker boundary into named proof instead of leaving it as an implementation-only seam. This bead owns the validation lanes for canonical worker object round-trips, rr agent request/response behavior, denied-operation semantics, and at least one support-appropriate provider path where a real Roger-managed worker retrieves context and submits a WorkerStageResult that Roger validates and materializes.

The goal is to keep the new worker model from becoming another hidden abstraction that appears complete before it has named proof. The coverage should stay cheaper than a heavyweight E2E where possible and should align with the provider tier actually being claimed.
acceptance_criteria:
Unit/schema coverage proves the worker object model round-trips and fails closed on nonce or binding mismatch.
Integration coverage proves rr agent context reads, bounded retrieval, result submission, and explicit denials.
At least one provider acceptance path shows a Roger-managed worker using the gateway and returning a validated WorkerStageResult.
Validation metadata is wired into the shared harness/CI tiers so worker support claims are visible in the matrix instead of ad hoc.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G0 worker-boundary leaf set under rr-x51h.1.*.

title: G0B: Make search-planner truth and degraded retrieval explicit
description:
Round 06 elevated search-planner truth as a separate current-scope gap instead of letting it hide inside the already-landed search and memory slice. Roger now has repo-first retrieval and lexical-versus-semantic degraded behavior, but the current graph does not yet own the planning seam that turns compatibility ingress like query_mode=auto into explicit planned search intent before execution.

This group exists so the implementation cannot drift back toward a quiet “just run the simple query” path. It owns the planned-versus-resolved search intent model, RecallEnvelope truth fields, SessionBaselineSnapshot and worker-facing recall posture, and recovery_scan as an explicit degraded mode instead of a silent fallback.
acceptance_criteria:
Leaf beads cover search-intent planning, recall-envelope truth fields and degraded recovery_scan behavior, and validation for resolved versus requested planner intent.
The group keeps planner truth separate from general search engine implementation and from wider provider work.
The resulting graph makes search-planner behavior testable and visible to future CLI, TUI, and worker surfaces.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 rr-x51h group epics after unique children were reparented.

title: Round 06 G8.1: Resolve query_mode=auto into explicit planned search intent before execution
description:
Implement the planning seam between compatibility ingress and executed retrieval so query_mode=auto stops behaving like a silent front door. The canonical search contract now says omitted or auto intent is migration-only ingress and must resolve to one concrete planned search posture before retrieval executes, results surface, or baseline state is persisted.

This bead owns the Roger-side planner step that turns requested ingress into explicit planned search intent such as exact_lookup, recall, related_context, candidate_audit, or promotion_review, with deterministic resolution rules and fail-closed handling for ambiguous or disallowed combinations. It should change the product contract from implicit heuristics to an inspectable planned intent.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Search entrypoints resolve compatibility ingress into one explicit planned search intent before retrieval executes or results are surfaced.
2. Requested intent versus planned intent is represented in Roger-owned state or envelopes clearly enough for later CLI, TUI, worker, and continuity lanes to consume without guessing.
3. Compatibility ingress remains available only as a migration aid and cannot silently become the steady-state search contract again.
4. Validation contract: unit coverage for planner resolution rules plus integration coverage showing auto ingress resolves to explicit intent deterministically and fails closed on invalid combinations.
notes: Primary sources: SEARCH_MEMORY_LIFECYCLE_AND_SEMANTIC_ASSET_POLICY.md sections on search intent versus engine mode and session baseline for recall posture. This bead owns the decisive contract change from implicit search heuristics to explicit planned intent.
close_reason: Implemented explicit search-query planning so rr search resolves omitted or auto ingress into concrete planned modes before lookup, fails closed on anchor-free related_context and unsupported promotion_review, and projects requested versus resolved intent through CLI and worker-owned envelopes. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-app-core --test search_query_planning_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-app-core --test review_worker_contract_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke search_reports_truthful_degraded_mode_and_stable_robot_fields -- --exact; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke search_resolves_auto_to_exact_lookup_and_blocks_anchor_free_related_context -- --exact; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke

title: Round 06 G8.2: Implement RecallEnvelope truth fields for requested versus resolved retrieval semantics
description:
Make Roger tell the full truth about why a search item surfaced and which search posture actually executed. This bead owns the canonical RecallEnvelope fields for requested_query_mode, resolved_query_mode, retrieval_mode, scope bucket, memory lane, trust or warning posture, degraded flags, explainability, and citation posture so every CLI, TUI, and worker retrieval surface projects the same underlying truth.

This is the canonical provenance slice for search. If it is too thin, every later surface will drift back into score-only or provider-specific explanations.
acceptance_criteria:
1. RecallEnvelope carries the requested-versus-resolved search truth, retrieval_mode, scope bucket, memory lane, trust or warning posture, degraded flags, explanation summary, and citation or surface posture needed to answer why an item surfaced now.
2. CLI, TUI, and worker retrieval are projections of the same RecallEnvelope semantics rather than separate search contracts.
3. Candidate, contradicted, and warning-only memory cannot masquerade as cited promoted guidance because the envelope preserves lane and posture truth explicitly.
4. Validation contract: envelope round-trip or projection coverage plus integration assertions that surfaced retrieval explanations match the canonical recall contract.
notes: Recovery_scan degraded-mode semantics remain owned by Round 06 G8.3; this bead is the canonical envelope-truth slice that everything else in the search lane builds on.

title: Round 06 G8.4: Add planner and degraded-retrieval validation across CLI worker and recall surfaces
description:
Add named proof for the new planner boundary instead of assuming existing search coverage is enough. This bead owns validation for planned-versus-resolved query mode behavior, recall-envelope truth fields, candidate-visibility posture, SessionBaselineSnapshot interactions, and degraded recovery_scan handling across the surfaces that will consume search results first: CLI, worker gateway, and later TUI.

The goal is to prevent planner truth from slipping back into implementation detail and to make the degraded-mode contract explicit before more agentic or Copilot-driven retrieval work lands.
acceptance_criteria:
Validation covers requested versus resolved planner intent, recall-envelope truth fields, and explicit degraded recovery_scan behavior.
Coverage exercises at least CLI and worker-facing retrieval paths, with bounded future-proofing for TUI consumption.
Validation metadata is wired into the shared harness and CI tiers instead of staying local to one test file.
The resulting proof makes search-planner behavior a first-class support contract rather than an undocumented implementation habit.

title: G1: Reconcile build, help, and provider-surface truth
description:
The canonical plan and Round 06 both treat the human-facing command surface as product truth, not mere ergonomics. Roger should not keep widening provider work while source-run help, router/provider exposure, README quickstart, robot docs, and status/help language tell different stories about what is actually shipped.

This group owns the literal operator-facing truth pass for the CLI and docs surface: source-run help/build must work, supported provider lists and capability tiers must align across help and docs, and the machine-readable/robot surfaces must stop implying claims the implementation has not yet earned.
acceptance_criteria:
Leaf beads cover source-run help/build truth, provider/router/help/docs alignment, and regression checks that keep those surfaces from drifting again.
The group explicitly reconciles current live support wording instead of relying on future Copilot or lifecycle work to fix the story later.
The resulting surface names only commands and provider tiers that Roger can defend at that point in the implementation.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G1 build/help/provider-surface leaf set under rr-x51h.2.*.

title: Make source-run rr help and build truth a regression-gated product entrypoint
description:
Round 06 called out the from-source help/build path as product truth rather than a dev-only convenience. This bead exists to make cargo-run or source-built rr help a regression-gated entrypoint, so Roger cannot keep shipping docs or provider claims on top of a broken local command surface.

The slice should cover the help path itself, the minimal build/package integrity needed for that path, and the regression checks that keep future manifest/router drift from silently breaking the source-run entrypoint again.
acceptance_criteria:
The from-source rr help/build path works and is defended by an explicit regression check.
Failures in the build/help entrypoint are treated as product-surface regressions, not informal developer friction.
The regression lane is cheap enough to run regularly and specific enough to point at CLI/package truth rather than generic workspace breakage.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G1 build/help/provider-surface leaf set under rr-x51h.2.*.

title: Reconcile router help README and robot docs to one truthful provider story
description:
Roger currently risks telling slightly different stories across CLI routing, help text, README quickstart, robot-docs, and support matrices. This bead owns the literal surface reconciliation so supported provider lists, capability tiers, command availability, and bounded-versus-first-class wording say the same thing everywhere.

This is not a docs-polish bead. It is the product-surface truth pass that prevents provider-claim drift while lifecycle hardening and Copilot admission are still in flight.
acceptance_criteria:
Router/help/docs/robot surfaces name the same supported providers and the same claimed tier or bounded behavior.
No surface implies Copilot live support, direct resume, rr return, or doctor/init availability before the implementation has earned it.
The slice records or enforces the current truthful wording in code and docs together so later provider work does not have to rediscover the mismatch.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G1 build/help/provider-surface leaf set under rr-x51h.2.*.

title: Round 06 G1.4: Surface provider capability truth in status and machine-readable envelopes
description:
Make provider truth visible in the surfaces agents and operators actually inspect after help text: rr status, sessions, robot envelopes, and related machine-readable outputs. This bead exists because narrowing help text alone is not enough if status or JSON payloads still imply stronger support than the provider really has.

The work should make the emitted provider capability story consistent with the canonical tier table and with the currently shipped slice, so bounded providers remain obviously bounded and new providers do not look live before proof exists.
acceptance_criteria:
Status and machine-readable envelopes expose provider capability/tier truth consistently with the current shipped slice.
Bounded-provider outputs remain explicit about degraded or unsupported resume, return, or command capabilities.
Regression coverage proves the machine-readable outputs stay aligned with the help/docs/router story as new providers land.
close_reason: Surfaced shared provider capability truth across rr status, rr resume, rr return, rr sessions, and machine-readable envelopes; bounded providers now expose explicit tier/status/support flags and regression coverage keeps these outputs aligned with help/docs/router truth. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke bounded_provider_outputs_are_truthful_for_status_resume_and_return -- --exact; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke sessions_lists_filters_and_compacts_with_explicit_follow_on_hints -- --exact; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke provider_support_claim_guard_keeps_help_robot_and_docs_in_lockstep -- --exact; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test provider_surface_truth_guard provider_support_truth_guard_matches_live_cli_help_and_docs -- --exact; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test provider_surface_truth_guard

title: G2: Harden lifecycle transactionality and bridge truth
description:
Retrofit the Roger lifecycle so review, resume, refresh, return, and browser-triggered launch no longer rely on loosely related writes or synthetic success states. The canonical plan now makes verified session binding and transactional lifecycle persistence part of the current provider-support scope rather than optional hardening.

This group owns the launch-attempt ledger, real failure classes, transactional persistence for user-visible lifecycle operations, real bridge dispatch through rr --robot, and retry-safe or stale-event behavior. It exists to stop Roger from recording sessions as launched when provider binding was never verified and to make crash recovery truthful.
acceptance_criteria:
Leaf beads cover launch-attempt state, transactional persistence for review/resume/refresh/return, bridge dispatch realism, and stale-event or partial-write recovery behavior.
The group is explicit that durable ReviewSession state must not be finalized before a verified provider session identifier exists.
Validation obligations are attached to the leaf beads rather than left to a later generic cleanup pass.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G2 transactional lifecycle leaf set under rr-x51h.3.*.

title: Add the launch-attempt ledger and verified lifecycle failure classes
description:
Implement the distinct launch-attempt lifecycle that the canonical plan now requires before durable ReviewSession state is finalized. Roger needs a separate ledger for pending, verified_started, verified_reopened, verified_reseeded, failed_preflight, failed_spawn, failed_session_binding, and abandoned so partial provider interactions stop looking like completed review sessions.

This bead owns the data model, state transitions, and failure evidence handling for launch attempts across review, resume, refresh, and return where applicable.
acceptance_criteria:
Launch-attempt state exists separately from durable ReviewSession state with explicit verified and failed classes.
Roger retains failure evidence in the launch-attempt ledger instead of leaving completed-looking sessions behind when provider binding fails.
The slice defines the authoritative transition rules closely enough that later transactional and bridge work can reuse them instead of inventing ad hoc status flags.
Validation contract: storage/app-core tests for allowed transitions, verified binding prerequisites, and failure evidence retention.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G2 transactional lifecycle leaf set under rr-x51h.3.*.

title: Make review resume refresh and return persistence transactional and retry-safe
description:
Retrofit the user-visible lifecycle operations so Roger writes binding, run creation, continuity updates, attention state, and related audit state as one coherent transaction per action. This bead exists because the canonical plan now treats transactional lifecycle semantics as part of the current provider-support contract rather than as later polish.

The slice should make review launch, resume/rebind, refresh run creation, and return handling crash-safe and retry-safe, and it should reject stale lifecycle events instead of replaying partial state on top of newer truth.
acceptance_criteria:
Review, resume, refresh, and return persistence commit transactionally per user-visible action.
Crash or partial failure does not leave Roger with completed-looking lifecycle state that never achieved verified provider binding.
Retry-safe and stale-event behavior is explicit enough that later bridge or Copilot work can depend on it safely.
Validation contract: transaction/crash-recovery integration coverage for partial-write and retry scenarios, including stale-event rejection where relevant.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G2 transactional lifecycle leaf set under rr-x51h.3.*.

title: Replace bridge pretend-success with real rr robot dispatch and canonical session ids
description:
Finish the serious bridge path so browser-triggered launch no longer returns synthetic or incomplete success envelopes. This bead owns the path where the bridge validates preflight, invokes the real rr CLI in machine-readable mode, parses the canonical response, and returns Roger-owned session identifiers only after the actual command succeeds.

The slice must also keep launch-only or no-status bridge behavior honest and must not reintroduce a hidden daemon or ambient current-PR mutation path while fixing the bridge truth problem.
acceptance_criteria:
Bridge dispatch uses the real rr --robot path and returns canonical Roger session ids only on actual success.
No bridge success envelope is emitted with missing or synthetic session identifiers.
Launch-only/no-status bridge behavior remains explicit and fail-closed where full readback is unavailable.
Validation contract: bridge integration/smoke coverage for success, preflight failure, and missing-session-id rejection paths.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G2 transactional lifecycle leaf set under rr-x51h.3.*.

title: G3: Implement bootstrap and provider-aware doctor surfaces
description:
Roger now needs one truthful bootstrap and preflight story across providers instead of letting onboarding drift between README prose, recovery notes, and ad hoc commands. The canonical plan has already settled on rr init as the Roger-owned bootstrap command and rr doctor as the canonical cross-provider preflight/debug surface, but the graph does not yet own the remaining implementation and truth-pass work.

This group owns lightweight init/bootstrap behavior, provider-aware doctor checks, and the docs/help/onboarding reconciliation needed so operators are routed through real shipped commands only.
acceptance_criteria:
Leaf beads cover rr init, provider-aware rr doctor, and the help/onboarding reconciliation required to keep bootstrap/preflight truthful.
Doctor expectations are explicit per provider and fail closed when Roger cannot preflight a claim non-invasively.
The group keeps bootstrap/doctor work separate from generic docs cleanup so product truth is defended by executable surfaces.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 rr-x51h group epics after unique children were reparented.

title: Round 06 G3.2: Implement lightweight rr init for Roger-owned local bootstrap
description:
Implement the Roger-owned bootstrap command that the canonical plan now names explicitly. This bead owns the minimal init path for creating the local store, writing version or metadata markers, verifying basic file permissions, and surfacing bounded provider guidance without pretending provider auth or policy is already verified.

The goal is a truthful bootstrap command, not an everything-installer. It should create the Roger-owned local foundations needed by later doctor, review, and bridge flows while keeping optional provider-specific setup advice clearly separate from completed Roger bootstrap.
acceptance_criteria:
rr init exists and bootstraps the Roger-owned local store safely.
Bootstrap output distinguishes Roger-local setup from provider-specific auth/install follow-up.
The command is truthful about what it created and what still requires doctor or first-launch verification.
Validation contract: CLI integration coverage for clean init, existing-store idempotence or fail-closed behavior, and machine-readable envelope truth where applicable.

title: Round 06 G3.3: Implement provider-aware rr doctor with per-provider preflight and fail-closed guidance
description:
Implement the cross-provider preflight/debug surface the canonical plan and provider-parity transition both now require. rr doctor should validate Roger-local health plus provider-specific prerequisites such as binary presence, repo/worktree location, hook/instruction assets where applicable, and policy/profile resolvability.

The key rule is fail-closed truthfulness: when auth or remote policy cannot be checked non-invasively, doctor should say that explicitly and route the operator to the first real launch or repair command rather than bluffing.
acceptance_criteria:
rr doctor exists and supports a provider-aware preflight surface with truthful outcomes for at least the current live providers plus the planned Copilot lane.
Doctor output distinguishes verified prerequisites from deferred first-launch checks such as auth that cannot be proven non-invasively.
Repair actions are concrete and route through real Roger commands or provider guidance rather than vague docs references.
Validation contract: CLI integration coverage for per-provider doctor output and failure classes, plus machine-readable envelope stability where applicable.

title: Round 06 G3.4: Reconcile onboarding quickstart and recovery guidance to shipped init and doctor commands
description:
Once rr init and rr doctor exist, the public and internal operator guidance needs to stop naming planned or obsolete bootstrap paths. This bead owns the truth pass across README quickstart, onboarding docs, recovery guidance, robot docs, and other operator-facing text so the bootstrap/preflight story routes through real shipped commands only.

This is a product-truth bead, not a cosmetic docs cleanup. It closes the loop between executable bootstrap/preflight surfaces and the guidance operators will actually follow.
acceptance_criteria:
Onboarding, quickstart, recovery, and robot-docs guidance route through the actual shipped init/doctor surfaces and do not imply unimplemented commands.
Docs distinguish Roger-local bootstrap from provider-specific follow-up honestly.
The slice updates the support surfaces that materially affect operator behavior in the same implementation lane as init/doctor, not as a deferred docs-only follow-up.

title: G4: Complete the explicit outbound draft approve post product surface
description:
The data model, post-time gate checks, and posting adapter work are no longer enough by themselves. The canonical plan and provider-parity transition now require a visibly complete product path for draft -> approve -> post, with explicit local state transitions and explicit human control.

This group exists to finish the operator-facing CLI surface for outbound actions without bypassing Roger's safety model. It owns draft materialization commands, approval commands, the operator-facing post path, and the visible state/recovery cues that prevent Roger from treating stored approval as vague permission to mutate GitHub.
acceptance_criteria:
Leaf beads cover draft, approve, and post CLI/state surfaces plus the open posting-adapter implementation lane.
The group keeps adapter implementation distinct from the human-visible product path and requires explicit target/payload identity at each transition.
Refresh, retarget, missing-local-state, and partial-failure recovery expectations are attached to concrete children rather than left implicit.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 rr-x51h group epics after unique children were reparented.

title: Round 06 G4.2: Implement rr draft for local outbound draft materialization and target-bound state
description:
Create the explicit CLI surface for turning Roger findings or grouped findings into local outbound drafts without posting anything remotely. This bead owns the operator-facing draft step, target-binding truth, payload preview lineage, and the local state transitions that make “drafted locally” visible before approval or post is even possible.

The slice should preserve Roger's rule that outbound material is rendered and reviewed locally first. It should not grant approval, mutate GitHub, or infer ambient current-PR context; it should bind the draft to the exact review target and payload that later approval and posting will reference.
acceptance_criteria:
rr draft materializes Roger-owned outbound drafts or draft batches locally with explicit target binding and payload lineage.
The command is non-mutating with respect to GitHub and does not imply approval or posting readiness without the later steps.
Drafted state is queryable by other Roger surfaces instead of remaining hidden inside store internals.
Validation contract: CLI/integration coverage for draft creation, grouped draft handling, and fail-closed behavior on missing target or stale local state.

title: Round 06 G4.3: Implement rr approve with payload-hash binding and invalidation explanation
description:
Create the explicit CLI approval step for local outbound drafts or batches. This bead owns the operator-facing approve action, the payload-hash and target-tuple binding that approval must attach to, and the explanatory behavior when approval is blocked or invalidated by refresh, retarget, rebase, or missing local state.

The slice should make approval visibly separate from drafting and posting. Approval is permission for one exact rendered payload on one exact target, not a generic flag that something like this may be posted later.
acceptance_criteria:
rr approve binds approval to the exact payload hash and immutable target tuple for one draft or batch.
The command explains invalidation or blocked approval states truthfully when refresh, retarget, or local-state drift has revoked eligibility.
Approval remains a local Roger action and does not call GitHub or bypass later post-time revalidation.
Validation contract: CLI/integration coverage for successful approval, invalidation after target drift, and fail-closed rejection when the draft payload or target tuple no longer matches.

title: Implement rr post as the operator-facing path over the approved posting adapter
description:
Wire the operator-facing rr post surface over Roger's explicit approval and posting adapter boundaries. This bead owns the command path that selects already-approved drafts or batches, revalidates target identity and approval freshness at post time, calls the posting adapter, and reports posted, partial, invalidated, or retry-needed outcomes back to the operator.

The command must stay visibly elevated and must not degrade into “whatever is current in this PR.” It should use only Roger-owned stored identifiers and should preserve exact failure or retry information when the adapter or target revalidation blocks the post.
acceptance_criteria:
rr post exists as the operator-facing Roger path over stored approved drafts or batches.
The command revalidates approval freshness and target identity before invoking the posting adapter and never posts from ambient PR focus.
Posted, partial, invalidated, and retry-needed outcomes are reported truthfully and preserved in Roger-owned state.
Validation contract: CLI/integration coverage for successful post, partial failure, approval invalidation at post time, and adapter failure/retry behavior.
close_reason: Superseded by rr-x51h.5.1 after the canonical outbound command surface was consolidated.

title: Round 06 G4.6: Surface outbound draft approval and posting states across status, findings, and recovery surfaces
description:
Make the outbound state machine visible across the Roger surfaces operators already inspect, rather than leaving it implicit in storage rows or only inside one command. This bead owns the surfaced state vocabulary for drafted, awaiting approval, approved, posted, superseded, invalidated, and retry-needed states across rr status, rr findings, and related repair/recovery outputs.

The goal is to keep the outward product path inspectable and auditable. Operators should be able to see why a draft cannot be posted, which approval was revoked, and what remains retryable after partial failure without spelunking internal tables.
acceptance_criteria:
Outbound states are surfaced consistently across the operator paths Roger already exposes, including status/findings or equivalent recovery surfaces.
The surfaced state machine distinguishes drafted, awaiting approval, approved, posted, superseded, invalidated, and retry-needed states clearly enough for operators and future TUI work.
Recovery cues explain why posting is blocked or what can be retried without mutating state behind the operator's back.
Validation contract: CLI/integration coverage for state presentation after draft, approval, invalidation, post success, and partial failure.

title: G5: Re-audit OpenCode and bounded-provider claims under the stricter truth rules
description:
The current repo already contains OpenCode, Codex, Gemini, and Claude support slices, but the canonical plan now applies stricter closure and support-claim rules retroactively. That means previously landed provider work is useful antecedent, not automatic proof that the current live claim level is still honest.

This group owns the re-audit lane: OpenCode must be rechecked against verified launch, continuity quality, worktree identity, and rr return truth; bounded providers must be reconciled against the actual live CLI/help/docs surface; and any unsupported or under-proven claim must narrow immediately instead of waiting for a later feature pass.
acceptance_criteria:
Leaf beads cover OpenCode re-audit, bounded-provider claim narrowing or proof expansion, and the docs/help/status adjustments required by the audit.
The group is explicit that older provider beads are not being discarded; they are being rechecked under newer anti-gap rules.
Any widened provider claim depends on matching proof beads instead of being inherited from historical adapter work.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 rr-x51h group epics after unique children were reparented.

title: Re-audit OpenCode Tier B against verified launch continuity worktree binding and rr return truth
description:
Re-check the blessed OpenCode path under the stricter rules that were written after the earlier OpenCode beads closed. This bead owns the audit of verified launch binding, continuity-quality reporting, worktree-aware target binding, bare-harness dropout, and rr return against the current live implementation and support wording.

The goal is not to redo the original OpenCode work blindly. The goal is to verify that OpenCode still earns the Tier B claims Roger wants to make after transactional lifecycle truth, stricter support-claim discipline, and newer worktree/approval invariants are applied retroactively.
acceptance_criteria:
The live OpenCode path is re-audited against verified launch truth, continuity quality, worktree/target binding, dropout, and rr return claims.
Any mismatch between the current claim level and the current implementation or proof is turned into either a fix follow-on or immediate support wording narrowing.
Closeout names the exact proof or narrowing outcome instead of inheriting historical closure evidence.
Validation contract: support-appropriate acceptance/smoke reruns or targeted new proof where the stricter rules exposed a gap.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h G5/G6/G7 leaf set after the retained Round 06 tree was normalized under rr-x51h.

title: Reconcile Codex Gemini and Claude live claims to the actual bounded provider surface
description:
Audit the bounded providers against the actual current CLI surface and support wording. Roger currently exposes Codex, Gemini, and Claude as bounded live-CLI providers, but the stricter provider-truth program requires the repo to be literal about what is really supported, what is degraded, and what is not implemented at all.

This bead owns the claim audit across CLI routing, help, README/support snapshot, robot/status envelopes, and release/test matrix wording for the bounded providers. It should narrow immediately where proof is thinner than the current wording, and create or link follow-on proof beads only where widening is justified.
acceptance_criteria:
Codex, Gemini, and Claude support wording matches the actual bounded provider behavior Roger currently exposes.
Unsupported capabilities such as locator reopen, dropout, rr return, or in-harness commands are not implied accidentally by docs/help/status output.
Any discrepancy results in explicit narrowing or a linked fix/proof bead, not silent drift.
Validation contract: targeted CLI/help/robot-output coverage plus provider-acceptance or bounded-smoke updates where the claim surface changes.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h G5/G6/G7 leaf set after the retained Round 06 tree was normalized under rr-x51h.

title: Turn provider claim mismatches into explicit narrowing or proof follow-ons
description:
Close the loop after the provider re-audit so the repo cannot discover mismatches and then leave them as tribal knowledge. This bead owns the follow-on graph work for claim mismatches found in the OpenCode and bounded-provider audits: immediate docs/help/status narrowing where support is overclaimed, and explicit fix/proof beads where Roger intends to keep or widen the claim later.

The purpose is to stop the graph from looking healthy while support wording and proof silently diverge again.
acceptance_criteria:
Any mismatch found by the provider re-audit is either narrowed immediately in surfaced wording or represented by an explicit follow-on bead linked from the audit.
The resulting graph makes it obvious which provider claims are earned, which are deliberately narrowed, and which still require additional implementation/proof.
No provider mismatch remains as an untracked note in a close reason or doc margin.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h G5/G6/G7 leaf set after the retained Round 06 tree was normalized under rr-x51h.

title: G6: Admit GitHub Copilot CLI through the same truth rules
description:
GitHub Copilot CLI is now the canonical first-class provider target in the product hierarchy, but it must not get a one-off exception. This group exists to admit Copilot through the same verified-lifecycle, support-tier, safety-policy, and proof rules that the canonical plan now requires for every provider.

The work here starts behind an explicit experimental gate and only widens when Roger has a real provider session identifier, Roger-owned policy/profile control, truthful reseed/reopen/return behavior, and matching validation evidence. It also owns the repository-scoped hook/instructions/profile work that lets Copilot operate in Roger's fail-closed review posture instead of silently inheriting permissive defaults.
acceptance_criteria:
Leaf beads cover session-copilot crate/config wiring, Copilot Tier A verified launch and reseed, Tier B reopen/return only after proof, and the required hook/instructions/operator-guidance surfaces.
The group keeps Copilot behind a feature flag until the support claim is honest.
Copilot-specific safety posture, hook artifacts, and continuity behavior are represented explicitly rather than hidden inside a generic adapter bead.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 rr-x51h group epics after unique children were reparented.

title: Create the session-copilot crate and feature-gated Copilot config or CLI wiring
description:
Create the initial Roger-owned integration surface for GitHub Copilot CLI without claiming live support yet. This bead owns the session-copilot crate, provider slug/config wiring, feature gating, environment/config keys, and the CLI/provider parsing changes needed so Copilot can exist as an implementation lane before it becomes a shipped support claim.

The slice should keep Copilot clearly experimental until later beads prove Tier A and Tier B behavior. It should also avoid centering the integration on ACP preview features; the baseline path remains direct CLI plus Roger-owned hooks.
acceptance_criteria:
A session-copilot crate and feature-gated provider wiring exist in the workspace without widening live support claims prematurely.
CLI/config/env surfaces expose the intended Copilot identifiers and profile hooks behind an explicit experimental gate.
The slice keeps ACP optional and later rather than making preview functionality a baseline dependency.
Validation contract: build/integration coverage for feature-gated provider parsing and config resolution, plus truthful help/output behavior while the gate remains experimental.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h G5/G6/G7 leaf set after the retained Round 06 tree was normalized under rr-x51h.

title: Implement Roger-owned Copilot Tier A launch reseed and raw-capture behavior
description:
Implement the first truthful Copilot support level: Tier A only. This bead owns verified Roger-managed interactive launch, binding to a real Copilot session identifier, raw output/transcript reference capture, review-target binding, ResumeBundle reseed into a fresh session, and truthful continuity-quality reporting without claiming locator reopen or rr return yet.

The slice must follow the same verified lifecycle rules as every other provider. No durable review session should be finalized until Roger has a real Copilot session id and the required launch-attempt evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
Roger can launch a Copilot-backed review session in Tier A mode with a real provider session identifier and verified target binding.
Roger can capture raw output/transcript references durably and reseed a fresh Copilot session from a ResumeBundle truthfully.
Continuity-quality reporting is explicit and does not bluff locator reopen or return support before Tier B work lands.
Validation contract: deterministic acceptance coverage for Copilot Tier A launch, raw capture, reseed, and degraded continuity outcomes.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h G5/G6/G7 leaf set after the retained Round 06 tree was normalized under rr-x51h.

title: Add Copilot hook profiles instructions and review-safe policy defaults
description:
Create the Roger-owned Copilot policy surface that keeps Copilot sessions inside Roger's review-safe posture. This bead owns the repository-scoped hook files, Copilot instructions files, policy profile digests, built-in MCP disabling rules, tool denial behavior, and the operator-visible guidance needed so Copilot does not silently inherit permissive defaults.

The critical requirement is that Copilot review sessions remain read-mostly, deny raw GitHub writes, deny broad shell or file mutation by default, and leave Roger as the authority over memory, approval, and posting.
acceptance_criteria:
Roger-owned Copilot hooks, instruction files, and policy profile defaults exist and are versioned or digested clearly enough for status/audit use.
Default review-mode policy denies raw GitHub writes, broad file writes, broad shell execution, provider-memory substitution, and permissive allow-all modes unless the user explicitly enters a higher-risk mode outside this slice.
Operator guidance explains the Copilot review posture and how first launch or doctor verifies the environment.
Validation contract: deterministic hook/policy tests plus bounded integration coverage for denial and audit behavior.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h G5/G6/G7 leaf set after the retained Round 06 tree was normalized under rr-x51h.

title: Implement Copilot Tier B reopen continuity probes dropout and rr return only after proof
description:
Implement the Copilot follow-on work that can make Copilot a true first-class Tier B provider, but only after the Tier A and lifecycle-truth prerequisites have landed. This bead owns reopen by locator, continuity probes, stale-locator downgrade to reseed, bare-harness continuation in direct Copilot CLI, and explicit rr return support back into Roger when the provider session is still valid enough.

The slice must not widen support wording just because Copilot has a session id. It must prove locator reopen and return behavior against Roger's continuity-quality rules and fail closed when the original session is stale or mismatched.
acceptance_criteria:
Copilot can reopen by locator and report usable versus degraded versus unusable continuity truthfully enough for Tier B claims.
Copilot can degrade to ResumeBundle reseed honestly when locator reopen is stale or mismatched.
Bare-harness continuation and rr return behave as explicit Roger-supported flows rather than informal human convenience paths.
Validation contract: Copilot acceptance and, where the claim level warrants it, real-provider smoke for reopen, degraded reseed, and return behavior.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h G5/G6/G7 leaf set after the retained Round 06 tree was normalized under rr-x51h.

title: G7: Add provider proof and audit hardening for the new claim level
description:
The newer provider-truth program needs its own proof lane instead of assuming older acceptance or smoke coverage will stretch to cover new claim levels. Once lifecycle truth, doctor/init, outbound CLI surfaces, provider re-audit, and Copilot admission begin landing, Roger also needs the supporting doubles, crash-recovery suites, audit artifacts, and support-claim gates that prevent another over-closed provider epic.

This group owns the hardening work that turns implementation slices into defensible product claims: deterministic Copilot doubles, transaction/crash-recovery coverage, provider-aware audit artifact classes, policy-digest status output, and real-provider smoke admission rules for first-class surfaces.
acceptance_criteria:
Leaf beads cover deterministic Copilot/provider doubles, crash-recovery proof, audit artifact classes and policy-digest status output, and smoke-lane gates for widened support claims.
The group explicitly separates local deterministic acceptance from the rarer real-provider smoke required for first-class claims.
Resulting support claims remain narrow when proof is incomplete and widen only when their specific hardening beads are closed honestly.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 rr-x51h group epics after unique children were reparented.

title: Add deterministic Copilot doubles and provider-acceptance suites for the new claim levels
description:
Add the deterministic provider test doubles and provider-acceptance suites needed for Copilot and for any widened provider claim from the re-audit lane. This bead owns fake Copilot session-state and hook payload fixtures, acceptance suites for Copilot Tier A and later Tier B, and any updated bounded-provider acceptance coverage needed once live wording changes.

The goal is to keep most proof cheap and replayable so Roger does not force every provider truth question into a heavyweight E2E or manual smoke path.
acceptance_criteria:
Deterministic Copilot doubles/fixtures exist for launch, hook, transcript, and continuity scenarios needed by the planned claim levels.
Provider-acceptance suites cover the concrete Tier A and Tier B behaviors Roger claims for Copilot and any widened bounded-provider support.
Validation metadata is wired into the shared harness/CI tiers and does not rely on ad hoc local test files only.
The resulting proof lane keeps deterministic acceptance separate from later real-provider smoke admission.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h G5/G6/G7 leaf set after the retained Round 06 tree was normalized under rr-x51h.

title: Add transaction and crash-recovery proof for launch-attempt and lifecycle partial failures
description:
Turn the lifecycle hardening work into named crash-recovery proof instead of assuming transactions are correct because the code compiles. This bead owns the integration tests for crash or interruption between provider launch and Roger commit, partial lifecycle persistence, retry-safe re-entry, and the failure classes that should leave launch-attempt evidence without final ReviewSession success.

The main purpose is to defend the new launch-attempt and transactional lifecycle rules against over-closure.
acceptance_criteria:
Crash-recovery coverage exercises failure between provider interaction and Roger commit, partial lifecycle persistence, retry-safe recovery, and stale-event handling.
Tests prove Roger does not leave completed-looking session state behind when verified binding never happened.
Failure artifacts point to lifecycle truth rather than generic provider errors.
Validation metadata is wired into the shared harness/CI tiers so crash-recovery proof is visible in the matrix.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h G5/G6/G7 leaf set after the retained Round 06 tree was normalized under rr-x51h.

title: Implement provider-aware audit artifact classes and policy-digest status output
description:
Implement the audit and operator-visibility layer that the provider-parity/Copilot plan kept detailed but the live graph never captured explicitly. This bead owns canonical artifact classes for launch attempts, hook captures, tool-denial events, provider transcript references, and continuity probe results, plus the status/output fields that tell the operator which policy profile, hook version, and instruction digest were actually active for a session.

The goal is to make provider safety posture and audit evidence inspectable without turning provider-local state into Roger's source of truth.
acceptance_criteria:
Canonical provider-aware audit artifact classes exist for launch attempts, hook events, denials, transcript references, and continuity probes.
Status or comparable output surfaces expose provider, policy profile, policy digest or version, hook profile version, instruction digest, built-in MCP posture, and worktree root where relevant.
The implementation keeps Roger authoritative while still preserving provider-side continuity evidence references.
Validation contract: storage/output integration coverage for artifact recording and status-surface truth.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h G5/G6/G7 leaf set after the retained Round 06 tree was normalized under rr-x51h.

title: Gate first-class provider wording on real-provider smoke admission only when warranted
description:
Make the support-claim gate explicit for first-class external surfaces so Roger widens wording only when the claim level truly warrants real-provider smoke. This bead owns the admission rules and the matching smoke lane for Copilot or any other first-class provider path that moves beyond deterministic acceptance and into live product claims.

The point is not to force every provider change into a heavyweight E2E. The point is to ensure Roger has one explicit place where real-provider smoke becomes mandatory for a first-class claim and where the claim must remain narrow if that proof is absent.
acceptance_criteria:
The graph and validation metadata make it explicit when real-provider smoke is required for a first-class claim and when deterministic acceptance is enough for a bounded claim.
Copilot or other first-class provider wording remains narrow until the matching smoke evidence exists.
The admission rule is wired into release/test/support surfaces instead of living only in prose.
Validation contract: release or gated-lane smoke metadata plus support-surface checks that fail or narrow claims when smoke evidence is missing.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h G5/G6/G7 leaf set after the retained Round 06 tree was normalized under rr-x51h.

title: Round 06 provider-truth canonicalization and live surface freeze
description: Convert the accepted provider-parity, transactional-launch, and Round 06 reconciliation posture into one canonical product truth before more implementation widens support claims.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Accepted provider-truth and launch-truth decisions are folded back into the canonical plan and support contracts.
2. The from-source rr help/build path and live provider surface can be exercised truthfully.
3. Help/provider snapshot guards make future support-claim drift test-visible.
notes: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 spine rooted at rr-x51h; unique child work was reparented under rr-x51h.2.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 rr-x51h group epics after unique children were reparented.

title: Round 06 G9.1: Merge accepted provider-truth and launch-truth decisions into the canonical plan
description:
Merge the accepted Round 06 provider, launch-lifecycle, bootstrap or doctor, and Copilot truth back into the canonical authority docs so implementation truth stops living in side plans and superseded bead families. This bead owns the canonical-plan and support-contract merge after the underlying implementation lanes are settled: provider order and claim tiers, verified launch-attempt rules, transactional session transitions, resolved-config and bootstrap or doctor boundaries, and the accepted Copilot admission posture.

If this merge-back step is weak, future agents will keep reading stale Round 06 packets as if they were still live truth.
acceptance_criteria:
1. PLAN_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md and the owning narrow contracts absorb the accepted Round 06 directions for provider hierarchy and tier wording, verified launch or session lifecycle, resolved config and bootstrap or doctor truth, and Copilot policy or continuity posture.
2. docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md, docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md, and any other Round 06 side-plan packets are explicitly downgraded, archived, or marked historical once their accepted truth is merged.
3. No active 0.1.x behavior remains defined only in side plans or in superseded rr-b58q shells; authority order is unambiguous for future implementation agents.
4. Validation: authority audit across plan and support docs plus doc-link review against the live bead graph and shipped help, bootstrap, and provider surfaces.
notes: Inputs: AGENTS.md authority rules, the canonical plan, provider support matrix, configuration capability contract, bootstrap or doctor contract, lifecycle contracts, and the final Copilot operator-truth bead. This is not a new side-plan; it is the merge-back step that removes side-plan authority drift.

title: Repair from-source rr help/build truth and freeze the live provider matrix
description: Restore a truthful from-source rr help entry path and capture the actual provider surface emitted by the live CLI before more docs or bead claims widen support.
acceptance_criteria:
1. cargo run/build help paths succeed from source and expose the real provider surface without hidden manifest/build breakage.
2. The live rr review/provider matrix is probed and recorded as the canonical implementation baseline for this bead wave.
3. Conflicts between router logic, usage text, and docs become explicit follow-on fixes rather than silent drift.
notes: Superseded by rr-x51h.2.1 and rr-x51h.2.2 after adopting rr-x51h as the canonical Round 06 spine.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G1 canonicalization/build/help leaf set under rr-x51h.2.*.

title: Add provider/help snapshot guards against support-claim drift
description: Make provider lists, tiers, help text, and support wording test-visible so docs and command surfaces cannot drift quietly after this shaping pass.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Snapshot fixtures capture provider exposure, tier wording, and help output for human and robot surfaces.
2. A regression lane fails when help/provider truth diverges from the canonical matrix.
3. The snapshot family is named in validation docs rather than left as ad hoc proof.
notes: Superseded by rr-x51h.2.3 after adopting rr-x51h as the canonical Round 06 spine.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 G1 canonicalization/build/help leaf set under rr-x51h.2.*.

title: Fix published rr installer checksum manifest mismatch
description:
Known bug: published releases currently ship `SHA256SUMS`, but install metadata and verifier expectations still point `rr-install.sh` at `<artifact-stem>-checksums.txt` first. On macOS/Linux the public one-line installer therefore emits a visible `curl 404` before succeeding via fallback to `SHA256SUMS`.

Acceptance:
- published install metadata, verifier expectations, and release assets agree on one checksum manifest name for the CLI installer path
- `curl -fsSL https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/releases/latest/download/rr-install.sh | bash -s -- --dry-run` completes without a visible 404/fallback probe
- docs/release notes wording stays truthful about the macOS/Linux installer path and checksum artifact naming

Validation:
- `bash scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh`
- `bash scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh`
- live dry-run against a published release asset URL with no leading 404
close_reason: Published installer checksum-manifest mismatch fixed on released assets; latest rr-install dry-run is clean (no visible 404), release seam tests pass, and CI-sensitive remote evidence recorded from https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/24081424286 (success). Required closeout-cycle gates pass: rch exec -- cargo build --workspace and rch exec -- cargo test --workspace.
