title: Infer safe next actions in extension and remove avoidable user intervention
description: Reduce avoidable extension clicks by inferring the next safe action when Roger already has enough local state. Scope includes demoting Refresh from an always-visible primary action into a contextual action, inferring a single primary CTA from session and attention state, tightening setup follow-through where registration can be observed automatically, and reducing unnecessary picker or rerun prompts when Roger can resolve the target truthfully. Validation contract: child slices must cover action-visibility rules, inferred-primary-action behavior, setup or re-entry inference where safe, and truthful smoke evidence that mutation-sensitive flows remain explicit.
close_reason: Closed feature after all child slices (rr-a9nu.1/.2/.3/.4) completed with targeted extension UX/setup inference evidence. Integration closeout validation in this cycle: rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (PASS); rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (PASS); scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-a9nu --local-only-reason "Feature-level integration close of completed child extension UX slices; validated with full workspace build/test in this closeout cycle and child-bead targeted smoke evidence." (PASS).

title: Make Refresh contextual and infer a primary Roger CTA from session state
description: Change the extension action model so Start, Resume, and Findings are the default primary actions while Refresh is shown only when Roger state makes it relevant, especially refresh_recommended or equivalent stale-session signals. The PR surface should visually emphasize one inferred primary CTA instead of treating every action as equally likely. Validation contract: focused tests and smoke evidence for default action visibility, refresh recommendation visibility, and inferred-primary-action emphasis.
close_reason: Implemented contextual PR-surface action model: Refresh is hidden by default and shown/promoted only when attention state indicates `refresh_recommended`; a single primary CTA is inferred per state (`start_review` default, `resume_review` for awaiting input/failure, `show_findings` for findings/approval). Preserved GitHub/Primer button semantics. Validation passed: `node --test apps/extension/src/content/main.test.js`; `bash scripts/swarm/smoke_extension_entry_ux.sh`; `rch exec -- cargo build --workspace`; `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace`; `scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-a9nu.1 --local-only-reason "Non-CI extension UX action-model slice; validated with content DOM tests, extension UX smoke, and full workspace build/test in current closeout cycle."`.

title: Reduce unnecessary resume or refresh disambiguation when Roger can infer the target session
description: Narrow the conditions that force picker-required re-entry for resume and refresh. When Roger has a single strongest target by repo, PR, continuity quality, and freshest binding, it should proceed without an extra disambiguation step and reserve the picker for real ambiguity only. Validation contract: unit or integration evidence for auto-selection rules plus blocked evidence for still-ambiguous cases.
close_reason: Implemented strongest-candidate auto-selection for resume/refresh picker-required re-entry when repo-local ambiguity can be truthfully resolved by ranking (PR match, launch-binding quality, continuity quality, freshness), while preserving picker block on ties/low-quality ambiguity. Added CLI unit/integration coverage for ranking and tie behavior. Evidence: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --lib (pass; includes new continuity/ranking inference tests), rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test session_resolution_smoke (pass), rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke resume_blocks_with_picker_when_repo_match_is_ambiguous -- --exact (pass), rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (pass), rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (pass), scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-a9nu.2 --local-only-reason ... (pass).

title: Validate inferred-action ergonomics remain explicit on mutation-sensitive flows
description: Exercise the inferred-action ergonomics lane: contextual refresh visibility, inferred primary CTA, setup follow-through, and reduced re-entry friction, while proving approval, posting, and other mutation-sensitive paths remain explicitly user-triggered. Validation contract: named smoke or acceptance evidence for each inferred behavior plus explicit notes on what still remains user action by policy.
close_reason: Validated inferred-action ergonomics while preserving explicit mutation-sensitive flows. Evidence: node --test apps/extension/src/content/main.test.js (pass, added canonical attention-state primary-action mapping + action inventory assertions proving launch-only action set without approve/post actions); bash scripts/swarm/smoke_extension_entry_ux.sh (pass); rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (initial fail due rusqlite/libsqlite3-sys links conflict, remediated by aligning packages/storage/Cargo.toml to rusqlite.workspace, rerun pass); rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (initial same links conflict, rerun pass after alignment); scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-a9nu.3 --local-only-reason ... (pass). Explicit policy retained: approval/posting remains user-triggered through Roger approval surfaces, not inferred or auto-dispatched by extension CTA model.

title: Auto-complete extension setup and doctor follow-through when identity registration is observed
description: Reduce avoidable rerun steps in guided extension setup by automatically continuing once browser-side extension identity registration is observed, rather than forcing the user through an extra setup or doctor command when Roger can finish the check truthfully. Validation contract: setup-flow evidence showing registration detection, automatic follow-through, and fail-closed behavior when registration never arrives.
close_reason: Implemented setup follow-through by adding a bounded registration-observation wait in rr extension setup: setup now auto-continues when extension identity is observed during wait and still fails closed when identity never arrives. Added setup envelope fields for wait budget and whether registration was observed during setup wait, plus smoke coverage for the observed-during-wait path. Validation in this closeout cycle: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke -- extension_setup_; bash scripts/swarm/smoke_extension_entry_ux.sh; rch exec -- cargo build --workspace; rch exec -- cargo test --workspace; scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-a9nu.4 --local-only-reason ...

title: Close the remote CI feedback loop for agent-driven implementation
description: Roger currently has remote CI and release workflows, but local agent execution can still drift because failing GitHub runs are not automatically surfaced back into the bead frontier or treated as hard closeout evidence. This epic exists to close that loop: agents should not be able to keep pushing code while remote truth is red and nobody converts the failure into work. Acceptance: (1) failing remote CI/release runs become visible actionable local work or attention state quickly; (2) closeout for CI-sensitive beads requires explicit remote or equivalent event-driven validation evidence rather than fixture-only confidence; (3) swarm/operator docs explain how failure ownership is assigned and how the frontier is widened when remote gates break. Validation contract: close only after child beads land and one deliberate failing-run rehearsal proves the failure becomes local actionable work rather than silent background noise.
close_reason: All child beads landed (rr-aip.1/.2/.3). Remote CI failures are now ingested into local actionable work with run/workflow/ref identity + dedupe, CI-sensitive closeout requires remote run evidence, and routing/ownership policy is documented with Agent Mail rehearsal (run_id 23877791018).

title: Materialize failing GitHub Actions runs into local Roger work
description: Implement a Roger-owned failure intake path that turns failing GitHub Actions runs into local actionable work instead of passive red badges. The path may create or update a bead, emit an attention state, or both, but it must preserve run identity, workflow path, ref, and failure summary so agents can pick it up locally. Acceptance: (1) a failing remote run can be ingested into a stable local record with run URL/id and workflow identity; (2) duplicate failures update the same local issue/thread rather than creating unbounded noise; (3) at least release and validation workflows are covered first; (4) one rehearsal with a known failing fixture run proves the failure becomes visible local work. Validation contract: integration/manual-smoke via fixture payloads plus one real GitHub run ingestion rehearsal recorded in the close reason.
notes: Implemented scripts/swarm/ingest_failed_actions_runs.py + scripts/swarm/test_ingest_failed_actions_runs.sh. Acceptance evidence: (1) fixture ingest test passes via bash scripts/swarm/test_ingest_failed_actions_runs.sh and proves stable local records carry run_url/run_id/workflow identity, release+validation workflow coverage, and duplicate update behavior; (2) live GH rehearsal in temp workspace via python3 scripts/swarm/ingest_failed_actions_runs.py --repo cdilga/roger-reviewer --project-root <tmp> --br-binary /Users/cdilga/.local/bin/br-0.1.34.pinned --parent-id none --per-page 100 created 5 intake issues on first pass (source=gh_api, candidates_total=34, candidates_supported=18, candidates_skipped=16) and updated same 5 issues on second pass (created=[], updated=5) including known failing run_id 23877791018 release-publish URL https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877791018.
close_reason: Added remote CI failure ingestion path and fixture test; validated with bash scripts/swarm/test_ingest_failed_actions_runs.sh plus live gh_api rehearsal against cdilga/roger-reviewer (--per-page 100) showing first-pass create=5 and second-pass dedupe update=5 (including run_id 23877791018).

title: Require remote validation evidence before closing CI-sensitive beads
description: Add a Roger-owned closeout rule for beads whose promises depend on remote CI or release truth: they must name and record remote run evidence, not only local fixture or shell evidence. This task should update the workflow docs and any helper scripts or templates needed so agents do not close release or CI-facing beads while the real remote gate is unproven or red. Acceptance: (1) CI-sensitive bead categories are explicitly named; (2) closeout expectations require run id or URL plus outcome for those categories; (3) at least one operator-facing helper or checklist makes missing remote evidence obvious before close; (4) the rule explains when local-only evidence is still sufficient. Validation contract: docs/process layer plus one rehearsal where an attempted closeout without remote evidence is caught by the new helper/checklist.
close_reason:
Implemented CI-sensitive closeout rule + operator helper/checklist.
- Added scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh
- Added scripts/swarm/test_check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh

Validation/rehearsal:

- bash scripts/swarm/test_check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh

title: Assign ownership and alert routing for remote CI failures in swarm runs
description: Document and implement the minimum swarm/operator policy so failing remote validation does not become everybodys problem and therefore nobodys problem. This task should define who gets paged or assigned, how a failing run is claimed, and when new repair beads are spawned versus attached to an existing thread. Acceptance: (1) the repo docs name an ownership model for remote CI failures; (2) Agent Mail or equivalent routing guidance is explicit; (3) the policy explains how to avoid duplicate repair beads while still making failures visible; (4) one bounded rehearsal shows a failing run is claimed by one worker and announced to the rest. Validation contract: docs/manual-smoke layer via a bounded rehearsal and recorded message/thread evidence.
notes:

close_reason:
Fixed CI intake failure for validation-nightly by hardening validation-tier command execution against wrapped shell quotes.

Root cause from run https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/24074050845 (.github/workflows/validation-nightly.yml, main/schedule):
- validation-tier set ROGER_VALIDATION_COMMAND with an outer quote wrapper in the runner context, causing `bash -lc` to treat the entire command as a literal executable name and fail with exit 127.

Landed repair:
- .github/actions/validation-tier/action.yml now normalizes one outer single- or double-quote pair before execution and fails closed if normalization yields an empty command.
- Added local regression script .github/actions/validation-tier/test_command_normalization.sh to prove plain/single-quoted/double-quoted command behavior and empty-command fail-closed handling.

Validation in this closeout cycle:
- bash .github/actions/validation-tier/test_command_normalization.sh (pass)

title: Make PR-page Roger entry feel GitHub-native with rail-pane and modal fallback
description: Replace the current detached/floating PR-page Roger entry experience with a first-class GitHub-native launch flow. Primary UX should attach Roger controls into stable GitHub PR action seams using GitHub-like buttons, or render a bounded Roger pane directly in the PR right rail above reviewers when that is the more coherent placement. If page-DOM attachment becomes unreliable due to GitHub layout drift, fall back to a page-local modal rather than a detached floating card. Browser-action popup remains an explicit manual fallback, not the primary happy path. Validation contract: child slices must cover placement precedence, GitHub-native styling/structure, modal fallback behavior, and truthful smoke evidence on real PR pages.

title: Round 06 Provider Truth and Graph Widening Program
description:
Round 06 exists because the provider-parity and GitHub Copilot CLI critique is not fully represented by the live bead frontier. This parent replaces the current one-bead frontier with a truthful program covering the missing implementation lanes: review-worker boundary, build/help truth, transactional lifecycle, bootstrap and doctor, explicit outbound flow, provider claim re-audit, Copilot admission, provider proof hardening, explicit search-planner truth, and final doc/support-claim reconciliation.

The goal is not to create admin busywork. The goal is to create independently provable slices that let the next implementation wave move in parallel without smearing lifecycle truth, provider truth, or support-claim ownership across unrelated beads.

Acceptance and validation:
- All child epics required by `docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md` exist as real beads with leaf-level acceptance and validation contracts.
- The explicit omissions called out in Round 06, especially search-planner truth and support-claim reconciliation, are represented in the graph rather than left implicit.
- Validation: final graph audit via `br` showing the Round 06 surface is no longer represented by a single corrupted open bead.

Notes:
Primary inputs: `docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md`, `docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md`, `docs/PLAN_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md`, and `docs/CONFIGURATION_CAPABILITY_AND_DEFAULTING_CONTRACT.md`.

Preferred execution order for the swarm is G0, G1, G2, G3, G4, GX1, G5, G6, G7, GX2. That ordering is guidance, not a substitute for explicit leaf dependencies.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical rr-x51h Round 06 execution tree after unique child coverage was reparented.

title: Round 06 G0 Review-worker runtime and tool boundary
description:
Round 06 explicitly says Roger should stop treating review-worker semantics as prompt glue and instead make the worker boundary a real owned contract. This epic isolates the worker object model, bounded reads, result submission, clarification/follow-up semantics, and the dedicated `rr agent` transport so later provider work cannot invent its own transport or memory semantics.

Acceptance and validation:
- Roger owns persistent worker task, invocation, read, and result semantics instead of leaving them implicit in harness prompts.
- The machine surface is distinct from operator surfaces and remains fail-closed on stale or malformed worker interactions.
- Validation: child beads provide `unit` and `integration` proof for the worker boundary before provider-specific work widens claims.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 wave rooted at rr-x51h and its leaf beads; this late duplicate shell does not carry unique execution ownership.

title: G0.1 Persist review-worker task, invocation, and result ledger
description:
Round 06 calls out the worker boundary as missing from the live graph even though the contracts already describe it. This bead makes Roger own the worker task, invocation, and terminal-result ledger in storage and app-core so provider work cannot improvise transport semantics.

Acceptance and validation:
- Persist `ReviewTask`, `WorkerInvocation`, and terminal result records with task/run/session/nonce binding and audit timestamps.
- Reject stale, cross-session, or duplicate-terminal writes fail closed.
- Support multi-turn history per task without allowing multiple terminal results.
- Validation: `unit` for schema and serde; `integration` for round-trip, stale nonce, duplicate terminal, and rollback cases.

Notes:
Primary inputs: `docs/REVIEW_WORKER_RUNTIME_AND_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT.md`, `docs/DATA_MODEL_AND_STORAGE_CONTRACT.md`, `docs/CORE_DOMAIN_SCHEMA_AND_FINDING_FINGERPRINT.md`. Antecedents: `rr-015`, `rr-018`.
close_reason: Superseded by canonical rr-x51h Round 06 leaves after preserving the unique extension-uninstall and docs-closeout coverage under rr-x51h.

title: G0.2 Implement Roger-owned worker read gateway
description:
The worker contract already defines bounded reads for context, findings, memory, artifacts, and status, but Roger does not yet expose that as a first-class gateway. This bead makes those reads real in app-core so the worker cannot widen scope by prompt convention.

Acceptance and validation:
- Worker read operations for context, findings, memory, artifacts, and status are implemented through one bounded gateway.
- Denied scope widening returns explicit machine-readable errors.
- Returned payloads include provenance and boundedness metadata rather than free-form text.
- Validation: `integration` for allow/deny cases and bounded payload snapshots.

Notes:
Primary inputs: `docs/REVIEW_WORKER_RUNTIME_AND_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT.md`, `docs/SEARCH_MEMORY_LIFECYCLE_AND_SEMANTIC_ASSET_POLICY.md`. Antecedents: `rr-018`, `rr-018.2`.
close_reason: Superseded by canonical rr-x51h Round 06 leaves after preserving the unique extension-uninstall and docs-closeout coverage under rr-x51h.

title: G0.3 Implement structured worker stage-result submission and finding-pack validation
description:
Roger must accept worker stage results only after validating session/run/task/nonce binding and the structured result envelope. This bead turns terminal results into Roger-validated proposals instead of allowing workers to mutate canonical state directly.

Acceptance and validation:
- `submit_stage_result` validates task binding, nonce binding, and result-envelope shape before any state change.
- Findings proposals, refusals, and repair-needed responses are all represented as structured outcomes.
- Malformed or mismatched results are rejected with explicit machine-readable reasons.
- Validation: `integration` for happy path plus wrong task, stale nonce, malformed pack, and repair-needed cases.

Notes:
Primary inputs: `docs/REVIEW_WORKER_RUNTIME_AND_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT.md`, `docs/PROMPT_PRESET_AND_OUTCOME_CONTRACT.md`. Antecedents: `rr-018`, `rr-015`.
close_reason: Superseded by canonical rr-x51h Round 06 leaves after preserving the unique extension-uninstall and docs-closeout coverage under rr-x51h.

title: G0.4 Implement clarification and follow-up request lifecycle
description:
Round 06 warns against collapsing clarification and follow-up semantics into generic result blobs. This bead gives Roger explicit lifecycle handling for clarification requests and follow-up proposals so operator surfaces can distinguish them from findings.

Acceptance and validation:
- Clarification requests and follow-up proposals are persisted and surfaced as first-class worker outcomes.
- Attention/status surfaces can distinguish clarification-needed, follow-up-proposed, and findings-ready states.
- Invalid or stale clarification/follow-up outcomes fail closed.
- Validation: `integration` for creation, stale rejection, and surfaced-state snapshots.

Notes:
Primary inputs: `docs/REVIEW_WORKER_RUNTIME_AND_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT.md`, `docs/ATTENTION_EVENT_AND_NOTIFICATION_CONTRACT.md`. Antecedents: `rr-018`, `rr-006.3`.
close_reason: Superseded by canonical rr-x51h Round 06 leaves after preserving the unique extension-uninstall and docs-closeout coverage under rr-x51h.

title: G0.5 Add dedicated `rr agent` transport and worker-boundary regression coverage
description:
The plan prefers a dedicated `rr agent` transport rather than stretching `--robot` into a second role. This bead lands the machine-only transport and locks the boundary with regression coverage and doc truth.

Acceptance and validation:
- `rr agent` accepts only bound session/run/task/nonce requests and returns machine-readable envelopes.
- Denied scope widening, stale binding, malformed results, and clarification/follow-up flows are covered by regression tests.
- Help and docs distinguish `rr agent` from operator commands and `--robot`.
- Validation: `integration` for end-to-end worker task/result flows; `unit` for envelope parsing; help/robot snapshot checks.

Notes:
Must update: `docs/ROBOT_CLI_CONTRACT.md`, `docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md`, `docs/TESTING.md`, `docs/VALIDATION_INVARIANT_MATRIX.md`, and the worker-boundary contract docs.
close_reason: Superseded by canonical rr-x51h Round 06 leaves after preserving the unique extension-uninstall and docs-closeout coverage under rr-x51h.

title: Round 06 GX2 Documentation, support-claim, and validation closeout
description:
Roger follows an agentic-first documentation posture: accepted truth belongs in the canonical plan and narrow support contracts, not in a pile of parallel side plans. This epic closes the loop by merging accepted Round 06 decisions back into authoritative docs, public/operator surfaces, and validation ownership artifacts.

Acceptance and validation:
- Canonical/support docs absorb accepted Round 06 truth and side-plan drift is removed or demoted.
- README, AGENTS, onboarding, review-flow, release/test, and validation-ownership surfaces all match the live implementation graph.
- Validation: doc/help/graph audits prove there is no ambiguous support claim left behind when the program closes.
close_reason: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 wave rooted at rr-x51h and its leaf beads; this late duplicate shell does not carry unique execution ownership.

title: Round 06 G9.1: Merge accepted Round 06 truth back into canonical and support docs
description:
Roger’s authority order says accepted truth belongs in the canonical plan and narrow support contracts, not in live side plans. This bead merges accepted Round 06 outcomes back into the canonical/support docs and demotes or archives side-plan drift.

Acceptance and validation:
- Accepted Round 06 decisions are merged into `docs/PLAN_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md` and the relevant narrow support contracts.
- `docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md` and `docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md` are explicitly reframed, archived, or demoted once their accepted truth is merged.
- Authority order is unambiguous after the update.
- Validation: doc-link and authority audit rather than code-only completion.

Notes:
Primary inputs: `AGENTS.md` authority rules and `docs/DOCS_TREE_INVENTORY_AND_CLEANUP_PLAN.md`.
close_reason: Superseded by rr-92l0.1 after the Round 06 docs-closeout lane was consolidated under rr-x51h.11.

title: Round 06 G9.2: Reconcile README, AGENTS, onboarding, review-flow, and validation claims with the new graph
description:
Perform the final public and operator truth pass after the Round 06 implementation and proof lanes land. This bead updates README, AGENTS, onboarding, review-flow, release or test, testing, validation ownership, and related operator docs so every public claim about providers, launch continuity, bootstrap or doctor, outbound approval, search planner, and proof ownership matches the actual bead graph and executable surface. It is the packaging pass for shipped truth, not a place to invent missing behavior or hide residual gaps.

This bead should leave the repo in a state where a new agent can read the public and operator docs and come away with the same support story that the code, help, robot envelopes, and validation lanes actually defend.
acceptance_criteria:
1. README.md, AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, onboarding, review-flow, release or test matrix, testing doctrine, validation invariant matrix, validation ownership docs, prompt packs, alien packets, extension smoke docs, and relevant swarm/operator runbooks all reflect the actual provider tiers, lifecycle limits, bootstrap or doctor path, automatic reconciliation posture, outbound approval flow, search-planner truth, and recovery posture.
2. Every widened claim points to a named suite, invariant, or bounded smoke lane; if proof is not yet real the wording stays planned, bounded, or degraded instead of vague.
3. Public and operator docs obey the README and AGENTS durability rules: no time-fragile state blurbs, no dev-only tool leakage, no hidden reliance on side plans, no support wording that outruns shipped commands, and no machine-specific absolute repo links in authority-bearing prompt/runbook docs.
4. Historical packets and superseded readiness/reconciliation briefs that remain in-tree are visibly status-fenced so future agents do not treat them as current product truth.
5. Validation: doc, help, and robot snapshot audit plus bead-graph authority audit using the existing drift guard and proof owners rather than ad hoc reading.
notes: Must include docs/REVIEW_FLOW_MATRIX.md, docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, docs/TESTING.md, docs/VALIDATION_INVARIANT_MATRIX.md, docs/VALIDATION_MATRIX_AND_FIXTURE_OWNERSHIP.md, docs/DEV_MACHINE_ONBOARDING.md, docs/PLANNING_WORKFLOW_PROMPTS.md, docs/REPO_ONBOARDING_AND_DISCOVERY_PROMPTS.md, docs/ALIEN_ARTEFACTS_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md, docs/ALIEN_WORKFLOWS_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md, docs/extension-entry-ux-smoke.md, docs/extension-visual-identity-smoke.md, and the relevant docs/swarm/*.md operator prompt packs where applicable. Treat docs/PERSONA_JOURNEYS_AND_CHAOS_RECOVERY.md as a first-class input for recovery wording, flow truth, and E2E-story alignment. Search-planner and recall wording are also in scope anywhere operator surfaces, review-flow docs, or validation doctrine would otherwise drift behind rr search or robot envelopes. This bead is the final public or operator truth pass after outbound approval docs, invariant wiring, smoke-proof where required, and planner-surface truth land; it must not invent missing product truth or close ahead of rr-ph77.6, rr-x51h.8.5, rr-x51h.8.6, rr-x51h.9.2, and the provider or help drift guard.

title: Round 06 G2 Transactional lifecycle and bridge truth
description:
Roger cannot widen provider claims while review, resume, refresh, return, and bridge launch can still overstate success before durable state is committed. This epic hardens the lifecycle with explicit attempt state, transactional binding, and real bridge dispatch instead of synthetic success.

Acceptance and validation:
- Review/resume/refresh/return transitions become auditable and fail closed on partial failure or stale state.
- Bridge launch paths surface real CLI results instead of pretend success.
- Validation: failure-injection and recovery coverage defend lifecycle truth before later provider admission work starts.

title: G2.1 Add launch-attempt ledger and transactional review/resume binding
description:
Roger cannot claim a successful review or resume until the durable binding is actually committed. This bead adds an explicit launch-attempt ledger or equivalent state machine and makes core review/resume binding transactional and auditable.

Acceptance and validation:
- Review and resume paths record pending versus committed launch state explicitly.
- Partial failure cannot leave a completed-looking session or locator binding behind.
- Retry-safe transaction boundaries exist for initial binding work.
- Validation: `integration` with failure injection, rollback, and partial-write coverage.

Notes:
Primary inputs: `docs/HARNESS_SESSION_LINKAGE_CONTRACT.md`, canonical plan lifecycle sections. Antecedents: `rr-018.1`, `rr-003.3`, `rr-0qvg.3`.
close_reason: Superseded by canonical rr-x51h Round 06 leaves after preserving the unique extension-uninstall and docs-closeout coverage under rr-x51h.

title: G2.2 Make refresh, return, and resume-bundle transitions transactional and fail closed
description:

Acceptance and validation:
- Refresh, return, and resume-bundle transitions use explicit state transitions and fail closed on partial failure.
- Stale or mismatched bundle/locator state is rejected with surfaced recovery guidance.
- Continuation paths do not overstate success before durable state is committed.
- Validation: `integration` for stale locator, missing bundle, retry, and rollback cases.

title: G2.4 Add lifecycle and bridge recovery surfacing plus regression coverage
description:

acceptance_criteria:

acceptance_criteria:

title: Round 06 G0.5: Persist worker invocation and result audit trail
description: Every worker call needs its own audit lineage so review-task execution is reconstructable even when provider sessions, retries, or clarification loops get messy.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Worker request/response digests, binding metadata, and partial/failed submissions are append-only and durable.

3. Audit records survive restart and remain searchable for debugging and proof.

Validation contract: storage/integration tests for worker audit persistence and restart recovery. Primary refs: docs/REVIEW_WORKER_RUNTIME_AND_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT.md and docs/DATA_MODEL_AND_STORAGE_CONTRACT.md.

Blocked 2026-04-16 during initial implementation pass: active exclusive file reservations from BoldFinch on packages/storage/src/lib.rs, packages/storage/tests/*, and packages/cli/src/lib.rs overlap the required migration/API/wiring surface. Read-only investigation confirmed app-core already defines canonical WorkerInvocation and WorkerToolCallEvent shapes, but storage has no corresponding migration or persistence API yet, so this remains a real schema slice to resume once the reservation conflict clears or the work is explicitly handed off.

title: Investigate NTM assign dry-run mutation and cross-project assignment leakage
description:
While validating `rr-v51u` on 2026-04-15, two separate NTM assignment-safety problems appeared:

1. `ntm assign roger-reviewer--v51u-repro --dry-run --agent=codex --limit=1 --json` still created a real assignment for `rr-x51h.3.2` and required `--clear-pane=0` cleanup.
2. A nominally separate session spawned from `rr-v51u-empty --label watch` reported `working_directory: /Users/cdilga` and `ntm assign rr-v51u-empty--watch --watch --stop-when-done --watch-interval=2s --delay=0s --json` pulled live Roger beads (`rr-x51h.3.2`, `rr-8isd.2.1`, `rr-x51h.1.3`) instead of truthfully reporting exhaustion.

These are not `rr-v51u` detection failures, but they make future operator validation unsafe and can perturb the live queue if left undocumented or unfixed.

Acceptance criteria:
1. There is a validation-safe way to observe `ntm assign` / `ntm assign --watch` idle-agent behavior without mutating live Roger assignments, or the commands are repaired so `--dry-run` is truly non-mutating.
2. Sessions spawned outside the Roger workspace do not unexpectedly source Roger beads or inherit Roger working-directory scope.
3. Exact cleanup and validation commands are recorded so future swarm validation does not repeat the accidental assignment side effects.
close_reason: Closeout 2026-04-15: added repo-local fail-closed assign-watch entrypoint at scripts/swarm/run_assign_watch.sh plus shell smoke scripts/swarm/test_run_assign_watch.sh. The wrapper now (1) resolves the session via `ntm status --json` and aborts unless `working_directory` matches this repo root, (2) provides safe `--check` mode that prints scoped `ntm status` + `ntm activity` without invoking `ntm assign`, and (3) rejects forwarded `--dry-run` because the current external NTM build mutated assignments during rr-v51u validation. Validation run: bash -n scripts/swarm/run_assign_watch.sh && bash -n scripts/swarm/test_run_assign_watch.sh; bash scripts/swarm/test_run_assign_watch.sh; ./scripts/swarm/run_assign_watch.sh --session roger-reviewer --check. Upstream NTM still needs separate follow-up for true dry-run semantics / cross-project scoping, but this repo-local path now gives a truthful safe operator entrypoint and prevents the observed validation footguns from recurring through Roger runbooks.

description:

On real GitHub pages, especially dark mode, the surface is visually inconsistent and can become unreadable, as shown by the current washed-out disabled buttons and status text.

Acceptance Criteria:
- the injected Roger panel uses GitHub/Primer-aligned visual tokens or computed GitHub CSS variables instead of a bespoke hardcoded white/gray inline palette
- buttons, headings, status text, and attention badges remain readable on both GitHub light and dark themes
- the panel feels like a native GitHub affordance rather than a separate toy overlay, including spacing, border, radius, and button treatment
- styling remains bounded to the thin extension surface; Roger local/TUI authority rules stay unchanged
close_reason: Integrated rr-d0ny.1 + rr-d0ny.2: panel styling now uses GitHub/Primer tokens with improved dark fallback contrast and a canonical light/dark readability smoke contract. Validation context in this cycle includes rch exec -- cargo build --workspace and rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (both pass).

title: Repair GitHub release asset, checksum, and updater contract parity
description: Make the published release assets, generated install metadata, installer scripts, and rr update artifact expectations describe the same file names and checksum contract. This bead owns the current live mismatch where a fresh install can succeed but the installed binary's own update preflight fails against the latest GitHub release because the expected checksum asset naming does not match what was published.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Release automation outputs, install metadata, `rr-install.sh`, `rr-install.ps1`, and `rr update` agree on one canonical checksum/asset naming contract instead of preserving contradictory expectations.
2. Repo-local release-contract probes pass for the repaired contract: `bash scripts/release/test_build_install_metadata_bundle.sh`, `bash scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh`, and `bash scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh`, plus any targeted parity probe needed if the PowerShell path changes.
3. A fresh install from a published-like or live latest release can run `rr update --dry-run --robot` without failing on `checksums_missing` or an equivalent release-asset contract mismatch.
4. Closeout records which cohorts are now honestly defended (Unix installer, PowerShell installer, installed-binary updater, same-version no-op) and narrows docs if any cohort remains under-proven.
notes:
Primary sources: `UPDATE_RELEASE_AND_TESTED_UPGRADE_CONTRACT.md` blocker ledger, `release-publish-operator-smoke.md`, `RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md`, and the release workflow/scripts under `scripts/release/`.

Execution answer: published-artifact truth is the goal, not one workflow file landing in isolation. Agents should feel free to touch release automation, asset naming, checksum generation, installer metadata, installer behavior, and updater assumptions together if that is what it takes to make a fresh published-like install actually function.

Validation contract: start with the repo-local release probes named above, then use the cheapest truthful published-like probe that exercises installer + updater continuity end-to-end. If Windows installer behavior changes, provide targeted `rr-install.ps1` proof or narrow parity claims explicitly; do not inherit Unix proof. If live stable-release smoke is used, closeout should record the tag and UTC timestamp in the same shape required by `release-publish-operator-smoke.md`.

title: Search-planner truth and degraded retrieval boundary
description: Make planner intent, RecallEnvelope truth, degraded retrieval, baseline search posture, and cross-surface memory handoff explicit before search semantics drift behind vague query language again.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Requested versus resolved planner intent is explicit before retrieval executes.
2. CLI, TUI, worker, and robot surfaces project one canonical recall model.
3. lexical_only and recovery_scan remain explicit degraded modes, not silent healthy-path fallbacks.
notes: Superseded by rr-x51h.9 after the search-planner lane was completed under the canonical Round 06 spine.

title: Transactional launch lifecycle, bridge truth, and bootstrap preflight
description: Replace synthetic launch success with durable launch attempts, atomic session finalization, real CLI-backed bridge dispatch, and truthful bootstrap/preflight surfaces.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Roger never reports a launched review session before verified provider binding exists.
2. Bridge success means a real rr --robot command succeeded and returned canonical Roger ids.
3. rr init, rr doctor, status, and robot surfaces expose the actual launch and preflight posture.
notes: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 spine rooted at rr-x51h; unique child work was reparented under rr-x51h.3 and rr-x51h.4.

title: Implement the LaunchAttempt aggregate and normalized verification envelope
description: Persist a durable launch-attempt object before provider launch so Roger can prove an attempt existed, correlate verification with a nonce, and distinguish failure classes without minting fake review sessions.
acceptance_criteria:
1. LaunchAttempt state covers pending, dispatching, awaiting verification, terminal success, and terminal failure.
2. Provider verification captures a normalized envelope with provider, provider_session_id, attempt_nonce, source, and raw evidence reference.
3. No durable ReviewSession appears before verified provider binding exists.
notes: Superseded by rr-x51h.3.1 after adopting rr-x51h as the canonical Round 06 spine.

title: Make review/resume/refresh/return finalization atomic after verification
description: Retrofit every user-visible lifecycle action so Roger finalizes session/run/binding/continuity truth in one store transaction only after provider verification succeeds.
acceptance_criteria:
1. review, resume, refresh, and return each create a pending attempt, verify provider evidence, then commit durable Roger truth atomically.
2. Crash or fault after provider verification but before final commit leaves failure/attempt evidence, not fake success.
3. Lifecycle actions stop leaking partially-created session or run state.
notes: Superseded by rr-x51h.3.2 after adopting rr-x51h as the canonical Round 06 spine.

title: Round 06 G2.5: Reconcile abandoned launch attempts and retry semantics truthfully
description: Teach startup and doctor flows to classify old launch attempts explicitly instead of replaying them magically or allowing stale verification evidence to complete the wrong session.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Pending or in-flight attempts are classified as recoverable, failed, or abandoned with explicit guidance.
2. Retry creates a new attempt id rather than reusing stale execution state.
3. Stale hook/provider evidence cannot finalize a later attempt.
notes: Validation contract: restart/reconciliation integration suites with stale-evidence and partial-failure fixtures.

3. Bridge success is impossible when the underlying CLI returns blocked, degraded, schema-mismatched, or missing-id payloads.

title: Round 06 G2.6: Add bridge failure taxonomy and honest no-status guidance

acceptance_criteria:

title: Round 06 G3.1: Implement canonical rr init bootstrap
description: Ship one truthful bootstrap command for local Roger setup instead of split lore across docs, side-plans, and provider-specific instructions.
acceptance_criteria:
1. rr init creates or repairs the local Roger store and writes required version/metadata markers.
2. rr init verifies permissions and emits bounded provider guidance without claiming unsupported readiness.
3. Docs and onboarding stop pointing at bootstrap steps that are not real commands.
notes: Superseded by rr-8isd.5.1 under rr-x51h.4; kept rr-g7j6.8 only for unique status provenance coverage.

title: Round 06 G3.2: Implement provider-aware rr doctor
description: Provide one fail-closed preflight surface that tells operators whether Roger, the chosen provider, and the current repo/worktree posture are actually ready for the requested path.
acceptance_criteria:
1. rr doctor and rr doctor --provider <name> validate binaries, cwd/worktree roots, hooks/instructions, store/bootstrap state, and launch-policy resolution.
2. Auth checks are stated explicitly as verified, not verified, or deferred to first launch.
3. Doctor guidance routes operators to real repair steps instead of ambient assumptions.
notes: Superseded by rr-8isd.5.2 under rr-x51h.4; kept rr-g7j6.8 only for unique status provenance coverage.

title: Round 06 G3.6: Surface launch/config provenance in status and robot output
description: Operators need to see which provider, launch profile, policy profile, hook version, worktree root, and MCP posture were actually active for a session before they can trust or debug it.
acceptance_criteria:
1. status and robot envelopes expose provider, policy profile, digest/version fields, hook profile, custom-instructions digest, built-in MCP state, and worktree root.
2. Provenance fields describe what was active, not what Roger wishes had been active.
3. The same provenance is available to doctor and recovery flows where relevant.
notes: Validation contract: status/robot snapshot assertions plus provenance integration checks.

title: Reconcile root-level flow and architecture diagrams with the canonical command and action hierarchy
description: Make ROOT_LEVEL_FLOW_AND_ARCHITECTURE_DIAGRAMS.md a truthful top-level map of Roger's real surfaces, action hierarchy, and authority boundaries. This bead owns diagram updates for intake, review, memory, draft, approval, posting, bridge boundaries, and recovery paths so the top-level diagrams explain the same product the code and plan now describe.
acceptance_criteria: ROOT_LEVEL_FLOW_AND_ARCHITECTURE_DIAGRAMS.md matches the canonical product surfaces, mutation-elevation model, and recovery or degraded boundaries Roger currently claims. Diagram language lines up with the command palette of real rr surfaces rather than historical or hypothetical flows. title: Publish rr-install bootstrap scripts as first-class release assets
description: Every published release should carry the Roger-owned bootstrap installer entrypoints themselves ( and ), not just core archives and metadata. This bead owns release workflow and verification changes so a user on the GitHub Releases page can discover and use the installer directly without guessing raw GitHub URLs or checking out the repo first.
acceptance_criteria:
Published releases upload rr-install.sh and rr-install.ps1 as first-class release assets alongside core archives, checksums, and install metadata
The release-verify-assets or release-publish gate verifies the presence and expected naming of the installer bootstrap assets so a release cannot claim one-line install without them
The resulting release page gives a fresh user a direct installer entrypoint without requiring a source checkout or guessed raw GitHub URL
notes:
Created after install-flow audit on 2026-04-07. Existing installer logic is present in scripts/release, but the GitHub Releases page does not currently publish the bootstrap scripts themselves, which breaks the agreed one-line flow from the release surface.

Implementation update (2026-04-07): release-build-core now uploads rr-install.sh and rr-install.ps1 as first-class release assets (artifact: core-install-bootstrap), release-publish downloads that artifact into upstream/assets, and verify_release_assets.py now fails closed if either installer bootstrap asset is missing or misnamed.

Additional publish-gate hardening in this cycle (2026-04-07):
- scripts/release/publish_release.py now rejects verified manifests that omit install bootstrap entries for rr-install.sh and rr-install.ps1.
- scripts/release/test_publish_release.sh now asserts release-plan assets include rr-install.sh + rr-install.ps1 and fails closed when those manifest entries are removed.
- scripts/release/test_release_publish_workflow.sh now explicitly checks that core-install-bootstrap is downloaded into upstream/assets.

Validation run in this cycle:
- python3 -m py_compile scripts/release/publish_release.py
- bash scripts/release/test_publish_release.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_release_publish_workflow.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_release_publish_trigger.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh
- rch exec -- cargo build --workspace
- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace

Remaining closeout blocker: bead is CI-sensitive (label: release), so remote run evidence (--run-url + --outcome) is still required by scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh before marking done.
close_reason: Reopen remediation landed for release-note installer surface regression. Fixed scripts/release/publish_release.py _render_notes to restore explicit installer command section and optional follow-on section, including canonical URLs for releases/latest and pinned tag bootstrap scripts (rr-install.sh/rr-install.ps1). This restores the publish-release contract expected by rr-gyoj closeout audit and keeps release notes aligned with first-class installer asset publication. Validation run in this cycle: python3 -m py_compile scripts/release/publish_release.py (pass); bash scripts/release/test_publish_release.sh (pass); bash scripts/release/test_release_build_core_workflow.sh (pass); bash scripts/release/test_release_publish_workflow.sh (pass); bash scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh (pass); rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (pass); rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (pass). title: Repair release/update truth and the tested upgrade path
description: Turn the new update contract into owned implementation work instead of leaving it as a docs-only truth correction. This epic covers GitHub release asset mechanics, installer/updater parity, rr update semantics across channels and platforms, and the end-to-end upgrade rehearsals needed before Roger can honestly claim a tested upgrade path.
acceptance_criteria: The graph contains explicit leaves for release asset or checksum parity, updater semantic parity across user cohorts, published-to-published upgrade rehearsals with evidence, and any Windows PowerShell or WSL-specific truth work needed before Roger can honestly claim a tested upgrade path. No install or update support claim remains grounded only in planning or docs truth without a matching implementation or validation bead. Update blockers for stable, RC, Windows, WSL, Linux arm64, and blocked-path users are represented explicitly instead of being hand-waved as later cleanup.
notes:
Primary sources: UPDATE_RELEASE_AND_TESTED_UPGRADE_CONTRACT.md, TESTING.md, RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, VALIDATION_INVARIANT_MATRIX.md, and PLAN_FOR_SCHEMA_MIGRATIONS_AND_UPDATE_COMPATIBILITY.md. This epic exists because current latest-release reality still diverges from the intended rr update contract.

Execution posture: this epic closes by published-artifact and rehearsal process, not by updater code alone. Agents should expect to use published-like bundles, cohort-specific repair or blocked-path evidence, and preserved rehearsal artifacts. If these proofs expose a missing product or installer capability, spawn the missing implementation bead rather than papering over the gap with documentation.

title: Make WSL an explicit install and update cohort
description: WSL currently falls between Windows PowerShell wording and generic Linux wording. This bead owns making WSL explicit in the release or update graph: decide whether WSL is a blessed Unix-style install path, a distinct Windows-adjacent cohort, or a narrowed or blocked lane, then wire that truth into install guidance, updater semantics, and proof expectations.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The release or update backlog, contract docs, and matrix name WSL explicitly instead of implying it through generic Windows or Linux wording.
2. The chosen WSL entry path is explicit: Unix installer inside WSL, Windows PowerShell outside WSL, or a truthful blocked or degraded posture with repair guidance.
3. If WSL is claimed for 0.1.x, there is a named validation or rehearsal plan for it before release-candidate closeout; if not, the narrowing is explicit and consistent.
4. Any missing implementation or proof follow-ons are created with dependency edges instead of being left as implied future work.
notes: This is intentionally a graph-truth bead first. It exists so the next release-candidate pass cannot accidentally treat WSL as covered just because Linux and Windows paths both exist.

title: Implement FastEmbed integration for semantic search
description: Integrate FastEmbed or a similar embedding model to generate semantic candidates for  in . This includes: Developing a component (e.g., in  or as a sidecar process) responsible for generating embeddings from findings and memory items. Managing and updating the semantic index. Providing a mechanism to query this index and return s to . Ensuring  and  checks in  reflect the actual state of the semantic search components.
acceptance_criteria: 1.  returns results with accurate  when semantic assets are enabled and ready. 2. Semantic index can be built and updated successfully. 3. Performance of semantic search is within acceptable limits. 4. Degradation to lexical-only search is graceful when semantic components are unavailable.
notes:
Parent closeout evidence (2026-04-07):
- AC1 semantic scoring path: prior_review_lookup consumes provided or derived semantic candidates and sets semantic_score_milli and fused_score; covered by rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test prior_review_lookup_smoke -- --nocapture (pass).
- AC2 semantic index readiness/update path: semantic retrieval remains gated on index_state(semantic:<scope>) == ready and semantic index status updates are exercised in prior_review_lookup_smoke fixtures via upsert_index_state ready transitions (pass).
- AC3 bounded performance: retrieval and derivation stay bounded by limit.clamp(1,100) and derived-candidate truncation to limit; validated via the same targeted smoke suite plus full workspace pass.
- AC4 graceful lexical fallback: missing assets/index and embedder-unavailable/embed-failed derivation paths emit explicit degraded reasons while returning lexical-only results; covered by prior_review_lookup_smoke and semantic_embedder_state_smoke (pass).
- Required closeout gate: rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (pass) and rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (pass) in this cycle.
- CI evidence guard: scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-h993 --local-only-reason Non-CI-sensitive parent integration checkpoint; validated through child-bead targeted storage suites plus full workspace build/test in same closeout cycle. (pass).
close_reason: Integrated feature-gated FastEmbed semantic seam into prior_review_lookup with auto-derivation when semantic prereqs are ready, explicit degraded reasons for missing assets/index and embedder-unavailable/embed-failed paths, and regression coverage for hybrid and fail-closed lexical fallback behavior.

title: Align rr update channel, target, blocked-path, and repair semantics across user cohorts
description: Make rr update truthful for the actual user histories Roger now documents: stable users, RC users, Windows installer users, Linux arm64 users, direct published-binary users, symlinked or wrapper installs, local-build users, and damaged local-store users. This bead owns current-channel semantics, target detection parity with installers, honest blocked-path detection, and repair guidance that uses real release-backed commands rather than checkout-only advice.
acceptance_criteria: rr update defaults and machine-readable output are truthful about staying on or leaving a channel, target autodetection, and which install layouts are supported in place. Windows and Linux arm64 updater posture matches installer truth or is narrowed explicitly. Blocked paths such as local builds, wrappers, and symlinked installs fail closed with release-backed repair guidance instead of vague or repo-local suggestions. Validation contract: integration coverage over cohort classification and blocked-path envelopes, plus installer/updater parity checks.
notes:
Primary sources: UPDATE_RELEASE_AND_TESTED_UPGRADE_CONTRACT.md cohort matrix and invariant registry entries in VALIDATION_INVARIANT_MATRIX.md.

Execution answer: defer to real cohort truth, not to a single updater code path. If different cohorts need different blocked-path messaging, repair envelopes, or explicit narrowing, encode that honestly. Validation should favor cohort-classification integration and published-like updater checks; escalate to heavier rehearsals only where claim language or regression risk justifies it.
close_reason: Added installed-binary updater cohort smoke coverage in packages/cli/tests/update_release_contract_smoke.rs for explicit target override truth, explicit RC channel request truth, and blocked renamed-binary repair guidance with release-hosted reinstall commands. This tightens the cohort proof story without widening the updater contract beyond the docs, which still keep Windows latest-install proof manual and Linux aarch64 updater posture explicit-target/narrowed. Validation run: cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test update_release_contract_smoke (4 passed); bash scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh (pass). Commit: a6c4fbe.

title: Translate PJ-04 through PJ-06 and chaos-recovery paths into owned flows and missing follow-on beads
description: Map the setup/bootstrap, refresh/reconciliation, dropout/return, and other chaos-recovery journeys into owned product surfaces and recovery promises. This bead exists so the repo stops treating recovery behavior as a side effect of lower-level implementation and starts naming which beads own the user-visible repair path.
acceptance_criteria: PJ-04 through PJ-06 and the documented chaos-recovery paths each map to owning product beads, truthful degraded-mode wording, and the right proof lane. Where the mapping exposes missing recovery implementation or support-claim ownership, follow-on beads are created instead of leaving the journey as a docs-only promise. Setup/bootstrap and dropout/return truth line up with doctor/init, lifecycle, and bridge beads rather than floating separately.
notes:
Authority audit complete for PJ-04 through PJ-06 using canonical persona/flow docs, current TESTING ownership, canonical JSONL bead truth, and the existing E2E budget mapping.

PJ-04A crash/reboot recovery -> product owners rr-003.3 and rr-005.2; proof owners rr-011.6 and executable follow-on rr-6iah.1. Truth posture: resume/reseed/picker path must stay explicit rather than silently starting a fresh review.
PJ-04B live harness gone but local review survives -> product owners rr-003.3 and rr-003.4; proof owners rr-011.5, rr-x51h.9.3, and executable follow-on rr-6iah.5. Truth posture: local review survives, dropout/return/reseed stay explicit, and approval/posting state cannot mutate implicitly during fallback.
PJ-04C crash during in-flight update/follow-up/reconciliation -> product owners rr-x51h.3.2 and rr-g7j6.3; proof owner rr-x51h.8.2. This audit exposed a real ownership hole in the remaining bounded-reconciliation slice, so I created rr-x51h.3.2.1 to own atomic stale-state reconciliation events plus refresh-entrypoint retirement.
PJ-04D restart after crash with uncertain trust boundary -> product owners rr-x51h.5.2 and rr-x51h.3.2.1; proof owners rr-x51h.8.4 and executable follow-on rr-6iah.3. Truth posture: reuse is never silent once drift/invalidation may have occurred.

PJ-05A local draft -> approve -> post happy path -> product owners rr-ph77.1, rr-8isd.6.2, rr-x51h.5.1, and rr-1pz7; heavyweight proof owner E2E-01 only.
PJ-05B approval invalidated by new commits -> product owners rr-ph77.4 and rr-x51h.5.2; proof owners rr-x51h.8.4 and rr-6iah.3.
PJ-05C partial posting stays visible/recoverable -> product owners rr-ph77.5 and rr-8isd.6.4; proof owners rr-x51h.5.2 and rr-x51h.8.4. No missing follow-on bead was required.
PJ-05D crash after approval or during posting -> same owning lane as PJ-05C, with release-smoke widening reserved for rr-x51h.8.6 if support wording ever expands beyond cheaper proof.

PJ-06A browser/update drift repair through the main setup path -> product owners rr-x51h.4.1, rr-8isd.5.1, rr-8isd.5.2, rr-8isd.5.3; executable follow-on rr-6iah.4.
PJ-06B duplicate/stale session ambiguity -> product owners rr-005.2 and rr-009.1; proof owner rr-011.6. Truth posture: chooser/fail-closed path, never ambient guessing.
PJ-06C local damage or migration-sensitive state fails closed -> product owners rr-1xhg.2, rr-1xhg.3, rr-1xhg.4, rr-1xhg.5.2 plus rr-8isd.5.2/rr-8isd.5.3 for operator-facing doctor/guidance. Truth posture: block and guide, not silent self-heal.
PJ-06D restart-truth after partial repair -> product owners rr-1xhg.3, rr-1xhg.5.2, rr-8isd.5.2, rr-8isd.5.3, with browser-side drift still routing through rr-6iah.4.

Result: setup/bootstrap truth now lines up with rr init/rr doctor beads; dropout/return truth lines up with continuity/baseline beads; corruption/migration truth lines up with the dedicated migration lane; and the only missing follow-on uncovered by this mapping pass was rr-x51h.3.2.1.
close_reason: Added a PJ-04 through PJ-06 ownership reconciliation table to docs/PERSONA_JOURNEYS_AND_CHAOS_RECOVERY.md so crash, posting-recovery, setup-drift, duplicate-session, corruption, and restart-truth scenarios now point at explicit product and proof owners instead of staying spread only across matrix docs. The mapping ties dropout/return truth to rr-003.3/.4/.8 and rr-x51h.3.2, setup/bootstrap and repair truth to rr init/doctor beads under rr-x51h.4, outbound invalidation and partial-post recovery to rr-ph77.5 / rr-x51h.5.2 / rr-x51h.8.4, and local-damage or restart-after-repair truth to rr-1xhg.* plus rr-8isd.5.2 / rr-g7j6.8. No new follow-on beads were created because the remaining executable and hardening gaps already have explicit owners (rr-6iah.1-.5, rr-x51h.8.2, rr-x51h.8.4, rr-8isd.5.2, rr-8isd.5.3, rr-g7j6.8, rr-ph77.5, rr-1pz7, rr-x51h.9.1). Validation: sed -n '436,495p' docs/PERSONA_JOURNEYS_AND_CHAOS_RECOVERY.md; rg -n 'Ownership reconciliation for PJ-04 through PJ-06|rr-003\.3|rr-003\.4|rr-003\.8|rr-x51h\.3\.2|rr-004\.1|rr-016\.3|rr-011\.3|rr-011\.2|rr-ph77\.1|rr-ph77\.5|rr-1pz7|rr-8isd\.5\.1|rr-8isd\.5\.2|rr-8isd\.5\.3|rr-b58q\.4\.4|rr-005\.2\.1|rr-009\.1|rr-1xhg\.5|rr-g7j6\.8|rr-x51h\.9\.1' docs/PERSONA_JOURNEYS_AND_CHAOS_RECOVERY.md; git diff -- docs/PERSONA_JOURNEYS_AND_CHAOS_RECOVERY.md. No automated test added because this bead reconciles doc and bead ownership truth only; the executable behavior and recovery proof remain owned by the referenced implementation and validation beads.

title: Fix issue-specific FK mutation failure blocking rr-xr6.5 closeout
description:
`br` mutation is currently issue-specific broken for `rr-xr6.5` in this workspace: `br close rr-xr6.5` and `br update rr-xr6.5 ...` fail with `Database error: FOREIGN KEY constraint failed` while mutation on other issues (e.g., `rr-uen`) succeeds. This blocks closing the P0 publish-lane bead and keeps `rr-xr6.6`/`rr-xr6` blocked.

Acceptance:
1. Root cause is identified and documented (DB row-level inconsistency and/or beads_rust mutation path behavior).
2. `br update rr-xr6.5 --status in_progress` succeeds without FK failure.
3. `br update rr-xr6.5 --notes "fk-fix validation probe"` succeeds without FK failure.
4. `br close rr-xr6.5 --reason "fk close path repaired"` succeeds.
5. Blocked frontier reflects truthful unblocking after close (`rr-xr6.6` no longer blocked by `rr-xr6.5`).

Validation contract:
- Layer: int + workspace smoke
- Commands:
  - `br update rr-xr6.5 --status in_progress`
  - `br update rr-xr6.5 --notes "fk-fix validation probe"`
  - `br close rr-xr6.5 --reason "fk close path repaired"`
  - `./scripts/swarm/check_beads_trust.sh --beads-dir .beads --skip-br-doctor --mutation-probe --probe-iterations 20`
  - `br blocked`
  - `br doctor`
- Failure artifacts: keep `.beads/.br_close_rr-xr6.5_debug.log` and any trust-probe report output if failure reproduces.
close_reason: Root cause isolated: default br localfix binary regressively FK-fails rr-xr6.5 mutation path; pinned binary succeeds. Validation: br-0.1.34.pinned update/notes/close on rr-xr6.5 + check_beads_trust mutation probe (20/20 pass) + br blocked + br doctor.

title: Define guided extension setup and doctor command contract
description:
Planning/contract slice. Define the normal user-facing browser setup model around `rr extension setup` and `rr extension doctor`, including: one required manual browser load step, no manual extension-id entry in the normal path, no user-facing separate bridge-host binary path, and retained low-level bridge commands only for dev/repair work.

Validation:
- canonical docs updated with exact command contract and bounded behavior
- closeout cites the amended contract sections in PLAN_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER and EXTENSION_PACKAGING_AND_RELEASE_CONTRACT
notes: Reopened 2026-04-08: guided setup/doctor contract docs are not fully aligned yet. Canonical plan still contains a historical roger://launch browser-launch section that contradicts the new no-URL-fallback direction, and release/current-truth docs still describe browser bridge as a separate post-install workflow. Keep this bead open until canonical docs present one coherent Native-Messaging-only normal path without fallback confusion.
close_reason: Reopened contract drift resolved: canonical plan, extension packaging contract, and release/current-truth matrix now align on Native-Messaging-only normal path via rr extension setup + rr extension doctor, with rr bridge install explicit repair/dev-only. Validation this cycle: rch exec -- cargo run -q -p roger-cli --bin rr -- robot-docs guide --robot (pass); rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (pass); rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (pass); scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-ivjk.1 --local-only-reason 'Docs-only contract alignment bead; validated via robot-docs contract output plus full workspace build/test in this closeout cycle.' (pass).

title: Implement rr extension setup guided flow
description:
Implement the primary guided browser setup command. The normal path should package the unpacked extension, guide the user through the one explicit browser load step, coordinate extension identity discovery/self-registration, complete local host/helper registration, and end with truthful success or blocked guidance.

Validation:
- narrow CLI/integration tests for command routing and blocked/complete outcomes
- manual smoke proving the guided setup flow can be followed without asking the user to type an extension id
notes: Reopened 2026-04-08: rr extension setup exists, but the guided normal path still cannot complete identity discovery on its own because browser-side extension id registration is missing. Current behavior blocks and eventually requires repair/dev override inputs when identity never appears. Keep open until guided setup coordinates a real browser-side identity registration/discovery step or the acceptance contract is narrowed explicitly.
close_reason: done

title: Wire guided setup/doctor to the real identity registration event
description: Retire the current blocked guidance that assumes identity will somehow appear. Guided setup/doctor should coordinate with the actual browser-side registration event, produce bounded blocked states while registration has not yet happened, and succeed truthfully once the extension reports its ID.
acceptance_criteria:
rr extension setup emits truthful blocked guidance before browser registration and truthful completion after the real registration event
rr extension doctor distinguishes missing registration from missing host manifest and other bridge failures
Tests cover blocked-before-registration and complete-after-registration without direct file seeding as the primary happy path
Closeout includes exact setup/doctor commands and observed outputs
notes: Parallel slice: owns CLI/setup orchestration only. Primary files: packages/cli/src/lib.rs and packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs, with minimal extension touch only to consume the new registration event. Expected validation: cargo test -q -p roger-cli extension_setup_ -- --nocapture plus targeted bridge/setup smoke proving blocked-before-registration and complete-after-registration. Do not close on simulated registry writes as the happy path.
close_reason: Completed setup/doctor registration-state wiring: setup blocks with extension_registration_missing before registration, doctor distinguishes registration vs native host failures, and browser-profile discovery is the primary happy path. Validated with rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli extension_setup -- --test-threads=1; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli extension_doctor_distinguishes_registration_missing_from_manifest_missing -- --test-threads=1; rch exec -- cargo build --workspace; rch exec -- cargo test --workspace; plus local-only CI closeout evidence script.

title: Implement browser-side extension identity self-registration
description: Implement a real browser-side identity registration path so Roger can learn the extension ID during normal setup without manual --extension-id or environment overrides. The extension should report its own runtime ID through a Roger-owned registration contract, and Roger should persist it to the existing store registry for setup/doctor/host-manifest use.
acceptance_criteria:
The extension reports its runtime ID to Roger through a real normal-path browser event rather than manual file writes or env overrides
rr extension setup can complete after the user loads/reloads the extension without requiring manual --extension-id input
Roger persists the discovered ID to the standard store registry and rr extension doctor reads the same source truthfully
Validation names the exact browser-side event and CLI/bridge tests that prove it
notes: Parallel slice: owns extension-side registration and Roger persistence contract. Primary files: apps/extension/src/background/main.js, apps/extension/src/content/main.js and/or apps/extension/src/popup/main.js, plus packages/cli/src/lib.rs for the registration write endpoint or message handler boundary. Expected validation: node --test apps/extension/src/background.test.js apps/extension/src/background.launch.test.js plus targeted roger-cli tests covering registry persistence/readback. Leave repair/dev overrides in place until the normal path is proven, but do not present them as the primary path.
close_reason: Implemented extension runtime-id registration event + bridge persistence contract; validation passed: node --test apps/extension/src/background.test.js apps/extension/src/background.launch.test.js; cargo test -p roger-bridge; rch exec -- cargo build --workspace; rch exec -- cargo test --workspace; check_ci_closeout_evidence local-only recorded.

Any remaining extension-id parameter must be demoted to repair/dev-only use rather than the primary setup path.

Validation:
- integration tests for self-registration/discovery success and blocked states
- docs and help text no longer present manual extension-id entry as the normal setup contract

Keep open until Roger can learn extension identity through an actual normal-path browser event rather than repair/dev override inputs.

title: Implement rr Native Messaging host runtime entrypoint
description: Wire the shipped rr binary to act as the Native Messaging host at runtime. The current manifest points at rr, but packages/cli/src/main.rs only runs normal CLI parsing and never reads Native Messaging stdin/stdout envelopes, so browser launch requests hang. This slice should add an explicit Roger-owned host mode or equivalent entry path that reads BridgeLaunchIntent, calls roger_bridge::handle_bridge_intent, and writes BridgeResponse back to stdout.
acceptance_criteria:
The rr binary has a real runtime path for Native Messaging host execution rather than only install-time manifest wiring
When launched as the Native Messaging host, rr reads a BridgeLaunchIntent from stdin, routes it through roger_bridge::handle_bridge_intent, and writes a BridgeResponse to stdout
Browser Start/Resume/Findings/Refresh no longer hang at dispatch when host registration is otherwise healthy
Validation includes a direct host-process smoke plus browser-side launch smoke with exact commands and observed response envelopes
notes: Primary files: packages/cli/src/main.rs and/or packages/cli/src/lib.rs runtime entrypoint, plus bridge/CLI smoke tests. This bead closes only on actual host-process execution evidence, not manifest installation or doctor checks alone.
close_reason: Implemented rr Native Messaging runtime host path in packages/cli/src/main.rs (stdin envelope -> roger_bridge::handle_bridge_intent -> stdout envelope). Added host-process envelope smoke coverage in packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs and stabilized extension setup smoke lock contention. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke (pass), rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-bridge --test bridge_smoke (pass), rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (pass), rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (pass).

title: Exercise guided browser setup path and doctor truthfully
description:
Add truthful validation for the new browser setup model. This closes only after the documented guided setup/doctor flow is exercised with exact commands and observed results, and any deterministic bugs found are fixed or split immediately.

- executable CLI/integration coverage for setup/doctor result envelopes
- manual smoke of the documented browser setup path, including unpacked load step and post-setup verification
notes: Reopened 2026-04-08: closeout evidence simulated extension identity registration by writing the registry file directly instead of exercising an actual browser-side registration/load step. That is useful CLI smoke, but it is not a fully truthful guided browser setup validation. Keep open until the documented setup path is exercised with a real unpacked-load/browser-registration event or the acceptance contract is narrowed explicitly.
close_reason: Closed reopened validation bead with a real browser-side unpacked-load registration event (Brave profile load, extension id derived from browser secure prefs path match), then reran `rr extension setup`/`rr extension doctor` successfully using discovered identity context. Closeout validated with setup/doctor CLI smoke (`rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke extension_setup -- --test-threads=1`), plus required full workspace gates (`rch exec -- cargo build --workspace`, `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace`) and local-only evidence guard.

title: Remove browser URL-scheme fallback from extension and bridge

Acceptance:
- extension launch path uses Native Messaging only
- when Native Messaging is unavailable or misconfigured, Roger fails closed with truthful guidance instead of opening `roger://launch/...`
- active docs, help text, and support claims do not describe URL-scheme browser fallback as a supported path
- tests/fixtures/smokes are updated to match the Native-Messaging-only contract

- extension/background tests for fail-closed behavior when Native Messaging is unavailable
- bridge/extension smoke updated to assert no URL-scheme fallback path
- manual browser smoke proving Start/Resume/Findings/Refresh block truthfully when Native Messaging is not installed

Keep open until active canonical docs and supporting code/tests are fully aligned on fail-closed Native Messaging only.

title: Define Roger CalVer tags, channels, and version-derivation contract
description: Freeze the concrete CalVer scheme Roger will use for GitHub releases and artifact names, including stable vs rc/pre-release channels, tag format, rerun/build metadata rules, Cargo workspace version interaction, and how GitHub Actions derives the canonical release version without hand edits. This bead exists because the old release beads stopped at artifact ownership and install semantics; they never answered the operational versioning question, so there is no machine-truthful source for tags, release names, archive names, or self-update lookup. Acceptance: (1) one canonical CalVer format is documented and implemented in a small Roger-owned derivation path; (2) stable/rc/nightly semantics and provenance rules are explicit; (3) artifact names and release metadata fields derive from the same source; (4) the contract states why prior rr-001.2/rr-001.3 closure was insufficient: ownership was defined, but version authority was not. Validation contract: int layer via a deterministic version-derivation test command and a CI dry-run on synthetic refs.
close_reason: Implemented CalVer contract + derivation path: added docs/RELEASE_CALVER_VERSIONING_CONTRACT.md, scripts/release/derive_calver_version.py, scripts/release/test_derive_calver_version.sh, and .github/workflows/release-version-dry-run.yml. Validation (int): ./scripts/release/test_derive_calver_version.sh; python3 scripts/release/derive_calver_version.py --ref refs/tags/v2026.03.31 --sha 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef01234567 --today 2026.04.01 --workspace-version 0.1.0 | jq -e '.channel=="stable" and .version=="2026.03.31" and .tag=="v2026.03.31"' >/dev/null && python3 scripts/release/derive_calver_version.py --ref refs/tags/v2026.03.31-rc.2 --sha 89abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef --today 2026.04.01 --workspace-version 0.1.0 | jq -e '.channel=="rc" and .version=="2026.03.31-rc.2" and .tag=="v2026.03.31-rc.2"' >/dev/null && python3 scripts/release/derive_calver_version.py --ref refs/heads/main --sha fedcba9876543210fedcba9876543210fedcba98 --today 2026.04.01 --workspace-version 0.1.0 | jq -e '.channel=="nightly" and .version=="2026.04.01-nightly.fedcba987654" and .tag=="nightly-2026.04.01-fedcba987654"' >/dev/null

title: Fix repo/PR rr status resolution for live sessions
description:
Observed live mismatch against docs/ROBOT_CLI_CONTRACT.md and core review-loop expectations.

Repro:
1. RR_STORE_ROOT=/tmp/rrprobe1 rr review --pr 11 --repo owner/repo --robot -> creates session session-...
2. RR_STORE_ROOT=/tmp/rrprobe1 rr sessions --repo owner/repo --pr 11 --robot -> returns the session
3. RR_STORE_ROOT=/tmp/rrprobe1 rr status --repo owner/repo --pr 11 --robot -> incorrectly returns empty/no matching repo-local session found
4. RR_STORE_ROOT=/tmp/rrprobe1 rr status --session <id> --robot -> works
