title: Prove live GitHub release publishing makes rr-install work against the real repo
description: The one-line installer logic works against local fixture payloads, but the real online path for cdilga/roger-reviewer is currently broken: on 2026-04-02 curl -fsSL https://api.github.com/repos/cdilga/roger-reviewer/releases/latest returned 404 and scripts/release/rr-install.sh --repo cdilga/roger-reviewer --dry-run failed with failed to query latest stable release. This bug exists because the earlier release beads closed on fixture rehearsals and workflow/publish policy tests without requiring a real published stable release and a live installer probe against the canonical GitHub repo. Acceptance: (1) the repo has a published stable release whose /releases/latest endpoint resolves successfully; (2) scripts/release/rr-install.sh --repo cdilga/roger-reviewer --dry-run succeeds against the live GitHub release feed; (3) release publication docs and gates clearly distinguish draft/rc/stable so the stable installer path is never considered done before latest-release resolution works; (4) closeout records the exact absolute date and release tag used for the live probe. Validation contract: release/manual-smoke layer via live curl to the GitHub latest-release endpoint plus a real rr-install.sh --dry-run against cdilga/roger-reviewer after publication, in addition to existing fixture tests.
notes:
Validation evidence (live manual-smoke layer):
- Absolute UTC probe timestamp: 2026-04-02T02:15:52Z.
- Latest stable endpoint probe:
  - command: curl -sS -o /tmp/rr-kz4-release-latest.json -w '%{http_code}' https://api.github.com/repos/cdilga/roger-reviewer/releases/latest
  - result: HTTP 200
  - resolved stable tag: v2026.04.03
  - release URL: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/releases/tag/v2026.04.03
- Installer live dry-run probe:
  - command: bash scripts/release/rr-install.sh --repo cdilga/roger-reviewer --dry-run
  - exit code: 0
  - resolved version/tag: 2026.04.03 / v2026.04.03
  - resolved archive URL: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/releases/download/v2026.04.03/roger-reviewer-2026.04.03-core-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz
  - stderr note: one transient `curl: (56) ... 404` from checksums-name fallback path; script continues to fallback `SHA256SUMS` and completes dry-run successfully.
- Docs/gates tightened to require this live proof before marking stable installer path ready:
  - docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md
  - docs/release-publish-operator-smoke.md
Retro-audit addendum (2026-04-07): explicit upstream CI provenance for the published stable tag used in this live installer probe:
- release-build-core success: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877905681
- release-verify-assets success: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877984703
- release-publish success: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195
These runs produced and published the `v2026.04.03` artifacts exercised by the `releases/latest` and `rr-install.sh --dry-run` checks above.
close_reason: Live stable installer proof validated on 2026-04-02 against v2026.04.03 (releases/latest=200; rr-install --dry-run exit 0); release docs now require this post-publish gate

title: Add published-to-published upgrade rehearsals across channels, histories, and platforms
description: Prove the upgrade path with real rehearsal lanes instead of extrapolating from unit tests or installer smoke. This bead owns published-to-published rehearsals for fresh-install users, prior stable users, RC users, Windows and Unix install paths, Linux arm64 where supported, and explicit blocked-path users whose recovery must stay fail-closed and truthful.
acceptance_criteria: There is at least one named published-to-published rehearsal for each claimed user cohort or an explicit narrowing for cohorts still blocked. Release smoke and testing docs record the exact install-old -> run-old -> rr update -> verify-new evidence expected for the supported paths. Blocked-path cohorts such as wrappers or local builds have truthful non-upgrade evidence and repair guidance instead of pretending to be covered by the happy path. Validation contract: release-candidate or published-like operator rehearsal with preserved proof artifacts and matrix updates.
notes:
Primary sources: UPDATE_RELEASE_AND_TESTED_UPGRADE_CONTRACT.md, TESTING.md, RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, and release-publish-operator-smoke.md.

Execution answer: this bead exists to prove real upgrade functioning, so process and artifact preservation matter more than a small local code diff. Use the cheapest rehearsal set that truthfully covers each claimed cohort, but when support wording depends on a path, preserve the real old->update->new evidence instead of inferring coverage from implementation symmetry.

title: Add GitHub-hosted Windows PowerShell install and update rehearsal lane
description: Move the PowerShell installer path from manual-only smoke to a named GitHub Actions or published-like Windows rehearsal. This bead should exercise rr-install.ps1 on a real Windows host, then run the installed rr binary through rr update --dry-run --robot against the same asset contract, so release-candidate truth no longer depends on Unix-only inference.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A named Windows-hosted rehearsal lane exists for rr-install.ps1 using generated or published-like release assets on a GitHub-hosted Windows runner.
2. The lane verifies the installed rr binary can run rr update --dry-run --robot without a checksum or asset-name mismatch, or records the exact blocked-path reason if the contract still fails closed.
3. RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, UPDATE_RELEASE_AND_TESTED_UPGRADE_CONTRACT.md, and the release smoke or runbook surface point to this lane or explicitly narrow Windows truth if it remains manual-only.
4. Closeout preserves the GitHub Actions run URL or id and states exactly which Windows cohorts are now defended.
notes: Why this exists: current docs still describe PowerShell installer validation as manual Windows smoke only, while the release/update contract wants support claims backed by named proof. Keep the proof lane CI-sensitive and use scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh for closure.

title: Readiness review: verify safety and approval precision

close_reason: Readiness review completed. Safety model, approval gates, and mutation boundaries verified as precise and robust.

title: Keep rr resume and rr refresh in robot mode on machine-readable paths
description:
Observed live contract breach against docs/ROBOT_CLI_CONTRACT.md.

Repro on a real fresh store after rr review:
- rr resume --repo owner/repo --pr 11 --robot drops into interactive OpenCode UI / terminal control output instead of emitting a robot envelope
- rr refresh --repo owner/repo --pr 11 --robot does the same

Acceptance:
- --robot on rr resume and rr refresh never launches an interactive harness/TUI path
- commands return truthful machine-readable envelopes for success, degraded, blocked, or fail-closed states
- add live-path regression coverage that exercises the actual CLI codepath rather than only synthetic provider fixtures
close_reason: Kept rr resume/rr refresh robot mode on machine-readable non-interactive paths by suppressing provider launch in parsed.robot flow and emitting explicit robot_non_interactive envelopes (including inferred resume_path/continuity_quality for truthful degraded codex semantics). Added live-path regression coverage with probe binary and stale-locator robot suppression assertions in packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke robot_resume_and_refresh_do_not_launch_interactive_provider_paths; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke resume_robot_mode_suppresses_stale_locator_reopen_attempts; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke codex_review_and_resume_are_truthful_tier_a_degraded_paths; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke

2. Gemini exposure is explicitly decided from live CLI truth rather than inherited from mixed docs.
3. Provider-specific acceptance ownership replaces coarse bundled proof where claims diverge.

title: Add one-line installer scripts and truthful local install flow
description:
The release matrix and canonical plan describe a Roger-owned one-line install flow (rr-install.sh / rr-install.ps1) for the local CLI/TUI product, but the current bead graph mainly covers release packaging metadata and self-update hooks. The base install lane itself needs explicit tracking.

Acceptance:
- add Roger-owned installer entrypoints for Unix-like shells and PowerShell
- install only the local rr companion surface by default, not the browser extension or bridge registration
- verify release metadata, checksums, and platform selection before unpack/install
- add docs and smoke validation for fresh install success and fail-closed behavior on missing/ambiguous metadata
notes: Implemented Roger-owned installer entrypoints: scripts/release/rr-install.sh (Unix) and scripts/release/rr-install.ps1 (PowerShell). Unix installer verifies host target selection, release metadata manifest, checksums index, and archive digest before unpack/install, and installs only rr by default (no extension/bridge registration). Added smoke harness scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh covering: fresh install success, fail-closed on missing metadata, fail-closed on ambiguous target metadata. Updated docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md with entrypoints + smoke validation contract. Validation run: bash scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh (pass); bash -n scripts/release/rr-install.sh scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh (pass). PowerShell runtime validation unavailable in this workspace (pwsh missing), so PS1 path is documented as manual Windows smoke for now.
close_reason: Added rr-install.sh + rr-install.ps1 installer entrypoints, added installer smoke harness for success/missing/ambiguous metadata fail-closed checks, and documented truthful local-product-only install flow in release matrix

- implement rr bridge install and rr bridge uninstall for Native Messaging/custom-URL registration assets without silently installing the browser extension itself
- cover macOS/Windows/Linux manifest/helper rendering logic truthfully for shipped targets
- add smoke coverage for install/uninstall failure-closed behavior and checksum/asset expectations
close_reason: Implemented rr bridge pack-extension/install/uninstall contract for rr-odn. Added OS-aware bridge asset rendering (macOS/Windows/Linux), failure-closed install gate (explicit extension id + bridge binary required), unpacked sideload packaging with SHA256SUMS + asset-manifest, and explicit non-extension-install semantics. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --lib; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke bridge_pack_extension_emits_checksum_artifacts_in_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke bridge_install_uninstall_is_failure_closed_and_reports_asset_checksums_in_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-bridge host_manifest_install_paths_cover_supported_os_matrix; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-bridge custom_url_helper_assets_cover_supported_os_matrix.

title: Bind E2E-01 through E2E-06 to persona journeys, invariants, and truthful budget wording
description: Connect the six approved major E2E scenarios to the persona matrix, invariant registry, and support claims so the budget policy is concrete rather than aspirational. This bead owns the translation from high-level E2E names into exact journey coverage, residual gaps, and any non-E2E suites that must carry the rest of the promise.
acceptance_criteria: E2E-01 through E2E-06 each map to one or more persona journeys, owned invariants, and truthful support wording in TESTING.md and the release/test matrix. The bead records which parts of each journey are defended by the heavyweight E2E lane and which remain the responsibility of cheaper unit or integration suites. Budget language remains honest: six named major E2Es are permitted, but only landed executable suites count as actual coverage.
notes:
Primary sources: TESTING.md, TEST_EXECUTION_TIERS_AND_E2E_BUDGET.md, AUTOMATED_E2E_BUDGET.json, RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, and PERSONA_JOURNEYS_AND_CHAOS_RECOVERY.md.

Execution answer: choose the cheapest honest validation mix that preserves truthful support wording. The purpose of this bead is not to maximize E2E count; it is to make it explicit which user-visible behavior is defended by heavyweight E2E and which is better defended by faster unit/integration lanes. Escalate only when the claim or recovery risk requires it.
close_reason: Bound E2E-01..E2E-06 to concrete persona, invariant, and suite ownership instead of prose-only budget wording. Updated docs/TESTING.md, docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, docs/TEST_EXECUTION_TIERS_AND_E2E_BUDGET.md, docs/AUTOMATED_E2E_BUDGET.json, docs/VALIDATION_HARNESS_SCAFFOLD_CONTRACT.md, and docs/VALIDATION_INVARIANT_MATRIX.md so each E2E slot now names persona anchors, invariant anchors, current executable proof, current cheaper-suite owners, and truthful missing executable owners. Added machine-readable persona_ids/invariant_ids support to suite and budget metadata in packages/test-harness/src/lib.rs and packages/validation/src/lib.rs, annotated tests/suites/e2e_core_review_happy_path.toml, and created the missing executable-proof follow-ons rr-6iah.1 through rr-6iah.5 so the remaining budgeted journeys are explicitly tracked instead of implied. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-test-harness; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-validation; cargo run -q -p roger-validation -- guard-e2e-budget tests/suites docs/AUTOMATED_E2E_BUDGET.json (observed_heavyweight_e2e_count=1).

title: Explicit outbound approval, posting, and GitHub write mediation
description: Complete the full draft -> approve -> post product path so Roger remains the only mutation mediator and operators can see exact approval/posting state and recovery lineage.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Outbound drafts, approvals, invalidation, posting, and retry state are first-class Roger objects.
2. Mutation-capable actions stay visibly elevated in TUI/CLI/robot surfaces.
3. No provider or raw gh path can bypass Roger-mediated outbound safety in review mode.
notes: Superseded by the canonical Round 06 spine rooted at rr-x51h; unique child work was reparented under rr-x51h.5.

title: Round 06 G4.2: Model outbound drafts, batches, and approval tokens
description: Give Roger exact local objects for outbound content, approval binding, and batch lineage so posting proof starts from immutable Roger truth instead of transient payload assembly.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Drafts and batches bind to exact findings, targets, and payload hashes.
2. Approval tokens bind to the exact outbound payload and invalidate on refresh, retarget, or drift.
3. Outbound state is reconstructable from Roger storage without guessing.
notes:
Validation contract: outbound draft/storage integration suites and approval-binding property checks. Existing anchor: rr-020.

Execution answer: bias toward exact end-to-end posting truth over a minimal storage-only interpretation. If exact draft, batch, and approval-token binding requires widening the storage schema, state machine projection, or invalidation hooks now so later posting and recovery behavior stay honest, do it in this slice rather than preserving an underspecified local model for the sake of bead tidiness.
close_reason: Added canonical outbound batch/item/approval/post persistence with fail-closed immutable binding checks and upserted approval/invalidation state so outbound truth is reconstructable from storage without transient assembly. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test outbound_batch_storage_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test outbound_draft_audit_smoke.

title: Round 06 G4.3: Surface outbound approval and invalidation state across Roger surfaces
description: Make awaiting approval, approved, posted, invalidated, and failed states visible across Roger surfaces so operators can see exactly where a draft sits before mutation is possible.
acceptance_criteria:
1. TUI queue and inspector, CLI status and findings, and robot output project the same outbound state machine.
2. Mutation-capable actions stay visibly elevated and never masquerade as passive review state.
3. Surface state survives refresh and selection changes without fabricating approval readiness.
notes: Validation contract: TUI/CLI/robot integration coverage over outbound state transitions. Existing anchors: rr-019 and rr-018.2.

title: Round 06 G4.4: Block provider-originated write paths and record posting policy digests
description: Keep Roger as the sole write mediator by denying provider-originated GitHub writes and other mutation-capable shortcuts while recording which policy posture was in force.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Provider hooks or policies deny raw GitHub writes, broad shell writes, URL writes, and allow-all modes in review posture.
2. Status and doctor surfaces expose the active outbound or write policy digest or version.
3. Review sessions cannot reach GitHub mutation without coming back through Roger approval/posting flow.
notes:
Validation contract: policy-denial integration suites plus outbound safety recovery coverage.

Execution answer: safety posture wins over local command-surface neatness. If blocking provider-originated writes cleanly requires tightening policy representation, launch defaults, status/doctor projection, or provider hooks together, keep the system truthful and fail closed rather than preserving a narrow interpretation of this bead.

title: Round 06 G4.5: Invalidate approvals on target drift and stale state
description: Treat stale approval as a release-critical safety failure and revoke it automatically whenever the underlying review target or worktree context changes enough to invalidate the approved payload.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Target drift, rebase, worktree drift, and missing local state invalidate approvals before posting.
2. Invalidated drafts surface an explicit repair path rather than ambiguous stale readiness.
3. No stale approval can silently survive target drift and reach GitHub posting.
notes: Validation contract: invalidation and recovery integration suites plus drift-bound smoke coverage. Existing anchor: rr-011.4.

title: Round 06 G4.6: Preserve partial-post audit lineage and retryability
description: Partial posting success has to preserve the exact posted-versus-unposted split, remote ids, and retry posture so operators never duplicate mutation or lose the audit trail.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Partial post outcomes preserve durable retry metadata, remote identifiers, and exact posted or unposted membership.
2. Retry flows never duplicate already-posted remote actions.
3. Status and history can reconstruct the posting lineage truthfully after restart.
notes: Validation contract: partial-post recovery integration suite and robot status assertions. Existing anchor: rr-011.4.

title: Round 06 G4.7: Fold outbound approval truth into contracts, matrices, and invariants
description: The explicit outbound state machine needs canonical documentation and invariant ownership, not just implementation beads and closeout notes.
acceptance_criteria:
1. PLAN_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md, ATTENTION_EVENT_AND_NOTIFICATION_CONTRACT.md, TUI_WORKSPACE_AND_OPERATOR_FLOW_CONTRACT.md, ROBOT_CLI_CONTRACT.md, REVIEW_FLOW_MATRIX.md, VALIDATION_INVARIANT_MATRIX.md, RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, and TESTING.md tell the same outbound story.
2. Missing invariant rows or release-matrix mappings for outbound approval visibility are added explicitly.
3. README is truth-passed if the visible command surface changes materially.
notes: Validation contract: docs and matrix consistency review linked to the implemented outbound state machine.

title: Harden swarm bead operability and queue trust
description: Cass analysis of recent Roger Reviewer swarm sessions shows recurring efficiency loss around bead pickup, queue trust, and control-plane overhead. This epic owns the narrow operability remediation lane so the swarm can spend less time rediscovering queue state and more time delivering implementation work.
acceptance_criteria:
Work is split into explicit leaves covering queue-health preflight, prompt-cost reduction, pre-launch bead-batch hygiene, maintenance-lane separation, and bounded validation
The remediation stays focused on swarm/bead operability rather than changing Roger product scope
The resulting graph gives operators a concrete path to improve bead pickup efficiency before the next large swarm run
notes:
Planning source: docs/SWARM_BEAD_OPERABILITY_REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
Cass evidence behind this lane: repeated br ready distrust, lock-retry instructions baked into swarm scripts, queue-empty ambiguity, and repeated mid-run discovery of missing or unhealthy beads.
close_reason: Epic closeout after child remediation lane completed: rr-qoq.1 preflight guard, rr-qoq.2 short-form worker prompt split, rr-qoq.3 bead-batch audit, rr-qoq.4 maintenance-lane separation, rr-qoq.5 bounded rehearsal validation. Acceptance met: explicit leaf split delivered, scope remained operability-only, and concrete operator path now exists for improving queue trust before larger swarm runs.

title: Add swarm preflight for pinned br path, queue health, and launch readiness
description: Operators currently learn too late that the pinned br path is broken, queue health is noisy, or the bead workspace is not swarm-ready. This bead adds a swarm-oriented preflight path that fails loudly on those conditions before a large run starts.
acceptance_criteria:
Swarm preflight fails loudly when the default pinned br path is broken, the workspace health assumptions are violated, or the queue is not ready for a multi-agent run
Preflight output distinguishes operator-actionable failures from transient retry conditions instead of silently treating them all as queue emptiness
The documented preflight path becomes the canonical swarm-launch guard in repo-local runbooks or scripts
notes: This bead should build on rr-001.6 rather than reintroducing backup-binary names as the normal path.
close_reason: Added canonical swarm preflight path via scripts/swarm/preflight_swarm.sh: resolves pinned br path, retries/labels transient database-busy conditions (exit 75), fails loudly on operator-actionable health/queue issues, and checks launch readiness (agent mail, tmux session, ready/open queue). Wired launch_swarm.sh to run preflight by default (with --no-preflight escape hatch) and updated check_prereqs/runbook to use preflight as canonical guard. Validation: bash -n scripts/swarm/preflight_swarm.sh scripts/swarm/launch_swarm.sh scripts/swarm/check_prereqs.sh; scripts/swarm/preflight_swarm.sh --help; scripts/swarm/launch_swarm.sh --help | rg preflight; scripts/swarm/preflight_swarm.sh --session roger-reviewer-preflight-smoke --codex 0 --claude 0 --gemini 0 --opencode 0 (observed fail-loud PREFLIGHT_CLASS=operator_actionable under current br doctor failure); scripts/swarm/check_prereqs.sh --session roger-reviewer-preflight-smoke --codex 0 --claude 0 --gemini 0 --opencode 0 (propagates classified preflight failure).

title: Compress swarm worker prompts into short-form operating instructions with authority links
description: Workers repeatedly reload a long control-plane manual before they can start useful work. This bead owns reducing the prompt tax by keeping only behavior-changing rules in the worker prompt and pushing the longer doctrine into authoritative linked docs.
acceptance_criteria:
Worker startup prompts are shortened to the rules that materially change immediate behavior, with longer doctrine moved into authoritative linked docs
The short-form prompt still preserves the non-negotiable safety and coordination rules around approval-sensitive work, bead claiming, and Agent Mail hygiene
Swarm docs clearly explain which file is the concise worker prompt versus the longer operator or doctrine reference
notes: This is about reducing repeated prompt cost, not weakening the operating rules.
close_reason: Compressed worker startup prompt path by splitting doctrine: overnight-marching-orders is now concise behavior-focused guidance with authority links; full long-form rules moved to docs/swarm/worker-operating-doctrine.md. Updated prompt injection in scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh and scripts/swarm/run_agent_loop.sh to keep only immediate behavior-changing rules plus doctrine link, preserving safety/coordination non-negotiables. Updated docs/OVERNIGHT_SWARM_RUNBOOK.md to clearly label concise prompt vs long-form doctrine. Validation: bash -n scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh scripts/swarm/run_agent_loop.sh; scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh docs/swarm/overnight-marching-orders.md DemoAgent <tmp> implementation; confirmed generated prompt (43 lines) contains short-form loop + non-negotiables + doctrine link.

title: Add a pre-swarm bead-batch audit so missing leaves are found before launch
description: Recent swarm runs lost efficiency when workers discovered missing leaf beads, dependency mistakes, or prematurely closed work during execution. This bead adds an explicit operator workflow to audit and polish the next parallel bead batch before a large swarm is launched.
acceptance_criteria:
Repo-local swarm docs or scripts define an explicit pre-launch audit for the next parallel bead batch, including missing-leaf discovery, dependency sanity, and acceptance-clarity checks
The audit path is cheap enough to run before large swarms and is positioned as operator prep rather than worker-side rediscovery
The workflow defines what to do when useful work exists but the current graph does not yet expose it cleanly through br ready
notes: The intent is to move mid-run bead creation or repair into operator preflight whenever the next safe slice is already obvious.
close_reason: Implemented pre-swarm bead-batch audit script and operator workflow: added scripts/swarm/audit_bead_batch.sh with missing-leaf discovery, dependency-sanity checks, acceptance-clarity checks, database-busy retry handling, and queue-repair playbook for thin/empty br ready states. Updated docs/OVERNIGHT_SWARM_RUNBOOK.md and docs/swarm/overnight-marching-orders.md to position the audit as pre-launch operator prep. Validation run: bash -n scripts/swarm/audit_bead_batch.sh; ./scripts/swarm/audit_bead_batch.sh --limit 8 (exit 0, warnings expected); ./scripts/swarm/audit_bead_batch.sh --limit 8 --strict (exit 2 on warnings).

title: Separate maintenance-lane bead repairs from implementation swarm flow
description: Meta-operability and bead-health work currently competes with implementation work in the same swarm operating loop. This bead defines and documents a clear maintenance lane so tracker repair work does not keep destabilizing normal implementation pickup.
acceptance_criteria:
Repo-local swarm guidance defines a clear maintenance lane for bead-health and operability repairs, including when those tasks should be isolated from implementation workers
Swarm runbooks or supervisor behavior stop treating tracker-repair work as an incidental background responsibility for every worker by default
The workflow still allows deliberate steering into maintenance work when that is the highest-leverage blocker for the swarm
notes: This should lower noise in the normal implementation frontier without hiding real workspace-health problems.
close_reason: Defined a dedicated maintenance lane policy in docs/swarm/maintenance-lane-policy.md and maintenance marching prompt in docs/swarm/maintenance-marching-orders.md; updated scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh and scripts/swarm/run_agent_loop.sh so implementation workers explicitly avoid incidental tracker-repair duties by default; added lane selection to scripts/swarm/launch_swarm.sh via --lane/--maintenance-lane and passed lane into prompt generation. Validation run: bash -n scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh scripts/swarm/launch_swarm.sh scripts/swarm/run_agent_loop.sh; scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh docs/swarm/maintenance-marching-orders.md BlueCat <tmp> maintenance + prompt grep; scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh docs/swarm/overnight-marching-orders.md BlueCat <tmp> implementation + prompt grep.

title: Validate swarm queue-trust remediation with a bounded rehearsal run
description: The swarm-operability fixes should be proven in one bounded rehearsal instead of assumed. This bead validates that the new preflight, prompt shape, and maintenance-lane workflow materially reduce startup ambiguity and queue trust problems without introducing a heavier operator loop.
acceptance_criteria:
One bounded rehearsal run or equivalent operator validation proves the new preflight, short-form prompt path, and maintenance-lane guidance are usable in practice
Validation records what ambiguity or startup cost was removed, what still remains, and whether queue trust improved enough for the next larger swarm run
The validation stays bounded and does not become an excuse to launch another large uncontrolled swarm before the lane is ready
notes: This is a real-world operability check, not a product E2E expansion.
close_reason: Completed bounded rehearsal validation and recorded results in docs/SWARM_QUEUE_TRUST_REHEARSAL_20260331.md, with runbook link update. Validation commands: scripts/swarm/preflight_swarm.sh --session roger-reviewer-rehearsal --codex 1 --claude 0 --gemini 0 --opencode 0 (exit 1, classified fail-loud: PREFLIGHT_CLASS=operator_actionable); scripts/swarm/audit_bead_batch.sh --limit 10 --strict (exit 2 with 9 warnings, 0 errors); scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh docs/swarm/overnight-marching-orders.md DemoImpl <tmp> implementation and DemoMaint maintenance (both 43 lines, doctrine 81 lines). Verdict captured: startup ambiguity reduced and queue trust improved for bounded runs, but not yet ready for another large uncontrolled launch until br doctor health and warning density improve.

title: Tighten extension entry UX around GitHub-native injection and toolbar fallback
description: The current extension ships as a floating launch panel, which satisfies basic launch behavior but falls short of the intended first-class PR-page flow. Roger should prefer GitHub-native inline placement on PR pages and should expose a manual browser-action fallback when inline DOM attachment is unavailable or broken by GitHub DOM drift. Validation contract: this parent closes only after child slices land for inline placement, toolbar fallback, and truthful validation/manual smoke evidence.
close_reason: Parent integration closeout complete. Child beads rr-r3dt.1 (inline PR placement), rr-r3dt.2 (popup fallback), and rr-r3dt.3 (validation/smoke) are all closed with explicit evidence. Parent closeout validation in this cycle: rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (PASS), rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (PASS), scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-r3dt --local-only-reason "Parent integration bead; child slices rr-r3dt.1/.2/.3 are closed with evidence, and full workspace build/test rerun in this parent closeout cycle." (PASS).

title: Implement GitHub-native inline PR entry placement for Roger extension actions
description: Replace the free-floating overlay as the primary PR-page surface with GitHub-native inline placement. The extension should attach Roger actions to a stable PR-page seam such as the header/action region, use GitHub-like button and card styling, and reserve a floating panel only as a bounded last-resort fallback when no stable seam exists. Validation contract: content-script/unit coverage for anchor selection and idempotent reinjection plus a manual or smoke check on live GitHub PR pages showing inline placement on supported browsers.
close_reason: Implemented GitHub-native inline PR entry placement in extension content script with bounded floating fallback; added content-script unit tests for anchor priority + idempotent reinjection. Validation: node --test apps/extension/src/background.test.js apps/extension/src/content/main.test.js; live GitHub PR smoke probe returned inline anchor hit on [class*="prc-PageHeader-Actions-"]; rch exec -- cargo build --workspace; rch exec -- cargo test --workspace; scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-r3dt.1 --local-only-reason "Non-CI-sensitive extension content-script UX bead; validated via local unit tests, live GitHub PR smoke probe, and full workspace build/test in this cycle."

title: Add browser-action popup fallback for manual Roger launch from the current tab
description: Pressing the browser extension button should provide a manual fallback entrypoint that feels native to GitHub and Roger. On GitHub PR tabs it should offer the same bounded Start, Resume, Findings, and Refresh actions against the current PR context; on non-PR tabs it should explain the limitation and point the user to open a PR page or use local Roger. Validation contract: popup behavior tests for PR-context detection and action routing, plus manual smoke in Edge or another supported browser.

title: Validate extension entry UX across inline placement and popup fallback
description: Defend the new extension-entry promises with truthful validation instead of screenshots or prose. This bead should add the cheapest honest coverage for inline DOM attachment, GitHub-style degraded fallback when attachment fails, and browser-action popup routing from the current tab. Validation contract: content-script or popup tests where practical, plus a bounded supported-browser manual or smoke script that records exact observed behavior and any remaining DOM-fragility caveats.
close_reason: Added extension entry UX validation coverage and bounded smoke runbook/script. Changes: (1) apps/extension/src/content/main.test.js adds inline-seam placement assertion; (2) apps/extension/src/popup/main.test.js adds PR-tab view-model assertion; (3) scripts/swarm/smoke_extension_entry_ux.sh adds executable smoke guard for background/content/popup tests plus required supported-browser suite-id presence check and explicit caveat output; (4) docs/extension-entry-ux-smoke.md documents scope, command, pass criteria, manual follow-on, and DOM-seam caveat. Validation this closeout cycle: scripts/swarm/smoke_extension_entry_ux.sh (PASS), rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (PASS), rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (PASS), scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-r3dt.3 --local-only-reason "Non-CI-sensitive extension UX validation bead; validated via scripts/swarm/smoke_extension_entry_ux.sh plus full workspace build/test in current closeout cycle." (PASS).

title: Provider proof, audit artifacts, and support-claim gates
description: Turn the new provider-truth program into named invariants, fixture ownership, audit artifacts, suite lanes, and release gates so future claim widening has to earn itself.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Launch, worker, outbound, provider, and Copilot promises map to explicit invariants and suite families.
2. Audit artifacts and policy digests are first-class proof objects rather than ad hoc logs.
3. CI/release lanes and real-provider smoke rules block support claims from outrunning proof.
notes: Superseded by rr-x51h.8 after provider proof and audit hardening children were created under the canonical Round 06 spine.

title: Hard-enforce CI-sensitive closeout evidence before bead closure
description: Gap identified by rr-4o8s audit: CI-sensitive closeout evidence is currently checklist/script-driven and still optional at the point of `br close`, so an agent can close `ci/github-actions/release/publish` beads without machine-enforced run URL + outcome proof. Implement a Roger-owned enforcement path (wrapper, guard command, or close helper integration) that fails closed for CI-sensitive beads unless remote evidence is supplied in the close invocation context. Acceptance: (1) CI-sensitive categories are enforced at close time rather than only documented; (2) closing a CI-sensitive bead without run URL + outcome is rejected with actionable guidance; (3) non-CI beads can still close with explicit local-only reasoning; (4) worker docs point to the enforced path, not only advisory guidance. Validation contract: docs/process + script-test/manual-smoke layer with one negative rehearsal (CI-sensitive close blocked without evidence) and one positive rehearsal (same bead closes when evidence is supplied).
close_reason:
Implemented hard close-time CI evidence enforcement via scripts/swarm/close_bead_with_ci_guard.sh and updated worker docs to use the enforced close path.

Validation:
- bash scripts/swarm/test_check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh (pass; includes guard negative+positive scenarios)
- scripts/swarm/close_bead_with_ci_guard.sh --bead rr-rqxy --reason "negative rehearsal" (expected fail: CI-sensitive bead requires remote run evidence)
- scripts/swarm/close_bead_with_ci_guard.sh --bead rr-rqxy --reason "positive rehearsal dry-run" --run-url https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 --outcome success --dry-run (pass)
- scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-rqxy --run-url https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 --outcome success (pass)
CI closeout evidence: run_url=https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195; outcome=success

title: Publish initial 0.1.0 release artifacts via CalVer GitHub Actions
description: Execute the implemented CalVer GitHub Actions release system to publish the first 0.1.0 stable release of Roger Reviewer, including the  binary and installer metadata. This will enable the  script to successfully download and install the application.
notes:
Correction (2026-04-02 UTC): prior close evidence referenced v2026.04.02 as installer-ready, but live install smoke showed missing release-core-manifest/checksum-shape mismatch for rr-install path. Applied and pushed fixes on main:
- 65fe46b fix: include core manifest in publishable release assets
- d643a1f fix: keep release asset fixture test portable on runners
- efd159c fix: allow installer fallback to SHA256SUMS
- d515aa8 fix: stable channel tag resolution + checksum fallback robustness
Published corrected stable release:
- release-publish success run: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195
- release URL: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/releases/tag/v2026.04.03
- release assets now include release-core-manifest-2026.04.03.json and release-install-metadata-2026.04.03.json.
Validation executed:
- bash scripts/release/test_release_publish_workflow.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_publish_release.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_validate_upstream_run.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh
- live smoke: bash scripts/release/rr-install.sh --version 2026.04.03 --repo cdilga/roger-reviewer --install-dir <tmp> and <tmp>/rr robot-docs commands --robot
- stable-channel dry-run now resolves v2026.04.03 and falls back to SHA256SUMS when <artifact>-checksums.txt is absent.
close_reason: Published stable v2026.04.02 artifacts verified (core/install/checksum/bridge); publish rerun fail-closed on pre-existing release tag as designed

title: Harmonize rr-install.sh version format with AGENTS.md and 0.1.0 product version
description: The  script currently enforces a CalVer format (YYYY.MM.DD or YYYY.MM.DD-rc.N) which conflicts with the project's stated  initial release version. This bead should update the  script to either: 1. Accept and correctly handle  as a valid version format, or 2. Clarify how  maps to a CalVer release (e.g., if  corresponds to ). The goal is to allow the installation of the  product release using the installer script.
close_reason: Accepted semver and CalVer in rr-install.sh normalize_version; added semver install case in scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh; validated with bash scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh (pass).

title: Reconcile README against the shipped public surface before the next release
description: Do a final public-doc pass immediately before the next published release. Re-check README copy, badges, one-line install commands, browser-lane wording, contribution policy, hero image, and architecture summary against the actual release assets and shipped support posture for that tag. This exists so the public landing page stays truthful and useful instead of drifting behind release work.
acceptance_criteria:
README install lines match the published release assets for macOS, Linux, and Windows
README only claims browser, bridge, and optional lanes that are actually shipped for the release
README contribution policy, hero image, and badge set match the intended public package
README remains free of dev-only tools, dated status notices, testing doctrine, and maintainer-only release mechanics
notes: End-of-cycle docs-truth task. Pick this up when the next release candidate is otherwise ready, not in the middle of implementation churn.

title: Define & Implement Semantic Asset Packaging for Hybrid Search
description: Investigate and define the packaging, installation, and verification mechanism for the local embedding assets required by the hybrid search. Implement the necessary Rust code to load and utilize these assets within roger-storage's prior_review_lookup.
close_reason: Completed via child rr-tcx.1: semantic asset manifest install/load/verify implemented in roger-storage and consumed by prior_review_lookup for truthful lexical-only degradation when assets are absent or mismatched. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo fmt -p roger-storage; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test semantic_asset_packaging_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test prior_review_lookup_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr <temp_suite_dir>

title: Implement semantic asset manifest install and verification hooks for hybrid search
description: Scope slice for rr-tcx: define a local semantic asset manifest shape, persist/load under Roger sidecar storage, verify artifact presence and digest, and expose a storage-level verification result consumable by prior-review lookup callers. Acceptance: (1) storage API can write/read semantic asset manifest deterministically; (2) verification fails closed on missing artifact or digest mismatch and reports structured reasons; (3) prior-review lookup callers can consume verification status to drive truthful lexical-only degraded reporting without claiming semantic parity. Validation contract: cargo test -p roger-storage --test semantic_asset_packaging_smoke; cargo test -p roger-storage --test prior_review_lookup_smoke; cargo test -p roger-storage
notes: Validation actually run: 1) rch exec -- cargo fmt -p roger-storage 2) rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test semantic_asset_packaging_smoke 3) rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test prior_review_lookup_smoke 4) rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage 5) tmpdir=$(mktemp -d); cp tests/suites/int_search_semantic_asset_packaging.toml "$tmpdir/"; cp tests/suites/int_search_prior_review_lookup.toml "$tmpdir/"; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr "$tmpdir"
close_reason: Completed semantic asset manifest install/read/verify hooks with fail-closed missing-path and digest-mismatch behavior, wired lookup degraded-mode gating to manifest verification, and added suite metadata. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo fmt -p roger-storage; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test semantic_asset_packaging_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test prior_review_lookup_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage; tmpdir=/var/folders/d7/lj1cjbfs2gjb0gff7n541pf00000gn/T/tmp.ueo3eYgOdY; cp tests/suites/int_search_semantic_asset_packaging.toml "/"; cp tests/suites/int_search_prior_review_lookup.toml "/"; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr ""

title: Implement Caching for Git Information Retrieval
description: Implement a simple caching mechanism for git commands (e.g., infer_repository_from_git, infer_git_branch) within roger-cli to improve CLI responsiveness. This is a first step towards optimizing git interactions.
notes:
Validation 2026-04-01:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli cached_per_repo_path
Result: pass (tests::repository_lookup_is_cached_per_repo_path + tests::branch_lookup_is_cached_per_repo_path).

Outcome:
- Existing git lookup cache implementation is present in packages/cli/src/lib.rs and now validates cleanly; prior compile blocker note is no longer applicable in current workspace state.
close_reason: Revalidated cache contract: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli cached_per_repo_path (2 cache tests passed).

title: Implement the QMD-inspired retrieval lift and prior-review performance follow-on
description:
Represent the accepted retrieval follow-on that lives beyond the full Round 06 search-truth wave. This epic covers three intentionally large, cohesive slices: the SearchPlan or retrieval-objective control-plane lift, the candidate-audit or bounded semantic-hydration retrieval-behavior lift, and the explicit prior_review_lookup hot-path optimization once the new retrieval contract is already truthful.

The purpose of this epic is to keep the QMD-inspired uplift and optimization ideas from remaining stranded in plan prose while also preventing them from cutting across unfinished Round 06 truth work. It exists to say clearly: first make search truth canonical, then add the retrieval lift on top of that truth.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The graph contains explicit, agent-meaningful leaves for retrieval control-plane lift, retrieval-behavior lift, and hot-path optimization rather than one vague 'improve search' bucket or a spray of micro-beads.
2. The epic makes it explicit that QMD and optimization follow-ons build on canonical planner intent, RecallEnvelope truth, degraded retrieval semantics, session baseline truth, and candidate-versus-promoted memory boundaries.
3. Search-retrieval uplift concepts from the QMD and optimization docs no longer remain stranded only in plan prose, and their boundaries are clear enough that one agent can own one large cohesive slice at a time.
notes:
Primary sources: QMD_RETRIEVAL_LIFT_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md, EXTREME_OPTIMIZATION_ROUND_01_DB_QUERY_AND_FETCH_PATH_ARTEFACT.md, SEARCH_MEMORY_LIFECYCLE_AND_SEMANTIC_ASSET_POLICY.md, and the Round 06 search-planner beads. The split here is intentional: control-plane truth, retrieval-behavior lift, and hot-path performance work are separate large slices, not accidental oversplitting.

Execution posture: this epic should give agents room to pursue excellence through measurement. Use profiling, before-or-after ordered-id comparisons, degraded-mode checks, and perf-harness evidence to choose the right implementation, but do not let optimization work smuggle in contract changes that belong to the Round 06 truth beads.

title: Repair Codex pane detection for NTM swarm control surfaces
description: A live `ntm spawn roger-reviewer --label ops6 --cod=6 --no-user --auto-restart --stagger-mode=smart` session launched six working Codex panes in `/home/ubuntu/dev/roger-reviewer`, but `ntm status` classified all six as `user` panes, `scripts/swarm/broadcast_marching_orders.sh` skipped them because pane titles did not match the expected `__cod_` pattern, and `ntm assign --watch` reported no idle agents. This regression weakens the direct NTM operator loop even though the panes themselves are live.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A fresh `ntm spawn ... --cod=...` session identifies Codex panes as Codex agents in `ntm status`, `ntm activity`, and related operator surfaces instead of classifying them as generic user panes.
2. `scripts/swarm/broadcast_marching_orders.sh` or an equivalent repo-local rebroadcast path can target live Codex panes without relying on stale pane-title assumptions.
3. `ntm assign --watch` can see idle Codex agents in the spawned session and either assign ready beads or truthfully report exhaustion for the same live session.
4. Runbooks or prompts are updated only where behavior actually changed; do not paper over broken detection with docs alone.
notes: Observed on 2026-04-15 during live session `roger-reviewer--ops6` in `/home/ubuntu/dev/roger-reviewer`. Validation should include one bounded live NTM session plus exact command output from `ntm status`, `ntm activity`, and the rebroadcast or assign path after the fix.
close_reason: Closeout 2026-04-15: updated scripts/swarm/broadcast_marching_orders.sh to discover target panes from 'ntm status --json' with a tmux current-command/title fallback instead of relying on stale pane-title prefixes alone, and added scripts/swarm/test_broadcast_marching_orders.sh to cover both the NTM JSON path and the tmux fallback path. Validation run: bash -n scripts/swarm/broadcast_marching_orders.sh && bash -n scripts/swarm/test_broadcast_marching_orders.sh; bash scripts/swarm/test_broadcast_marching_orders.sh; ntm version -> 1.13.0; fresh live session 'roger-reviewer--v51ucheck' via 'ntm spawn roger-reviewer --label v51ucheck --cod=2 --no-user --auto-restart --no-cass-context --no-recovery --ready-timeout 2m' showed Codex panes in both 'ntm status roger-reviewer--v51ucheck' and 'ntm activity roger-reviewer--v51ucheck' and the updated './scripts/swarm/broadcast_marching_orders.sh --session roger-reviewer--v51ucheck --prompt-file <tmp> --delay-seconds 0' sent prompts to panes 0 and 1 without depending on title matching. Fresh live session 'roger-reviewer--v51uassign' via 'ntm spawn roger-reviewer --label v51uassign --cod=1 --no-user --auto-restart --no-cass-context --no-recovery --ready-timeout 2m' showed '1 total, 1 available' in 'ntm activity roger-reviewer--v51uassign'; 'ntm assign roger-reviewer--v51uassign --agent=codex --dry-run --watch --stop-when-done --watch-interval=1s --limit=1' saw the idle Codex pane and mapped rr-x51h.3.2 to pane 0, but this external NTM build still executed the initial assignment pass despite --dry-run, so the temporary watch process/session was cleaned up and that caveat remains outside the repo-local rebroadcast fix scope.

title: Add candidate-audit retrieval lift and bounded semantic-hydration behavior on top of SearchPlan
description:
Add the first real retrieval-behavior lift after SearchPlan and the canonical memory-boundary work are in place. This bead owns candidate-audit retrieval behavior, richer lexical-plus-semantic evidence composition, bounded semantic hydration over Roger's curated semantic corpus, and the explainability needed for those richer results to stay within Roger's authority and trust model.

This bead is not a storage micro-optimization and it is not a memory-promotion policy bead. It is the product-facing retrieval lift that makes candidate_audit and richer semantic evidence useful without letting tentative or contradicted memory quietly gain promoted authority.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Roger gains deliberate candidate-audit and bounded semantic-hydration retrieval behavior compatible with SearchPlan and the canonical RecallEnvelope contract.
2. Lexical and semantic evidence can be fused more richly while still preserving explicit buckets and boundaries for promoted memory, tentative candidates, and evidence hits.
3. Retrieval-quality improvements do not silently widen memory authority, promotion semantics, degraded-mode truth, or cross-scope access.
4. Validation contract: integration coverage over candidate-audit and semantic-ready versus degraded semantic-hydration behavior, plus proof that promotion or surfaced-memory authority remains unchanged without its own bead.
notes:
Primary sources: QMD_RETRIEVAL_LIFT_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md and SEARCH_MEMORY_LIFECYCLE_AND_SEMANTIC_ASSET_POLICY.md. This leaf is intentionally large and cohesive: it owns the retrieval-behavior lift, while the hot-path storage optimization remains a separate performance bead.

Execution answer: prefer the best measured retrieval behavior, not the most local implementation. Agents should use differential tests, evidence-bucket inspection, and degraded semantic checks to choose richer candidate-audit and semantic-hydration behavior, while preserving explicit memory-lane truth and refusing any silent authority widening.

title: Add supported-browser smoke coverage for PR-page launch on Chrome Brave and Edge
description:

This should not automatically become a second heavyweight E2E; it should start as targeted supported-browser smoke around the serious bridge path and truthful launch-only fallback.

Acceptance:
- add supported-browser smoke coverage for PR-page Roger launch wiring on Chrome, Brave, and Edge
- verify Native Messaging happy path where available and truthful custom-URL/launch-only fallback where appropriate
- capture failure artifacts/transcripts per the validation harness contract
- keep coverage within the one-blessed-E2E budget unless the E2E budget is explicitly revised
notes: Graph clarification 2026-04-07: rr-v6x is the implementation lane for executable supported-browser smoke coverage. The separate usage-proof scenario bead is rr-0qvg.6, which should not close this parent by prose or metadata alone.
close_reason:
Closed parent implementation lane after landing both required child slices:
- rr-v6x.1 (already closed): lower-layer bridge launch-path integration coverage for native-messaging preference, launch-only fallback, and artifact expectation helpers.
- rr-v6x.2 (closed in this cycle): executable per-browser smoke runners behind existing suite ids via packages/bridge/tests/smoke_browser_launch_chrome.rs, smoke_browser_launch_brave.rs, smoke_browser_launch_edge.rs, with shared assertions in packages/bridge/tests/support/browser_smoke.rs.

Acceptance proof:
1) Supported-browser smoke coverage now has real executable runner entrypoints for Chrome/Brave/Edge, not metadata only.
2) Native Messaging and truthful custom-url launch-only fallback behavior are exercised by runner assertions using choose_launch_path + handle_bridge_intent.
3) Transcript/envelope capture and failure-artifact contract is asserted through required_launch_artifacts plus roger-validation failure_artifact_paths for each suite id.
4) E2E budget guard remains within one blessed heavyweight E2E (observed_heavyweight_e2e_count=1).

title: Round 06: Truthful provider parity, worker boundary, and Copilot admission wave
description:
Comprehensive bead wave for the next implementation phase shaped by the provider-parity side-plan and the Round 06 reconciliation brief. This wave exists because the live graph still does not represent the full program needed to make Roger truthful about provider support, lifecycle continuity, worker boundaries, outbound posting, bootstrap/doctor surfaces, and Copilot admission.

The wave is intentionally organized around proof groups rather than package names. Closed baseline beads remain anchor prerequisites; this wave adds the missing containers needed to implement the stricter post-Round-05 and provider-parity truth rules without reopening the old spine as if it were still sufficient.
acceptance_criteria: The graph contains explicit group epics for worker boundary, build/help truth, transactional lifecycle, bootstrap/doctor product surface including rr extension uninstall, outbound flow completion, provider re-audit, Copilot admission, provider proof hardening, search-planner truth, and documentation/support-claim closeout; each group has child beads with concrete ownership, proof expectations, and dependencies instead of vague package-level placeholders
notes:
Primary source packet for this wave:
- AGENTS.md
- docs/PLAN_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md
- docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md
- docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md
- docs/BEAD_CREATION_INPUTS.md
- relevant support contracts for worker/runtime, config/bootstrap, search, release/update, testing, and validation

Important shaping rules:
- closed baseline provider/lifecycle beads are anchors, not reopened parents
- docs/support-claim reconciliation is explicit work, not implicit cleanup
- rr-1pz7 is repaired and folded under the outbound-flow epic instead of standing in for the whole lane

title: Round 06 G0: Review-worker runtime and tool boundary
description: Create the first-class review-worker boundary that Round 06 says is missing. This group owns the semantic split between Roger as manager of review sessions and the worker that performs staged review work inside provider sessions. It should stop worker behavior from being implied by prompt glue or smuggled into human CLI commands.
acceptance_criteria: The graph explicitly covers worker objects, worker read/proposal operations, dedicated rr agent transport, and transport-level negative paths; future provider work can depend on this group instead of inventing its own worker semantics
notes:
Primary sources:
- docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md (worker-boundary portions)
- docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md (G0)
- docs/REVIEW_WORKER_RUNTIME_AND_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT.md
- docs/AGENT_ACCESS_AND_IN_SESSION_OPERATION_CONTRACT.md

This group is the canonical bead conversion for the in-session agent-access contract: worker object model, worker read/proposal boundary, rr agent transport, and audit lineage all live here so the operator-versus-agent boundary is not left as support-doc prose.

Execution posture: give implementation agents substantial freedom on internal transport shape, storage representation, module boundaries, and envelope plumbing as long as the Roger-owned contract stays literal. The leaf beads in this group should close on boundary truth, advisory-only mutation posture, audit lineage, and validation proof rather than on reproducing one imagined crate split or prompt flow.

title: Round 06 G0.1: Define canonical review-worker object model and result envelopes
description:
Define the full Roger-owned worker object model that every review-worker transport and provider integration must use: ReviewTask, WorkerContextPacket, WorkerCapabilityProfile, WorkerInvocation, WorkerToolCallEvent, and WorkerStageResult. This bead is not just a schema naming pass. It is the contract slice that replaces prompt glue, provider-specific result shapes, and human-CLI-shaped return paths with one canonical task/execution/result model owned by Roger.

The model must carry task identity, session or run binding, task strategy, allowed scopes, nonce or attempt binding, raw event lineage, structured findings-pack return, clarification requests, memory-review requests, and follow-up proposals strongly enough that future rr agent transport, provider adapters, and proof lanes can all round-trip one task without inventing side semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Canonical Roger-owned shapes exist for ReviewTask, WorkerContextPacket, WorkerCapabilityProfile, WorkerInvocation, WorkerToolCallEvent, and WorkerStageResult, with field ownership and lifecycle semantics defined clearly enough for storage, transport, and validation work.
2. The default review path is expressible as one ReviewTask, one or more WorkerInvocation turns, and one terminal WorkerStageResult without hidden prompt glue or provider-owned state.
3. WorkerStageResult can carry structured findings packs, clarification requests, memory-review requests, and follow-up proposals while remaining advisory rather than mutating canonical finding, memory, approval, or posting state directly.
4. Validation contract: unit or schema round-trip coverage plus integration contract checks for session or task binding, nonce or stale-submission rejection, and result-envelope compatibility with later rr agent transport work.
notes:
Primary sources: REVIEW_WORKER_RUNTIME_AND_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT.md sections on ReviewTask, WorkerContextPacket, WorkerCapabilityProfile, WorkerInvocation, WorkerToolCallEvent, and WorkerStageResult, plus the canonical plan's Contract 1A worker boundary. This bead should be large enough to settle the core object model for the whole worker lane, not just rename structs.

Execution answer: default to truthful whole-system function over provisional slice purity. If the cleanest object model requires touching adjacent transport, storage, validation, or status projection code in the same slice, that is in bounds. What matters is that Roger owns the contract literally and that later worker, continuity, and audit beads inherit a coherent model instead of a locally neat but globally awkward one.
close_reason: Implemented canonical review-worker object model and result-envelope path in app-core/prompt-engine, then aligned plan/support contracts to the live schema: WorkerStageResult now explicitly carries advisory clarification requests, memory-review requests, and follow-up proposals; worker invocation binding and stale task nonce rejection are enforced and covered. Validation: br sync --status; cargo test -p roger-app-core --test review_worker_contract_smoke; cargo test -p roger-prompt-engine --test stage_execution_smoke.

title: Round 06 G0.2: Define worker read/proposal operations and review-mode policy boundary
description:
Define the complete manager-owned worker API and review-mode policy boundary on top of the canonical worker object model. This bead owns the required read operations such as worker.search_memory, worker.list_findings, worker.get_finding_detail, worker.get_artifact_excerpt, and worker.get_status, plus the bounded write-back or proposal operations such as worker.submit_stage_result, worker.request_clarification, memory-review handoff, and follow-up proposal submission.

The key job is to make the read-versus-proposal boundary explicit enough that workers can do meaningful review work without ever being mistaken for operators or for Roger's approval and posting authority. This is where Roger decides what a worker is allowed to know, what it is allowed to propose, and what it is never allowed to mutate directly.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The worker API defines named read operations and bounded proposal or write-back operations with clear input and output envelopes, allowed scopes, and fail-closed behavior for out-of-policy requests.
2. worker.search_memory, finding access, artifact excerpts, and manager-owned status reads all consume Roger-owned contracts rather than provider-specific semantics or broad repo access.
3. Proposal operations remain advisory: workers cannot mutate canonical findings directly, promote memory directly, approve or post GitHub output, or bypass Roger's review-mode safety posture.
4. Validation contract: integration and negative-path coverage for policy denial, scope enforcement, and proposal-versus-operator-boundary behavior across the worker transport seam.
notes: Primary sources: docs/REVIEW_WORKER_RUNTIME_AND_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT.md sections on required read operations, required proposal operations, and the rr agent versus --robot boundary; docs/AGENT_ACCESS_AND_IN_SESSION_OPERATION_CONTRACT.md for the operator-versus-agent access model, in-session command subset, and fail-closed posture when a harness cannot support Roger-safe in-session affordances. This bead is intentionally large: it should settle the meaningful worker API and policy boundary, not just add one or two commands.
close_reason: Defined the manager-owned worker API boundary in app-core: named read and advisory proposal operations, request/response envelopes, Roger-owned read payload contracts for search/findings/artifact/status, and fail-closed authorization across task binding, allowed operations, scopes, and capability posture. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-app-core --test review_worker_contract_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-prompt-engine --test stage_execution_smoke.

title: Round 06 G0.3: Implement dedicated rr agent transport separate from --robot
description: Implement the dedicated rr agent transport for worker sessions so Roger has one explicit machine-facing execution surface for staged review work. This bead owns transport-neutral invocation, session scoping, and result submission mechanics; it does not repurpose --robot or human help surfaces for worker execution.
acceptance_criteria: rr agent exists as the dedicated worker transport; worker invocations are scoped to the intended Roger session/review task; results return through Roger-owned envelopes rather than ad hoc provider text or recycled --robot schemas
notes:
Primary proof lane: integration plus contract tests. This bead depends on the settled worker objects and worker policy boundary rather than redefining them.

Implemented on 2026-04-16:
- `rr agent` now acts as the dedicated worker transport separate from `--robot` and returns `rr.agent.response.v1` envelopes over the canonical worker operation response payload.
- CLI requests are bound to the intended `ReviewTask` / session tuple and fail closed on mismatched request bindings.
- Worker context, status, search, finding detail, artifact excerpt, clarification/memory-review/follow-up proposals, and stage-result submission are routed through the canonical app-core transport instead of a recycled robot surface.

Automated validation added/updated:
- `packages/app-core/tests/agent_transport_smoke.rs`
- `packages/cli/tests/agent_transport_smoke.rs`

Validation run:
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test agent_transport_smoke -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test agent_transport_smoke -- --nocapture`

Scope note:
- broader worker transport rejection, continuity, and schema acceptance coverage remains in `rr-x51h.1.4`.
close_reason: Implemented the dedicated rr agent transport with canonical Roger-owned envelopes plus new app-core and CLI smoke tests covering bound session/task validation, dedicated transport separation from --robot, and stage-result submission.

title: Round 06 G0.4: Add worker transport rejection, continuity, and schema acceptance
description: Add the negative-path and acceptance coverage for the rr agent transport: stale or cross-session submissions, bounded context enforcement, schema rejection, and no hidden approval/post behavior. This bead is the proof container that stops the worker boundary from becoming another under-tested adapter seam.
acceptance_criteria: Worker transport acceptance proves that stale or mismatched submissions fail closed, result envelopes validate, bounded context is enforced, and workers cannot escalate into approval or posting by accident
notes:
Primary proof lane: integration / negative-path validation. Link later to provider proof hardening instead of hiding all of this inside a generic validation epic.

Implemented on 2026-04-16:
- added explicit `rr agent` acceptance tests for invalid request schema rejection, transport-kind mismatch rejection, cross-session context-packet rejection, and denial of fake posting operations.
- extended CLI smoke coverage so bound-session mismatch and hidden posting-operation attempts fail closed through Roger-owned denial/error envelopes.
- preserved the dedicated `rr agent` transport boundary without widening into approval or posting flows.

Automated validation added/updated:
- `packages/app-core/tests/agent_transport_smoke.rs`
- `packages/cli/tests/agent_transport_smoke.rs`

Validation run:
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-app-core --test agent_transport_smoke -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test agent_transport_smoke -- --nocapture`

Scope note:
- persistence and audit-trail follow-on remains in `rr-bfae.4`; this bead only closes the transport acceptance/rejection proof boundary.
close_reason: Added negative-path rr agent acceptance proof for schema rejection, bound-context enforcement, and hidden posting-operation denial with new app-core and CLI smoke coverage.

title: Round 06 G0.6: Guard expanded rr agent operation surface and support claims
description: The dedicated rr agent transport is already shipped, but the live surface now claims a broader set of bound read/proposal operations than the original transport bead validated. This bead owns the missing proof slice: keep rr agent operation coverage and cross-surface support claims truthful as the surface expands.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Automated CLI integration coverage proves the live rr agent bound read/proposal surface for status, search, finding, artifact, and advisory operations, plus capability-based denial behavior. 2. Help, robot-docs, and in-session skill docs cannot drift from the live rr agent operation list, dedicated transport boundary, or rr.agent.response.v1 envelope. 3. Validation contract: targeted integration/contract tests at the cheapest truthful layer, recording the exact commands run in closeout.
notes: Validation contract: integration and contract tests. Primary proof commands: cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke rr_agent_ ; cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test provider_surface_truth_guard. This bead exists because rr-x51h.1.3 closed on narrower transport proof while the live support claim expanded.
close_reason: Added missing proof for the expanded rr agent surface without reopening the narrower shipped transport bead. packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs now covers bound status/search/finding/artifact/advisory operations plus capability-based denial behavior, packages/cli/tests/provider_surface_truth_guard.rs now guards rr agent help/robot-docs/schema drift and docs/skills/ROGER_INSIDE_ROGER_AGENT.md is aligned to the dedicated transport being live but narrower in-session. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke rr_agent_ ; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test provider_surface_truth_guard.

title: Round 06 GZ.1: Collapse parallel duplicate Round 06 shell families into one canonical execution tree
description: Multiple agents seeded overlapping Round 06 shell families (for example rr-x51h, rr-b58q, rr-4n7l, and related late duplicate group epics) while converting the provider-parity and Round 06 plans into beads. This bead owns the final graph hygiene pass that retires duplicate shells, re-parents any unique surviving leaves, and leaves one canonical execution tree for agents to follow.
acceptance_criteria: All duplicate Round 06 shell epics are either closed as superseded or re-scoped explicitly; surviving unique leaves are parented into the canonical Round 06 execution tree; execution agents no longer have to choose between overlapping top-level Round 06 parents.
notes: This is a graph-hygiene bead created because the bead wave was shaped in parallel by multiple agents through Agent Mail coordination.
close_reason: Closed after reparenting retained unique leaves and retiring duplicate Round 06 shell families into the canonical rr-x51h tree.

title: Round 06 G9: Documentation, support-claim, and authority closeout
description: Close the Round 06 loop by merging accepted truth back into canonical docs, narrowing or archiving side-plan drift, and reconciling public/operator support surfaces to the actual bead graph and shipped command surface. This epic exists because Round 06 changes support claims across providers, lifecycle, outbound flow, bootstrap, and search, and those claims must be reflected in Roger authority-bearing docs rather than left stranded in side plans or duplicate bead shells.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The graph contains explicit docs/support-claim beads for canonical-plan plus support-contract merge and for README/AGENTS/onboarding/review-flow/validation truth reconciliation.
2. Side-plan authority drift is removed, narrowed, or explicitly demoted once accepted Round 06 truth lands in canonical docs.
3. Closeout validation is a doc/help/graph authority audit rather than implicit cleanup.

title: Round 06 G1: Build/help truth and provider-surface truth
description: Restore a truthful product-entry and help surface before widening any provider claims. This group owns the broken from-source rr help/build path, then reconciles router/help/docs/robot/provider exposure so the shipped command surface tells one honest story.
acceptance_criteria: The graph contains one bead for the build/help entry break, one for provider-surface reconciliation, and one for an automated drift guard so provider claims cannot keep outrunning the live CLI surface
notes:
Primary sources:
- docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md (G1)
- docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md (D3A / W4 / W7)
- docs/ROBOT_CLI_CONTRACT.md

title: Round 06 G1.1: Restore truthful from-source rr help and build entry path
description: Restore the from-source Roger product-entry path so a clean workspace can build and interrogate `rr` again. This bead owns the exact failure where the workspace cannot even render `rr --help` from source, and it should treat that as product-surface debt because Roger still claims a truthful local Cargo path.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `cargo run -q -p roger-cli --bin rr -- --help` succeeds from a clean workspace and renders Roger usage/help instead of failing on manifest or entry-path breakage.
2. `cargo run -q -p roger-cli --bin rr -- help` or the canonical equivalent source-run help path also succeeds; if the canonical entry path changes, the same slice updates docs/help references so operators still have one truthful from-source command path.
3. If the repair touches manifest/build wiring rather than help dispatch alone, `cargo build -p roger-cli --bin rr` also succeeds and closeout records which exact source-run/build command path now proves the surface.
notes:
Primary proof lane: build/integration. Round 05 and Round 06 both call out the broken source-run path (`cargo run -q -p roger-cli --bin rr -- --help`) as product-surface truth debt, not mere developer inconvenience.

Execution answer: treat this as a truthful-entry restoration bead, not as a Cargo-manifest-only bead. If restoring from-source `rr` help/build requires touching packaging, command wiring, contract exports, or supporting docs in the same slice so operators can actually inspect the shipped surface honestly, that is preferred to a narrower fix that still leaves the product hard to interrogate.

Validation contract: exact closeout proof must name the source-run help command that now works. If packaging/build wiring changed, include `cargo build -p roger-cli --bin rr` as companion proof. If docs/help/robot discovery text changes in the same slice, include the exact companion probe used to show the surfaced command path stayed truthful.
close_reason: Closeout 2026-04-15: from-source rr entry/help path already works on the live workspace. Verified exact acceptance commands: cargo run -q -p roger-cli --bin rr -- --help (pass), cargo run -q -p roger-cli --bin rr -- help (pass), cargo build -p roger-cli --bin rr (pass). Regression coverage already exists in packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs::help_forms_exit_cleanly_for_quickstart_probe and packages/cli/tests/process_id_collision_smoke.rs, which builds the rr binary from source via cargo build -p roger-cli --bin rr. No code change required in this slice; the bead remained open after the source-run break was repaired earlier.

title: Round 06 G1.2: Reconcile provider exposure across router, help, robot envelopes, and docs
description: Reconcile the live provider surface so router logic, human help text, robot-facing output, canonical docs, and release/test matrices all tell one truthful story about provider exposure and hierarchy. This bead owns the gap where code, help, and docs still disagree about opencode, codex, gemini, claude, and copilot.
acceptance_criteria: Provider exposure is literal and aligned across router logic, help text, robot output, canonical docs, README, onboarding, and the release/test matrix; no surface claims reopen/dropout/return or provider parity without the matching proof
notes: Primary proof lane: integration plus docs/support-claim review. This is a prerequisite for later provider re-audit and Copilot admission wording.
close_reason: Closeout 2026-04-15: provider exposure is now aligned across live router/help output, robot discovery, and public docs. README now states the actual live rr review provider surface (opencode/codex/gemini/claude) and keeps Copilot explicit as planned-not-live. docs/ROBOT_CLI_CONTRACT.md now records that rr robot-docs guide/commands should surface provider-support inventory fields for automation. packages/cli/src/lib.rs and packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs now expose and verify supported_providers / planned_not_live_providers / not_supported_providers on rr robot-docs commands and add richer live provider support metadata (display_name, notes) to blocked review envelopes and guide output. Audited release/test matrix and onboarding surfaces in the same slice; no provider-claim drift there required edits. Validation: cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke (pass); cargo run -q -p roger-cli --bin rr -- --help (pass; help text lists live vs planned provider posture); cargo run -q -p roger-cli --bin rr -- robot-docs commands --robot (pass; review dry-run entry exposes provider arrays); cargo run -q -p roger-cli --bin rr -- review --pr 42 --provider copilot --robot (blocked as expected with live provider matrix).

title: Round 06 G1.3: Add automated guard against provider/help/support-claim drift
description: Add a lightweight automated guard that fails when provider exposure or support wording drifts between router/help/robot/docs/matrix surfaces. This bead exists so the repo stops reintroducing the exact drift Round 06 called out.
acceptance_criteria: One named guard or suite can detect when provider lists or claim levels diverge across the product-entry/help/docs surfaces; future support widening requires updating all linked truth surfaces in one slice
notes: Primary proof lane: integration/validation guard. Anchor previous machine-surface work: rr-011.9 and rr-005.4.
close_reason: Closeout 2026-04-15: added dedicated integration guard in packages/cli/tests/provider_surface_truth_guard.rs so provider-support changes must update the live help text, blocked review router payload, robot-docs guide and commands payloads, README.md, docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, and docs/ROBOT_CLI_CONTRACT.md in one slice. Added tests/suites/int_cli_provider_surface_truth.toml so the guard is discoverable in validation planning. Validation run: cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test provider_surface_truth_guard; cargo run -q -p roger-validation -- plan pr tests/suites.

comments.author: cdilga
comments.text: Reopened: Reopening to repair incorrect closeout text and validation evidence recorded in the bead audit trail.

title: Round 06 G2: Transactional lifecycle and bridge truth
description: Make lifecycle truth and bridge truth explicit proof containers. This group owns launch-attempt state, transactional review/resume/refresh/return persistence, real bridge dispatch to rr --robot, and stale-event rejection so Roger stops claiming launched sessions before provider binding is real.
acceptance_criteria: The graph contains separate leaf beads for launch-attempt truth, transactional lifecycle commits, real bridge dispatch, and stale-event / retry-safe failure evidence; none of those concerns are hidden inside Copilot or provider-specific beads
notes:
Primary sources:
- docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md (W1)
