- docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md (G2)
- docs/PLAN_FOR_TRANSACTIONAL_LAUNCH_LIFECYCLE_AND_BRIDGE_TRUTH.md
- docs/HARNESS_SESSION_LINKAGE_CONTRACT.md

This group is the canonical bead conversion for the transactional lifecycle side-plan: launch-attempt truth, transactional review/resume/return plus automatic reconciliation commits, bridge truth, and retry-safe stale-event handling all live here rather than in provider-specific leaves.

Execution posture: give agents freedom to choose the concrete ledger schema, transaction boundaries, retry machinery, and bridge dispatch implementation as long as the invariants hold. These leaves should optimize for crash safety, anti-ghost-state behavior, and truthful surfaced continuity rather than for preserving any one assumed persistence or eventing design.

title: Round 06 G2.1: Implement launch-attempt ledger and verified session-finalization rule
description: Introduce a distinct launch-attempt lifecycle with pending, verified, failed, and abandoned outcomes, and enforce the rule that Roger does not finalize a durable review session until a verified provider session identifier exists.
acceptance_criteria: Launch attempts are represented separately from completed review sessions; Roger cannot emit a launched/started success state before real provider binding succeeds; partial failures stay in attempt history instead of looking like completed sessions
notes:
Primary proof lane: integration plus crash/partial-failure tests. Closed anchors: rr-003.6, rr-003.7, rr-018.1, rr-014.

Execution answer: optimize for truthful lifecycle behavior across the whole launch path, not for a narrow ledger-only diff. If clean launch-attempt truth requires changes in session binding, bridge events, attention/state projection, or retry classification, those adjacent changes are in scope. A smaller patch that preserves ghost-state or ambiguous success is the worse outcome.
close_reason: Added durable launch_attempt ledger, review-launch transactional finalization after verified provider session IDs, and rollback/failure coverage in storage and CLI tests.

title: Round 06 G2.2: Make review/resume/return and automatic reconciliation commits transactional and retry-safe
description: Wrap review, resume, return, and automatic reconciliation persistence in truthful per-event transactions so session binding, run creation, continuity updates, invalidation effects, and attention-state changes commit atomically or fail closed together. This bead also owns the retirement of standalone manual refresh as a supported operator verb: stale-but-bounded state should be reconciled opportunistically and asynchronously on truthful Roger surfaces rather than through a separate command or extension action.
acceptance_criteria: Lifecycle events are transactionally safe; retries after partial failure do not create completed-looking ghost state; automatic reconciliation and user-visible continuity or attention updates reflect one committed event rather than piecemeal writes. No standalone manual `rr refresh` command or `refresh_review` extension action remains in supported Roger surfaces once this slice closes; re-entry, status, findings, and bridge surfaces reconcile bounded stale state automatically and fail closed with repair guidance when that is not safe.
notes:
2026-04-15: transactional review/resume/return launch finalization is now implemented and defended by integration coverage (
running 7 tests
test existing_session_launch_attempt_finalize_updates_session_state_atomically ... ok
test failed_review_launch_attempt_finalize_rolls_back_partial_session_writes ... ok
test same_session_writes_fail_closed_on_row_version_mismatch ... ok
test review_launch_attempt_finalize_is_atomic_and_records_verified_binding ... ok
test launch_binding_resolution_fails_closed_for_ambiguous_and_mismatched_state ... ok
test failed_existing_session_launch_finalize_rolls_back_partial_updates ... ok
test storage_smoke_persists_resume_and_approval_state_across_restart ... ok

test result: ok. 7 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.23s, 
running 2 tests
test return_records_verified_launch_attempt_and_single_committed_update ... ok
test resume_reseed_records_retry_safe_launch_attempt_and_updates_locator ... ok

test result: ok. 2 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.58s, plus targeted bridge/extension refresh-retirement checks). rr-x51h.3.2 is still not closeable because status/findings/robot surfaces continue to advertise  reconciliation without yet committing a bounded reconciliation event. Remaining truthful gap is being tracked in child bead CREATED_ID_PENDING; do not close parent until that child lands or the support claim is narrowed truthfully.

title: Round 06 G2.3: Replace bridge pretend-success with real rr --robot dispatch
description: Make the bridge call the real rr command surface in robot mode, parse the returned machine-readable payload, and only report success with canonical Roger session identifiers after the underlying command succeeds.
acceptance_criteria: Bridge launches return real Roger session IDs and canonical status payloads; no bridge path can claim success with missing session identity or stubbed dispatch behavior
notes: Implemented real bridge dispatch in packages/bridge/src/lib.rs: start_review/resume_review/show_findings now invoke rr in --robot JSON mode, require data.session_id from the command payload, and read back rr status --session <id> --robot before returning success. Main-worktree validation was partially blocked by unrelated dirty packages/cli/src/lib.rs agent work (CommandKind::Agent => handle_agent(parsed, runtime) without a local handle_agent), so direct rr-binary proof was run in a clean temporary worktree at HEAD plus this bead patch. Validation run: cargo fmt --all; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-bridge (main workspace); node --test apps/extension/src/background.launch.test.js (main workspace); in clean worktree, rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test bridge_robot_dispatch_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke rr_binary_native_host_path_returns_bridge_response_for_launch_intents -- --exact; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke rr_binary_native_host_path_handles_all_primary_launch_actions_without_hanging -- --exact; node --test apps/extension/src/background.launch.test.js.
close_reason: Bridge now dispatches to real rr --robot commands, requires canonical session identity, and returns bounded status readback; remaining generated-contract drift moved to rr-x51h.3.5.

title: Round 06 G2.4: Reject stale launch events and preserve retry failure evidence
description: Handle stale hook or bridge events, stale locator bindings, mismatched target/worktree bindings, and retries after partial failure so the verified lifecycle model stays truthful under churn.
acceptance_criteria: Stale or mismatched lifecycle events fail closed; retries cannot bind the wrong provider or Roger session; preserved failure evidence makes partial launches inspectable instead of ghostly.
notes: Proof lane: integration and negative-path recovery. This bead completes the transactional lifecycle group beyond the happy path.

title: Round 06 G2.7: Export bridge status-mirror fields through the generated extension contract
description: Update the Rust-owned bridge contract export so extension-side generated typing stops claiming the pre-status-mirror BridgeResponse shape. This follow-on owns bridge_contract_snapshot plus export/verify-contracts proof after rr-x51h.3.3 added real rr --robot dispatch and bounded status readback fields.
acceptance_criteria: apps/extension/src/generated/bridge.ts and the Rust bridge contract snapshot include the current BridgeResponse status-mirror fields (attention_state, generated_at, and bounded status snapshot metadata). rr bridge export-contracts / rr bridge verify-contracts truthfully detect drift for the new response shape instead of preserving the old launch-only contract. Validation names the exact CLI smoke or contract tests used; do not imply runtime bridge proof beyond the suites actually run.
notes:
Created because rr-x51h.3.3 can land the runtime bridge slice without touching the currently dirty/reserved packages/cli/src/lib.rs, but leaving the generated typing contract stale would hide drift instead of tracking it explicitly.

Follow-on note after rr-g7j6.5: the runtime BridgeResponse shape now also carries bounded failure taxonomy fields (failure_kind, launch_outcome) in addition to the status-mirror fields. Export/verify-contracts proof for this bead must cover the full current BridgeResponse shape, not only attention_state/generated_at/status.
close_reason: Bridge contract export is now truthful to the live Rust bridge envelope: apps/extension/src/generated/bridge.ts and bridge_contract_snapshot() include BridgeStatusSnapshot, BridgeFailureKind, attention_state/generated_at/status, launch_outcome/failure_kind, and the bounded register_extension_identity request fields instead of the older launch-only shape. rr bridge export-contracts and verify-contracts now guard against regression back to the launch-only contract. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --lib tests::bridge_contract_snapshot_tracks_status_mirror_and_failure_taxonomy_fields; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --lib tests::bridge_export_contracts_writes_generated_snapshot; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --lib tests::bridge_verify_contracts; cargo run -q -p roger-cli -- bridge export-contracts; cargo run -q -p roger-cli -- bridge verify-contracts --robot. No broader runtime bridge or browser-launch proof claimed in this closeout.

title: Round 06 G3: Bootstrap and doctor product surface
description: Productize bootstrap and doctor as first-class surfaces. This group owns resolved config/defaulting for provider capability profiles, canonical rr init, provider-aware rr doctor, and the documentation/onboarding reconciliation needed so help and recovery guidance route through real shipped commands only.
acceptance_criteria: The graph contains explicit beads for resolved config/defaulting, rr init, rr doctor, rr extension uninstall, and user-facing guidance reconciliation; bootstrap/doctor are not buried inside a generic CLI grab-bag
notes:
Primary sources:
- docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md (W7.1 / W7.2)
- docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md (G3)
- docs/CONFIGURATION_CAPABILITY_AND_DEFAULTING_CONTRACT.md
- docs/UPDATE_RELEASE_AND_TESTED_UPGRADE_CONTRACT.md

title: Round 06 G3.1: Extend resolved config/defaulting for provider capabilities and policy profiles
description:
Build the resolved-config baseline that later bootstrap, doctor, status, robot, and Copilot surfaces consume. This bead owns the provider capability registry and resolved baseline output, not raw config parsing alone. The resolved baseline must carry provider support tier, visibility or surface class, policy profile, hook and instruction versions, binary or install roots when operator-visible, repair-only overrides, launch or isolation mode, and provenance for every effective value. Routine surfaces such as rr init, rr doctor, rr status, and robot envelopes must consume this resolved view and keep raw config plumbing, ambient env state, and repair-only knobs out of the front door unless the operator explicitly asks for them.

This is the point where Roger decides what the product thinks is true about provider capability and configuration. If this bead stays vague, later bootstrap, doctor, and Copilot lanes will each invent their own partial truth.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Resolved config exposes provider capability metadata, support tier, surface class, policy-profile semantics, binary and asset provenance, and fail-closed or degraded reasons in one stable inspectable shape.
2. Routine CLI, TUI, and robot surfaces consume resolved config and show provider, support tier, isolation mode, and blocked or degraded reason without leaking raw TOML, env, or repair plumbing by default.
3. Repair-only overrides remain available for repair and maintainer flows, but they are demoted and surfaced with provenance whenever they influence preflight or launch decisions.
4. Validation contract: unit coverage for layered resolution and provenance capture, plus integration coverage for init, doctor, and status classification and fail-closed behavior.
notes: Primary sources: CONFIGURATION_CAPABILITY_AND_DEFAULTING_CONTRACT.md sections D1 through D5, the TUI and CLI integration rules, and the bootstrap or doctor boundary in PLAN_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md. This bead should define the canonical resolved provider object used by later init, doctor, status, and Copilot lanes rather than letting each surface infer its own view.

title: Round 06 G4: Explicit outbound flow completion
description: Finish the explicit outbound approval/posting workflow Roger already claims. This group owns the repaired GitHub posting adapter lane, rr draft/approve/post command surface, outbound-state invalidation/retry semantics, and surfaced mutation visibility across status/findings/TUI flows.
acceptance_criteria: The graph no longer treats the posting adapter as the whole feature; instead it covers adapter transport, explicit command surface, invalidation/retry semantics, and surfaced outbound states under one dedicated outbound-flow epic
notes:
Primary sources:
- docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md (W2)
- docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md (G4)
- docs/TUI_WORKSPACE_AND_OPERATOR_FLOW_CONTRACT.md
- docs/CORE_DOMAIN_SCHEMA_AND_FINDING_FINGERPRINT.md

title: Round 06 G4.4: Implement rr post with approval-token verification and exact-payload execution
description:
Add the operator-facing rr post command that consumes only Roger-approved drafts or batches, re-verifies the approval token or equivalent payload binding and immutable target tuple at execution time, routes through the GitHub posting adapter, and records immutable posting lineage. This bead owns the explicit draft -> approve -> post product command, the fail-closed rejection path for stale or mismatched approvals, and the execution envelope returned to operators or robot surfaces.

It must not infer ambient PR context, post unapproved material, or hide partial failure behind a single green success line.
acceptance_criteria:
1. rr post executes only the exact approved payload on the exact bound target; stale, superseded, or mismatched approvals fail closed with an explanatory result.
2. Post-time execution preserves immutable payload identity, approval reference, remote identifiers, per-item result normalization, and retry metadata for partial failures.
3. The surfaced command remains Roger-mediated end to end and cannot bypass the local approval state machine or exact-payload verification.
4. Validation contract: CLI and integration coverage for happy path execution, stale-approval rejection, target-drift rejection, and partial-failure result envelopes.
notes: This is the surfaced product command that sits on top of the rr-1pz7 adapter leaf. Treat it as the command-boundary slice of outbound posting, not as the whole docs, proof, or provider-policy closeout lane.

title: Round 06 G4.5: Add outbound visibility, invalidation, and partial-post recovery proof
description:
Prove and surface the outbound recovery story after draft, approve, and post land. This bead owns the inspectable state transitions and recovery behavior for invalidation on refresh, retarget, or worktree drift, duplicate-post prevention, partial-post retry, and auditable visibility across status, findings, TUI, and recovery surfaces.

The point is that operators should be able to understand exactly what can still post, what was revoked, what already posted, and what can be retried after failure or restart without inspecting storage internals.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Status, findings, TUI, and recovery surfaces project the same outbound state machine after draft, approval, invalidation, full post, and partial post.
2. Invalidation, duplicate-post prevention, and partial-post retry are backed by durable lineage and never masquerade as ready-to-post or fully-posted state.
3. Recovery output explains what changed, why approval was revoked or partially consumed, and what exact retry path remains available.
4. Validation contract: integration and recovery coverage for refresh or retarget invalidation, restart after partial post, duplicate-post guard, and surfaced state-projection truth.
notes: This bead is the implementation-to-proof bridge between the rr draft or approve or post surfaces and the later G7 proof lane. It should consume the partial-post lineage work from rr-ph77.5 rather than re-specifying retry heuristics in parallel.

title: Round 06 G5: OpenCode and bounded-provider claim re-audit
description: Re-audit the existing provider landscape under the stricter newer truth rules. This group owns the OpenCode benchmark re-audit plus explicit narrowing of Codex, Gemini, and Claude Code claims so provider exposure, help text, status surfaces, and docs all match what is actually proven.
acceptance_criteria: The graph contains explicit re-audit/narrowing beads for OpenCode, Codex, Gemini, Claude Code, and one provider-hierarchy/support-wording bead instead of assuming older close reasons still satisfy the newer anti-gap rules
notes:
Primary sources:
- docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md (W3 / W4 / D3A)
- docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md (G5)
- docs/ATTENTION_EVENT_AND_NOTIFICATION_CONTRACT.md

title: Round 06 G5.1: Re-audit OpenCode as the continuity benchmark under the stricter truth rules
description: Re-audit the blessed OpenCode path under launch-truth, transactionality, and continuity-quality rules so older close reasons do not silently stand in for the stricter newer standards.
acceptance_criteria: OpenCode start/reopen/reseed/return behavior is re-proven against the new launch-attempt and transactional rules; continuity quality reflects actual behavior instead of inherited assumptions.

title: Round 06 G5.2: Tighten Codex to honest Tier A live claims and fail-closed behavior
description: Reconcile Codex support to the actual live surface and proof level so Tier A remains useful but literal and deeper continuity or return claims do not leak into user-facing wording.
acceptance_criteria: Codex help/docs/status surfaces tell the literal Tier A story; unsupported deeper continuity or return semantics are not implied; the live CLI/provider router fails closed where deeper support is absent.
notes: Anchors: rr-1br and rr-011.1.

title: Round 06 G5.3: Tighten Gemini to honest live-surface and adapter-contract claims
description: Reconcile Gemini support to the actual live launch surface and adapter behavior so the repo stops oscillating between adapter-contract optimism and user-facing parity claims.
acceptance_criteria: Gemini surfaces say exactly what is live, what is adapter-contract only, and what remains out of scope; parity language disappears unless proven.
notes: Anchors: rr-003.2, rr-23vk, rr-qhb, rr-011.1.

title: Round 06 G5.4: Reconcile Claude Code exposure to truthful bounded claims
description: Reconcile Claude Code exposure across plan/help/router/docs so it is described no more generously than the live product surface deserves.
acceptance_criteria: Claude Code support wording is literal and bounded across help/docs/status/router surfaces; unsupported continuity or worker semantics are not implied by shared provider lists or stale docs.
notes: Proof lane: docs/help/status truth review with machine-surface checks where applicable.
close_reason: Verified Claude Code remains a literal bounded Tier A provider across live surfaces without widening continuity claims. Updated packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs so the bounded provider status/resume/return proof covers Claude and Gemini explicitly, while the existing provider_surface_truth_guard continues to lock help/robot-docs/README/release-matrix wording to the bounded Claude claim. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke bounded_provider_outputs_are_truthful_for_claude_and_gemini ; rch exec -- cargo test -q -p roger-cli --test provider_surface_truth_guard.

title: Round 06 G5.5: Freeze provider hierarchy and support wording across public and operator surfaces
description:
Freeze the authoritative provider hierarchy and claim language after the provider re-audit so every surface uses one order and one set of bounded promises: GitHub Copilot CLI is the authoritative #1 target but not a live claim until verified launch, policy, worker-boundary, and continuity proof exist; OpenCode is the authoritative #2 provider and current strongest landed continuity path; Codex, Gemini, and Claude Code are bounded Tier A providers only unless their deeper proof beads land. This bead does not widen implementation claims. It codifies the hierarchy, tier labels, disclaimers, and exposure rules across README, canonical plan, onboarding, release or test matrix, help, status or doctor wording, and robot-discoverable surfaces.

The point is to stop product hierarchy, implementation order, and live support claims from drifting apart again.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Every public and operator surface uses the same provider order and the same literal claim boundaries, including no Tier B, reopen, or rr return wording for bounded providers and no live Copilot wording before verified launch, policy, and continuity proof exists.
2. OpenCode remains explicitly documented as the current strongest landed continuity path until Copilot proof closes, and GitHub CLI remains outside the review-harness matrix.
3. The slice ties the wording freeze to the automated drift guard and existing proof surfaces so later support widening must update all linked truth surfaces in one slice.
4. Validation: help, robot, and docs snapshot audit plus the named guard from rr-x51h.2.3, with release or test matrix review for any widened claim.
notes: Primary inputs: the provider support matrix in PLAN_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md, RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, rr-x51h.2.2, and the README or onboarding truth rules in AGENTS.md. This is the cross-surface freeze bead for Round 06 provider truth; it should narrow claims aggressively rather than preserve aspirational wording.

title: Round 06 G5.6: Evaluate future provider admissions beyond the current support matrix
description:
The canonical plan leaves one bounded follow-on question open after the current provider wave: once OpenCode, Codex, Gemini, Claude Code, and Copilot have truthful tiering, which later providers or deeper tiers are worth admitting at all. That strategic admission question still needs a bead so it does not disappear behind the current matrix freeze.

This deferred spike owns the post-Round-06 provider-expansion evaluation. It should compare candidate providers and deeper tier claims against Rogers admission rules: deterministic launch truth, continuity quality, worker/prompt safety, auditability, validation cost, and the support wording burden they introduce.

Scope:
- identify which later providers or editor-hosted clients are plausible candidates for Tier A, Tier B, or Tier C after the current matrix stabilizes
- compare the session-control, continuity, tool-gating, and audit surfaces each candidate exposes
- define the minimum proof packet and fail-closed behavior a future provider must satisfy before follow-on implementation beads are created
- record which candidates should stay intentionally out of scope because they cannot satisfy Rogers safety or continuity bar

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The graph contains an explicit deferred spike for future provider admission rather than leaving the question only in canonical-plan prose.
2. The bead defines the admission rubric and evidence thresholds for future providers or deeper tier claims.
3. The bead states plainly that no provider beyond the current matrix is admitted or implied by this spike alone.

Validation contract: architecture-spike memo or support-matrix proposal comparing future provider candidates against Rogers admission rules and proof burden.
notes: Manual user approval required before this deferred spike may be reactivated, split, or implemented. Keep the current direct-path baseline unchanged until the user explicitly approves reopening this spike.

title: Round 06 G5.6.1: Evaluate pi_agent_rust admission as a future Roger harness
description:
Pi-Agent should not be admitted by implication just because `_exploration/pi_agent_rust` exists locally. This deferred spike makes Pi-Agent the first named post-`0.1.0` future-harness candidate and evaluates it against the same Roger admission rubric used for every provider: direct CLI launch truth, Roger-controlled safety/policy posture, deterministic audit capture, continuity-tier honesty, validation cost, and support-wording burden.

The goal is not to widen support now. The goal is to determine whether Pi-Agent deserves a future Tier A path at all, what would have to be disabled or fail closed by default, and whether its session/control model could ever justify Tier B or Tier C later without weakening Rogers local-first review posture.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Produce a concrete Pi-Agent admission memo or support-matrix proposal that compares `_exploration/pi_agent_rust` against Rogers Tier A/Tier B/Tier C contract rather than relying on brand intuition.
2. Define the most conservative plausible first slice if Pi-Agent is admitted later: likely Tier A only, with explicit notes on launch shape, transcript or artifact capture, policy/tool gating, continuity expectations, and what must fail closed.
3. State plainly whether Pi-Agent should remain out of scope, become a deferred Tier A implementation candidate, or justify follow-on research only.
4. No live support claim, provider parsing, CLI exposure, or README widening is implied by this spike alone.
notes: Primary planning inputs: `docs/PLAN_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md`, `docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md`, `docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md`, `docs/REFERENCE_SOURCES_AND_EXPLORATION_TARGETS.md`, and the local reference clone under `_exploration/pi_agent_rust`. Validation contract: architecture-spike memo or provider-admission proposal plus explicit follow-on bead recommendations when warranted.

title: Round 06 G6: Copilot admission program
description: Admit GitHub Copilot CLI through the same truth rules as every other provider. This group owns session-copilot, provider wiring, direct CLI plus hook launch, review-safe policy profile, worktree isolation, Tier A then Tier B continuity, and the explicit docs/onboarding changes needed to describe Copilot honestly.
acceptance_criteria: The graph contains discrete beads for provider wiring, verified launch path, policy/worktree enforcement, Tier A, Tier B, and Copilot-specific operator guidance; Copilot is not represented by one generic provider placeholder
notes:
Primary sources:
- docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md (W5)
- docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md (G6)
- docs/CONFIGURATION_CAPABILITY_AND_DEFAULTING_CONTRACT.md

title: Round 06 G6.1: Create session-copilot crate, provider parsing, config wiring, and feature gate
description: Create the dedicated session-copilot integration entry: workspace member, provider identifier, config/env plumbing, policy-profile wiring, and an explicit feature gate so Copilot enters through the same contract vocabulary as every other provider.
acceptance_criteria: Roger can parse and resolve provider=copilot through a dedicated crate and config path; Copilot remains gated until truthful launch and policy work land; provider admission does not bypass the existing harness/session linkage model.
notes: Anchors: rr-015 and rr-005.3.

title: Round 06 G6.2: Implement Roger-owned direct CLI plus hook launch with verified Copilot session IDs
description: Implement the first real Copilot launch surface using direct CLI invocation plus repository hooks. This bead owns pending launch attempts, interactive launch from the repo/worktree root, waiting for a real session-start hook artifact, and only then finalizing the Roger session binding.
acceptance_criteria: Roger can start a Copilot review session and verify a real Copilot session ID before claiming success; hook events are Roger-readable; launch is rooted in the repo/worktree and does not depend on ACP-first semantics.
notes: This is the core verified start path for Copilot Tier A.

title: Round 06 G6.3: Enforce review-safe Copilot policy profile, worktree isolation, and audit artifacts
description: Implement the default review_readonly Copilot posture: no built-in GitHub MCP by default, no raw gh writes, no write tool, no shell execution by default, no external URL access by default, worktree-root isolation, and Roger-owned audit artifacts for tool denials and transcript references.
acceptance_criteria: Copilot review mode runs under an explicit Roger policy profile with worktree isolation and bounded capabilities; denied actions are auditable; provider-local state is continuity evidence only, not Roger authority.
notes: This bead makes policy profile and worktree identity visible enough for doctor/status and later proof lanes.

title: Round 06 G6.4: Deliver Copilot Tier A with capture, normalization, reseed, and truthful continuity
description: Deliver the first truthful Copilot support level: start a Roger-owned session, bind it to a review target, capture raw output/transcript references durably, normalize findings, reseed from a ResumeBundle, and report continuity quality honestly without bluffing reopen depth.
acceptance_criteria: Copilot Tier A is truthful and test-backed; Roger can start, capture, normalize, reseed, and report degraded versus usable continuity honestly; support wording can admit Copilot as present but still bounded if deeper reopen/return is not yet proven.
notes: This bead should not claim Tier B reopen/return yet.

title: Round 06 G6.5: Deliver Copilot Tier B reopen, return, and degraded reseed fallback
description: Extend Copilot to truthful Tier B: reopen by locator/session ID, support bare-harness continuation in direct Copilot CLI, support rr return back into Roger, and degrade to reseed honestly when reopen is stale or unusable.
acceptance_criteria: Copilot can reopen by real locator/session ID, return into Roger, and degrade to reseed honestly when stale; Tier B wording is backed by real behavior rather than hopeful docs or adapter symmetry.
notes: Proof lane: provider acceptance plus real-provider smoke when support wording widens to first-class status.

title: Round 06 G6.6: Publish Copilot onboarding, operator guidance, and support-matrix truth
description:
Publish the truthful Copilot operator story only after the actual Tier A or Tier B boundaries are known. This bead documents the exact install and auth prerequisites, trusted-directory or worktree implications, required Roger policy profile, what local artifacts Roger records, what Copilot is forbidden from doing by default, how reseed, reopen, or rr return degrade when continuity is stale, and what provider tier is genuinely supported today. The goal is that an operator can decide whether to use Copilot without reading side plans or inferring hidden behavior from code.

This bead is not a marketing pass. It is the user-facing contract for Copilot once the implementation and proof lanes have done their work.
acceptance_criteria:
1. README, onboarding, help, provider matrix, and support docs describe Copilot as exactly the currently proven tier and nothing more; if only Tier A or degraded Tier B is live, the docs say that literally.
2. Operator guidance lists the Roger-imposed restrictions: no raw GitHub writes, no implicit write tools, shell, or network unless the active policy explicitly allows them, worktree-root isolation, and provider-local state as evidence rather than Roger authority.
3. Docs explain what Roger captures locally, including launch or preflight provenance, policy digest or version, audit artifacts or transcript references, and continuity state where available, plus what still requires first-launch or repair verification.
4. Validation: doc or help snapshot audit against shipped doctor, status, and help surfaces plus provider-matrix review; this bead closes only if wording matches the actual proof lane from rr-x51h.7.5 and the upstream provider-truth freeze.
notes: Do not widen Copilot claims ahead of the actual implementation and proof lane. This bead should read like an operator contract, not a roadmap or aspiration list.

title: Round 06 G6.7: Evaluate deferred ACP/MCP edge-adapter spike after direct CLI Copilot stabilizes
description:
The provider-parity/Copilot plan and the canonical plan both keep ACP/MCP as explicit later-stage integration questions rather than pretending the first Roger Copilot path should be ACP-first. That concept was still unrepresented in the graph.

This bead owns the deferred architecture spike that compares direct CLI plus hooks against ACP as a harness-control edge and MCP as a tool/context edge after the truthful direct path is proven. It exists so the repo does not lose the future-protocol question entirely while still keeping 0.1.x claims grounded in the direct CLI path.

Scope:
- evaluate whether Copilot ACP materially improves lifecycle control, continuity probes, or in-harness Roger commands without weakening safety posture
- evaluate whether any MCP exposure should be limited to Roger-owned tool/context edges rather than harness/session authority
- decide whether follow-on ACP or MCP work deserves separate beads for Copilot, Codex, Gemini, Claude Code, or editor-hosted clients such as VS Code or JetBrains
- explicitly record that no support claim widens from this spike alone

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The graph contains an explicit deferred spike for ACP/MCP protocol-edge evaluation instead of leaving the concept stranded in plan prose.
2. The bead defines the questions to answer, the safety boundaries to preserve, and the conditions that would justify follow-on implementation beads.
3. The bead states plainly that Roger remains direct-CLI-first until this spike proves ACP or MCP would reduce complexity without weakening review safety.

Validation contract: architecture-spike memo or ADR update comparing direct CLI plus hooks versus ACP/MCP against Roger session authority, auditability, continuity, and operator-safety rules.
notes: Manual user approval required before this deferred spike may be reactivated, split, or implemented. Keep the current direct-path baseline unchanged until the user explicitly approves reopening this spike.

title: Round 06 G7: Provider proof and audit hardening
description: Add the proof and audit hardening required to keep the provider wave truthful. This group owns deterministic Copilot doubles, crash/transaction recovery suites, provider acceptance expansion, outbound recovery proof, policy-digest/audit artifact visibility, and real-provider smoke lanes only where support wording requires them.
acceptance_criteria: The graph contains explicit proof beads for Copilot doubles, lifecycle recovery, outbound recovery, widened provider acceptance, support-claim/invariant wiring, and smoke/E2E-budget discipline instead of assuming implementation beads can close on adapter coverage alone
notes:
Primary sources:
- docs/PLAN_FOR_TRUTHFUL_PROVIDER_PARITY_AND_GITHUB_COPILOT_CLI.md (W6 / W7)
- docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md (G7)
- docs/TESTING.md
- docs/TEST_HARNESS_GUIDELINES.md
- docs/VALIDATION_INVARIANT_MATRIX.md

Execution posture: this group is process-first and proof-first. Agents should treat fixture families, invariant ownership, artifact preservation, and E2E-budget discipline as the authority here. If validation work exposes a missing product slice or missing recovery behavior, create a follow-on implementation bead rather than quietly widening support claims or improvising heavyweight smoke scope inside the proof bead.

title: Round 06 G7.1: Add deterministic Copilot doubles, hook fixtures, and session-state fixtures
description: Create the deterministic Copilot doubles and fixture families needed to validate launch, resume, hook payloads, transcript references, policy violations, stale locators, crash points, and local session-state files without requiring a live paid Copilot environment on every CI worker.
acceptance_criteria: The validation harness has deterministic Copilot doubles and named fixture families for launch/resume/hooks/transcripts/policy failure/crash cases; later acceptance and recovery beads can depend on these fixtures instead of inventing ad hoc mocks.
notes: Anchors: rr-025.2 and rr-025.3.

title: Round 06 G7.2: Add lifecycle crash-recovery and stale-event regression suites
description: Add the transaction/crash-recovery suites for review/resume/return and automatic reconciliation: crash between provider launch and Roger commit, crash after artifact write but before binding, stale hook event from prior launch attempt, repeated retry after partial failure, stale locator, and target/worktree mismatch.
acceptance_criteria: Roger proves that lifecycle failures fail closed and recover predictably; stale events do not bind to the wrong session; retry after partial failure is safe; transaction boundaries are defended by named suites and artifacts.
notes: This bead protects the core truthful lifecycle claim.

title: Round 06 G7.3: Expand provider acceptance matrix to match the live claims for every provider
description: Expand provider acceptance so OpenCode, Codex, Gemini, Claude Code, and Copilot each prove exactly the capability level Roger claims for them. This bead stops provider lists and docs from widening faster than acceptance coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Each provider has named acceptance coverage commensurate with its claim level; unsupported deeper capabilities fail clearly rather than pretending parity; Copilot is covered by deterministic doubles and the appropriate real-lane evidence when its claim widens.
notes: Closed anchor: rr-011.1 and earlier provider-specific beads; new work should not reopen the old validation epic as if it already covered this phase.

title: Round 06 G7.4: Add outbound invalidation, duplicate-post, and partial-post recovery proof
description:
Add the named proof layer for the explicit outbound flow once the implementation state machine exists. This bead turns invalidation, supersede or retarget handling, duplicate-post prevention, partial-post recovery, and retry metadata into invariant-owned validation with preserved failure artifacts and operator-readable closeout evidence.

This is the line that makes outbound safety a defended support claim rather than a close reason or informal smoke.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Named suites, fixtures, and invariants defend invalidation, supersede or retarget handling, duplicate-post prevention, partial-post recovery, and retry classification.
2. Failure artifacts and audit outputs are preserved well enough to explain exactly what posted, what stayed pending, and why retry is or is not allowed.
3. Release, test, and validation surfaces can point to concrete proof ownership for outbound recovery claims.
4. Validation contract: integration and recovery suites using the canonical fixture corpus plus artifact preservation consistent with the validation harness contract.
notes: This bead depends on the real outbound state machine and the fixture corpus, not on adapter happy-path coverage alone. Use rr-ph77.5 and rr-x51h.5.2 as the implementation substrate for the proof lane.

title: Round 06 G7.5: Add policy-digest status output, audit artifact classes, and invariant wiring
description: Introduce the audit artifact classes and policy-digest/status surfaces the provider-parity plan calls for, then wire the resulting claims to named invariants, suites, fixtures, and artifacts so support wording has explicit proof ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Status output can show provider, policy profile, policy digest/version, hook profile version, instructions digest, built-in MCP disabled/enabled, and worktree root where relevant; audit artifact classes are canonical; each widened claim maps to a named invariant and proof lane.
notes: This bead should make support-claim ownership explicit instead of leaving it implicit in close reasons.

title: Round 06 G7.6: Add real-provider smoke and E2E-budget gates only where support wording requires them
description:
Add real-provider smoke and release-proof only where widened support wording actually needs it. This bead names which external claims require live smoke or release rehearsal, such as first-class provider status, outbound mutation safety at a real boundary, or supported browser or bridge paths, and which bounded or internal claims remain adequately defended by cheaper proof layers. It also keeps the automated E2E budget explicit so smoke remains deliberate evidence rather than a hidden fourth validation lane.

The job here is not to add more end-to-end tests by default. The job is to make every expensive smoke lane intentional, justified, and tied to a support claim.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Every widened first-class external claim has a named smoke or release-proof lane with clear trigger conditions, owner, and artifact expectations; bounded claims that do not need smoke stay out of this lane.
2. No new heavyweight E2E is added without explicit budget justification and release or test matrix updates; smoke evidence stays scoped and limited.
3. Release, test, and public-support surfaces can point to the exact smoke or release-proof lane when wording requires it.
4. Validation contract: release or test matrix and E2E-budget audit plus one or more named smoke or rehearsal entrypoints where required.
notes: Primary inputs: RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, TEST_EXECUTION_TIERS_AND_E2E_BUDGET.md, rr-v6x, and the provider and outbound proof lanes. This bead should only widen smoke where claim language forces it; otherwise keep cheaper truthful proof layers in place.

title: Round 06 G8: Search-planner truth and degraded retrieval boundary
description: Bead the search-planner and degraded-retrieval truth that Round 06 elevated explicitly. This group owns planner-intent resolution, RecallEnvelope truth, and recovery_scan as a named degraded mode rather than a quiet fallback that turns into the de facto search path.
acceptance_criteria: The graph contains discrete beads for query-mode planning, RecallEnvelope requested-versus-resolved truth, degraded recovery_scan semantics, and the proof layer that defends those claims; no search bead in this wave is allowed to fall back to vague “improve query” language
notes:
Primary sources:
- docs/ROUND_06_PROVIDER_TRUTH_AND_BEAD_RECONCILIATION_BRIEF.md (W1B / D6)
- docs/SEARCH_MEMORY_LIFECYCLE_AND_SEMANTIC_ASSET_POLICY.md
- docs/BEAD_CREATION_INPUTS.md mandatory search-capture rules

Execution posture: give agents freedom on planner structs, retrieval-pipeline composition, ranking internals, and storage-fetch strategy as long as scope truth, degraded-mode truth, and candidate-versus-promoted memory boundaries remain explicit. This group should reward empirical design and differential testing, not lock agents into one imagined retrieval implementation.

title: Round 06 G8.3: Make recovery_scan a named degraded retrieval mode instead of a silent fallback
description: Make recovery_scan an explicit degraded retrieval mode with honest semantics, not a quiet fallback path that can silently become the main search behavior under pressure.
acceptance_criteria: When Roger falls back to recovery_scan or equivalent degraded retrieval, the envelopes and user/worker surfaces say so explicitly; degraded recovery never masquerades as healthy planned retrieval.
notes: This bead preserves the canonical degraded-mode rule from the search/memory contract.

title: Round 06 G8.5: Align rr search and robot output with planner truth
description: Extend the existing rr search command from lexical-degrade honesty to full planner-intent truth so the operator or robot ingress remains the canonical front door for search behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
1. rr search reports requested mode, resolved mode, retrieval mode, scope bucket, lane counts, and degraded reasons.
2. Robot output preserves the planner fields rather than collapsing them into final ranking only.
3. Search help and output stay aligned with the canonical recall contract.
notes: Validation contract: rr search CLI/robot snapshot and integration coverage. Existing anchor: rr-005.7.

title: Round 06 G8.6: Persist SessionBaselineSnapshot continuity for dropout, return, reseed, and worker reads
description: Search posture has to survive lifecycle edges just like provider continuity does, or every resumed session will quietly drift its recall defaults and allowed scopes.
acceptance_criteria:
1. SessionBaselineSnapshot exposes default query mode, allowed scopes, candidate visibility, prompt strategy, and degraded flags for the active session.
2. Dropout, return, reseed, and worker access can resolve the current baseline posture without guessing from recent prompts.
3. Provider continuity work consumes the same baseline truth rather than inventing its own search defaults.
notes: Validation contract: baseline continuity fixtures across CLI, worker, and provider continuity lanes.

title: Round 06 G8.7: Project canonical search truth into TUI Search/History
description: The dense operator search workbench should project the same planner and recall contract as rr search rather than growing a second search model for recall, related context, and promotion review.
acceptance_criteria:
1. TUI Search/History pivots across recall, related_context, candidate_audit, and promotion_review using the canonical RecallEnvelope semantics.
2. Candidate and contradicted memory stay visibly tentative in the TUI.
3. TUI search never invents hidden planner defaults or degraded-mode semantics.
notes: Validation contract: TUI/search integration coverage with canonical recall fixtures. Primary refs: docs/TUI_WORKSPACE_AND_OPERATOR_FLOW_CONTRACT.md.

title: Round 06 G8.8: Bind worker.search_memory and rr agent to the canonical recall contract
description: Active agents need to consume Roger search truth through Roger-owned retrieval, not provider-specific semantics or broad repo access.
acceptance_criteria:
1. worker.search_memory and rr agent return the same RecallEnvelope projections as operator surfaces.
2. Allowed scopes and memory lanes are enforced for worker retrieval.
3. Candidate/promoted memory boundaries remain explicit when workers request or propose memory follow-up.
notes: Validation contract: worker/search integration coverage with scope-bound fixtures and parity assertions. Existing anchors: INV-AGENT-001 and INV-CONTEXT-001.

title: Round 06 G8.9: Enforce candidate/promoted memory boundary and promotion_review handoff
description: Candidate memory, promoted memory, and promotion-review requests must remain visibly distinct so recall does not quietly inject tentative or contradicted memory as if it were established guidance.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Candidate, established, proven, deprecated, and anti_pattern memory posture stays explicit across search and review surfaces.
2. promotion_review and memory-review requests stay Roger-owned handoffs rather than hidden worker or provider mutation paths.
3. Warning-only or contradicted memory never injects as positive guidance by accident.
notes: Validation contract: memory-state boundary fixtures plus TUI/worker recall integration coverage.

title: Round 06 G8.10: Evaluate post-default semantic profile variants and rerank strategy
description:
The canonical plan keeps one bounded search follow-on open after the first Roger-owned semantic profile lands: when, if ever, should Roger add code-oriented, sparse, rerank, or other semantic profile variants beyond the default profile. That future profile-evolution concept was not yet represented explicitly in the bead graph.

This deferred spike owns the post-default semantic-profile evaluation. It exists to compare additional profile families only after the baseline semantic asset install, verification, hybrid retrieval, candidate/promoted memory boundary, and recall contract are stable.

Scope:
- evaluate whether code-oriented, sparse, rerank, or other profile variants materially improve Roger search enough to justify their asset and validation cost
- define the operator-facing commands, asset packaging, degraded-mode behavior, and status/doctor implications any additional profile would require
- compare profile variants against Rogers trust model, memory-promotion rules, and E2E/CI budget posture
- identify which variants should remain out of scope for 0.1.x because they add complexity without enough retrieval or operator value

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The graph contains an explicit deferred spike for semantic-profile evolution instead of leaving the concept only in the canonical-plan open-questions section.
2. The bead defines the evaluation rubric for additional profile families and the proof needed before implementation beads are created.
3. The bead states plainly that the initial Roger semantic-default profile remains the only supported baseline until this spike justifies expansion.

Validation contract: architecture or search-design memo comparing candidate semantic profile variants against retrieval quality, asset cost, degraded-mode truth, and operator-surface complexity.

title: Readiness review: verify rollout order realism
description: Evaluate whether the current rollout order and dependency spine are realistic for Roger 0.1.0, including whether major sequencing assumptions still hold.
close_reason: Rollout order realism evaluated. 7 issues identified: search/memory P3 vs Phase 2 commitment (high), rr-025 P0 blocked by rr-023 P3 (high), TUI start deferred until full CLI+refresh done (medium), OpenCode boundary validated only in rr-003.1 implementation (medium), FastEmbed asset delivery unresolved (medium), Gate A Gemini ambiguity (low-medium), 10+ bead sequential critical path with no Phase 1 parallelism (info). Full findings in Agent Mail thread 13 under topic implementation-gate-2026-03-29.

title: Fix cross-process artifact-id collisions in rr review continuation state
description:
Manual continuation-path rehearsal uncovered a deterministic cross-process ID collision in the CLI review path: repeated process invocations can generate duplicate artifact IDs (`bundle-<now_ts>-1`) and fail with `failed to persist ResumeBundle: sqlite error: UNIQUE constraint failed: artifacts.id`.

Repro commands (same repo + RR_STORE_ROOT, robot mode):
1) `rr review --pr 42 --robot` (exit 0)
2) `rr review --pr 43 --robot` (exit 0)
3) `rr review --pr 99 --provider codex --robot` (expected degraded Tier A path) -> observed exit 1 error due duplicate artifact ID.

Acceptance:
1. CLI-generated IDs used for session/run/bundle/binding are unique across separate `rr` process invocations sharing one store.
2. The codex bounded-provider review path returns the documented degraded/blocked semantics instead of internal storage errors in the above repro sequence.
3. Add lower-layer regression coverage that executes the CLI as separate processes (not only in-process `run()` calls) and asserts no duplicate-artifact-ID failure for shared-store continuation/review paths.
4. Record validation commands and outcomes in close reason.

Validation contract: integration layer via `cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke <new_process_invocation_test>` plus manual smoke replay of the repro sequence above.
notes: Blocked for now: required files packages/cli/src/lib.rs and packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs are under active exclusive reservations by PearlGorge (expires around 2026-04-07T06:46Z). Re-opened to avoid unsafe overlap; retry claim when reservations clear or coordinate handoff.
close_reason: Fixed cross-process artifact-id collision coverage by adding separate-process regression execution fallback in packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke (pass, includes separate_process_review_sequence_avoids_cross_process_artifact_id_collisions). Manual smoke repro with shared RR_STORE_ROOT: cargo run -q -p roger-cli --bin rr -- review --repo owner/repo --pr 42 --provider opencode --robot (exit 0), --pr 43 --provider opencode --robot (exit 0), --pr 99 --provider codex --robot (exit 5 degraded bounded support warning, no UNIQUE constraint).

comments.author: cdilga
comments.text: Reopened immediately after claim: active exclusive reservations by PearlGorge overlap required files (packages/cli/src/lib.rs, packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs). Avoiding unsafe concurrent edits; pivoting to another ready bead.

title: Implement CalVer GitHub Actions release system for Roger 0.1.x
description: Turn the already-approved release/install contracts into a real GitHub Actions release system for Roger. This epic exists because rr-001.2 and rr-001.3 closed as planning-definition beads: they clarified artifact classes, install/update expectations, and ownership, but they did not create or land implementation work for version derivation, matrix builds, checksum verification, publication, or release-channel automation. The repo currently has validation workflows only (.github/workflows/validation-*.yml), which means CI exists in a narrow testing sense but CD does not. This epic should deliver a truthful, GitHub-Actions-native CalVer release path that can build Roger core artifacts, package bridge/extension lanes separately, verify asset completeness and checksums, and publish tagged releases with explicit approval boundaries. Validation contract: close only after child beads land and a GitHub Actions rehearsal proves a full release candidate artifact set can be built and verified without ad hoc maintainer assembly.
notes: Reopened 2026-04-07 for graph truthfulness: child rr-xr6.5 was reopened because its recorded closeout evidence does not satisfy its own release-publish validation contract. Epic should not be considered closed again until release-publish proof is mapped explicitly.
close_reason: Epic acceptance revalidated: all implementation children are closed (`rr-xr6.1` through `rr-xr6.6`), and GitHub Actions rehearsal evidence proves end-to-end release automation without ad hoc local asset assembly. Evidence set: release-build-core success https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877905681, release-package-bridge success https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877905678, release-verify-assets success https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877984703, and release-publish success https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195. The release-publish-plan artifact for run 23878015195 records upstream run provenance, generated notes/checksums/manifests, and truthful support posture (`core_plus_bridge` with extension explicitly narrowed), satisfying the explicit publication-gate and lane-truth contract. CI-sensitive closeout evidence guard passed via scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-xr6 --run-url https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 --outcome success.

title: Define Roger CalVer tags, channels, and version-derivation contract
description: Freeze the concrete CalVer scheme Roger will use for GitHub releases and artifact names, including stable vs rc/pre-release channels, tag format, rerun/build metadata rules, Cargo workspace version interaction, and how GitHub Actions derives the canonical release version without hand edits. This bead exists because the old release beads stopped at artifact ownership and install semantics; they never answered the operational versioning question, so there is no machine-truthful source for tags, release names, archive names, or self-update lookup. Acceptance: (1) one canonical CalVer format is documented and implemented in a small Roger-owned derivation path; (2) stable/rc/nightly semantics and provenance rules are explicit; (3) artifact names and release metadata fields derive from the same source; (4) the contract states why prior rr-001.2/rr-001.3 closure was insufficient: ownership was defined, but version authority was not. Validation contract: int layer via a deterministic version-derivation test command and a CI dry-run on synthetic refs.
close_reason: Implemented CalVer contract + derivation path in roger-validation; added docs/CALVER_RELEASE_VERSIONING_CONTRACT.md and .github/workflows/release-calver-dry-run.yml; validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-validation --test calver_derivation && rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-validation && synthetic dry-run refs via cargo run -q -p roger-validation -- derive-calver (stable/rc/nightly matrix checks)

title: Add release-build-core GitHub Actions matrix for Roger companion archives
description: Implement the release-build-core lane the canonical plan and release matrix already call for. The workflow should build versioned Roger core companion archives for the blessed target matrix, stamp them with the derived CalVer version, and emit reproducible archive names plus machine-readable metadata for downstream verification and publication. This bead exists because the repo currently validates tests on ubuntu only but does not build any publishable companion archives; the old beads defined ownership but never created the actual matrix-build job. Acceptance: (1) GitHub Actions builds the required core targets or a truthful first shipped subset with explicit narrowing; (2) each archive includes rr and minimal runtime metadata; (3) filenames are versioned and stable; (4) the job exports structured outputs consumed by downstream verification/publish jobs. Validation contract: int layer via workflow/action smoke plus a release-candidate run that produces the expected archive manifest on GitHub Actions.
notes: Validation evidence (2026-04-01): gh workflow run release-build-core.yml --ref main -> runs 23821379428 and 23821455643 reproduced unsupported macOS runner lane (macos-13-us-default) and blocked aggregate-core-manifest. Applied matrix fix in .github/workflows/release-build-core.yml (x86_64-apple-darwin lane now runs on macos-14), pushed isolated validation branch brightpuma/rr-xr6-2-macos-runner-fix (commit fb0e99b), then ran gh workflow run release-build-core.yml --ref brightpuma/rr-xr6-2-macos-runner-fix -> run 23821543593 SUCCESS (derive-release-metadata + 4 build-core lanes + aggregate-core-manifest). Downloaded artifact with gh run download 23821543593 -n release-build-core-manifest; manifest release-core-manifest-2026.03.31-nightly.fb0e99bbe223.json reports schema roger.release-build-core.v1, built_target_count=4, targets={aarch64-apple-darwin,x86_64-apple-darwin,x86_64-pc-windows-msvc,x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu}, excluded_targets={aarch64-pc-windows-msvc}, matrix_scope=first_shipped_subset.
close_reason: Implemented and validated release-build-core matrix lane with truthful first-shipped subset + explicit exclusion metadata; confirmed GitHub Actions aggregate manifest generation via successful run 23821543593 on validation branch after fixing unsupported macOS runner mapping.

title: Add release-package-bridge and release-package-extension lanes with truthful optionality
description: Implement separate packaging lanes for Native Messaging registration bundles and extension sideload packages, preserving the plan requirement that browser artifacts stay optional and never get implied by the core release lane. This bead must wire contract verification for generated bridge bindings before extension packaging and must publish explicit lane metadata so release notes can say core-only vs core-plus-bridge vs core-plus-bridge-plus-extension truthfully. This bead exists because rr-007.1 froze the contract but did not create the CI/CD implementation. Acceptance: (1) bridge bundles are packaged per supported OS claim; (2) extension package generation runs only after contract-drift verification; (3) release metadata records which optional lanes were actually built; (4) a skipped lane narrows support claims rather than silently pretending parity. Validation contract: int layer via workflow smoke plus existing bridge and extension smoke tests on packaged outputs.
notes:
Implemented optional release lanes and lane-truth metadata:
- Added workflows: .github/workflows/release-package-bridge.yml, .github/workflows/release-package-extension.yml
- Added scripts: scripts/release/package_bridge_bundle.sh, scripts/release/package_extension_bundle.sh, scripts/release/build_optional_lane_summary.py
- Added validation scripts: scripts/release/test_build_optional_lane_summary.sh, scripts/release/test_package_bridge_bundle.sh

Acceptance mapping:
1) Bridge bundles per OS claim: package_bridge_bundle + matrix workflow for linux/macos/windows.
2) Extension packaging after contract verification: release-package-extension has verify-bridge-contract job as explicit upstream dependency; package_extension_bundle also runs rr bridge verify-contracts before pack.
3) Optional-lane metadata: build_optional_lane_summary emits release optional-lane summary artifact with per-lane built/skipped state and artifacts.
4) Skipped-lane narrowing: summary computes support posture (core_only/core_plus_bridge/core_plus_bridge_plus_extension) and narrowed_claims.

Local validation run:
- python3 -m py_compile scripts/release/build_optional_lane_summary.py
- bash -n scripts/release/package_bridge_bundle.sh scripts/release/package_extension_bundle.sh scripts/release/test_build_optional_lane_summary.sh scripts/release/test_package_bridge_bundle.sh
- ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/release-package-bridge.yml"); YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/release-package-extension.yml"); puts "yaml ok"'
- bash scripts/release/test_build_optional_lane_summary.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_package_bridge_bundle.sh
- rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli bridge_
- tmp smoke: derive_calver_version + bash scripts/release/package_extension_bundle.sh (zip + extension-bundle-manifest generated)

Remaining non-local acceptance piece: GitHub Actions release-lane rehearsal evidence must be captured when CI runs these workflows.
Retro-audit addendum (2026-04-07): non-local CI evidence is now recorded and the previously noted gap is satisfied.
- release-package-bridge workflow run https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877905678 completed with conclusion=success (event=push).
- release-verify-assets run https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877984703 completed success and reports optional lane status `release-package-bridge=built`, `release-package-extension=skipped` in release-asset-manifest.json.
- release-publish run https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 completed success and publish-plan artifacts record support posture `core_plus_bridge` with narrowed claim `extension_sideload_unshipped`, proving lane-truth metadata is carried into publication notes.
close_reason: Implemented optional release package lanes (bridge + extension), added lane-summary truth metadata, and validated via script smokes plus rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli bridge_.

title: Add release-verify-assets gate for checksums, manifests, and artifact completeness
description: Implement the release-verify-assets lane that recomputes SHA256SUMS, validates archive contents, checks release manifest completeness, and fails closed when any shipped asset or support claim is missing. This bead corrects the current gap where validation-release runs tests but there is no artifact-verification gate between build and publication. Acceptance: (1) every candidate release produces a SHA256SUMS file and machine-readable asset manifest; (2) verification detects missing archives, mismatched checksums, and lane-claim drift; (3) release jobs cannot publish when verification fails; (4) release notes or signing placeholders surface unsigned targets honestly instead of hiding them. Validation contract: int layer via fixture-backed asset-manifest tests plus a GitHub Actions rehearsal that intentionally exercises at least one failing verification case and one passing case.
notes:
Retro-audit evidence addendum (2026-04-07): remote run-level proof captured for the CI-sensitive verification gate.
- release-verify-assets workflow run: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877984703 (status=completed, conclusion=success, event=workflow_dispatch).
- The run artifact `release-verify-assets-report/release-asset-manifest.json` shows schema `roger.release-verify-assets.v1`, `publish_gate.publish_allowed=true`, and optional-lane summary entries present.
- Downstream publication rehearsal consumed this verify output successfully: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 (release-publish, success), proving publish gating is enforced from verified artifacts rather than fixture-only local checks.
close_reason: Implemented release-verify-assets lane with deterministic verifier + fixture rehearsals. Added scripts/release/verify_release_assets.py to validate core archive presence/checksum/content shape, optional-lane artifact availability, and support-claim drift; emits publish gate metadata plus SHA256SUMS + release-asset-manifest.json + release-notes-signing.md (unsigned targets surfaced explicitly). Added scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh covering passing fixture and intentional missing-archive, checksum-mismatch, and lane-claim-drift failures. Added .github/workflows/release-verify-assets.yml with explicit pass rehearsal (fixture-rehearsal + verify-candidate-assets) and intentional-fail rehearsal (rehearsal-intentional-fail), plus publish-gate-sentinel that only opens when verification reports publish_allowed=true. Validation: python3 -m py_compile scripts/release/verify_release_assets.py; bash -n scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh; bash scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh; ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/release-verify-assets.yml"); puts "yaml ok"'.

title: Add approval-gated GitHub release publication workflow for CalVer tags
description: Implement the release-publish lane as an explicit approval-gated GitHub Actions workflow that turns verified artifacts into draft or published GitHub releases without ad hoc local maintainer assembly. The workflow should support release-candidate rehearsal, stable publication from approved CalVer tags, and truthful notes that state which artifact lanes shipped. This bead exists because the current repo has manual validation-release only, which is not a publish system. Acceptance: (1) publication runs only from verified upstream artifacts and approved refs; (2) draft versus stable release behavior is explicit; (3) release notes include support-lane truthfulness and checksum references; (4) publication cannot bypass the verification gate. Validation contract: int/manual-release layer via GitHub Actions dry-run or draft-release rehearsal on a non-production tag plus documented operator smoke.
notes: Reopened 2026-04-07 after closeout audit: prior close reason ("fk close path repaired via pinned br binary after localfix regression isolation") does not map acceptance criteria to actual validation and does not record the required GitHub Actions dry-run/draft-release rehearsal evidence. Re-close only with explicit upstream run URL/id, outcome, publish mode, and operator-smoke evidence per rr-aip.2 and docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md.
close_reason: Acceptance mapped and revalidated for rr-xr6.5: (1) publication is gated to verified upstream artifacts + approved refs via release-publish validate_upstream_run/reverify steps and publish_release.py approved-ref/publish_gate checks; exercised by successful release-publish run https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 (workflow_dispatch, success) with upstream run provenance recorded in release-publish-plan artifact. (2) draft vs stable behavior is explicit via workflow_dispatch publish_mode (draft|publish) and publish_release.py mode policy; validated by scripts/release/test_publish_release.sh pass cases for RC draft + stable publish requiring operator_smoke_ack. (3) generated notes include support-lane truth + checksum references + run provenance; validated by scripts/release/test_publish_release.sh assertions and release-publish-plan artifact from run 23878015195 (Support Claims + Checksums lines present). (4) verification bypass is fail-closed through schema/publish_gate/run-identity/run-url/optional-lane parity checks; validated by scripts/release/test_release_publish_workflow.sh, scripts/release/test_validate_upstream_run.sh, and fail-case matrix in scripts/release/test_publish_release.sh. CI-sensitive evidence guard passed via scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-xr6.5 --run-url https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 --outcome success.

title: Implement Roger-owned installer metadata and self-update hooks against published CalVer releases
description: Implement the minimum Roger-owned release metadata consumption needed for the one-line install and update contract to become real: generated release metadata bundle shape, install-script lookup assumptions, and rr self-update plumbing against published CalVer releases. This bead exists because rr-001.3 defined install and update behavior, but there is still no shipped metadata or CLI plumbing to consume it. Acceptance: (1) release metadata bundle schema is finalized and generated by CI; (2) installer lookup rules for OS/arch/channel are aligned with publication outputs; (3) rr self-update or an equivalent internal update path can resolve and validate published metadata while failing closed on unpublished or local builds; (4) docs clearly state what is implemented now versus deferred. Validation contract: int layer via metadata-fixture tests and one end-to-end release-metadata rehearsal against a draft release or a local release-fixture mirror.
notes:
Retro-audit evidence addendum (2026-04-07): CI/release proof tied to published metadata path.
- Core metadata-producing run: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877905681 (`release-build-core`, completed/success) generated `core-install-metadata` consumed by publish lanes.
- Publish run: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 (`release-publish`, completed/success) attached verified assets for tag `v2026.04.03` and recorded upstream provenance in `release-publish-plan`.
- Live installer-readiness smoke (from rr-kz4 close evidence): at 2026-04-02T02:15:52Z, `releases/latest` resolved HTTP 200 to `v2026.04.03` and `bash scripts/release/rr-install.sh --repo cdilga/roger-reviewer --dry-run` exited 0 against published metadata.
This supplements the prior local fixture tests with explicit remote release evidence.
close_reason: Completed install-metadata contract closeout: documented rr self-update deferment + installer-driven published-update path in docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md; validated via bash scripts/release/test_build_install_metadata_bundle.sh && bash scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh && bash scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh

title: Add session-aware CLI smoke and degraded-capability coverage
description: Non-overlapping rr-018 support slice focused on smoke/degraded acceptance coverage for CLI review, resume, return, findings, status, and refresh.
acceptance_criteria:
Smoke scenarios cover review, resume, return, findings, status, and refresh from repo-local CLI entry with blessed OpenCode assumptions
Coverage includes stale locator handling that falls back to ResumeBundle or explicit recovery guidance without silent session retargeting
Coverage includes degraded or unsupported provider capability outputs and verifies they are explicit and truthful
Artifacts remain in tests/docs/fixtures lanes and do not duplicate rr-018 command-path implementation ownership
notes: Coordination note: created because br ready is empty and rr-018 is the active frontier. This bead is intentionally non-overlapping support for rr-018 and should not modify primary command routing implementation.
close_reason: Added CLI smoke/degraded coverage plus fixture metadata and passing test run

title: Keep the root README hero narrative and architecture summary aligned with shipped truth
description: Own the structural README story that explains what Roger is, how its main surfaces relate, and why approval and local-first continuity matter. This bead is intentionally earlier than the deferred release-polish pass: it keeps the prominent root-level narrative, hero framing, and architecture summary aligned with canonical truth and the live graph as implementation meaning shifts.
acceptance_criteria: The top README hero narrative, feature summary, and architecture section describe only surfaces and support levels the canonical plan and live graph can defend. README framing around local-first review, memory, approval gating, and extension scope stays synchronized with the canonical docs instead of drifting toward stale marketing shorthand. Validation contract: authority audit against PLAN_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md, AGENTS.md, root-level diagrams, and the current release-support docs.
notes: This bead does not replace rr-skdz, which remains the final release-time README truth pass against actual published assets.
close_reason: README now narrows the public root narrative to shipped truth: re-entry reconciliation is described only for rr review/rr resume/rr return; rr status and rr findings are documented as persisted readback surfaces rather than background reconciliation triggers; the command table now includes live rr search; the architecture summary now names rr agent as the bounded worker transport and narrows the extension to launch plus bounded mirror readback; and the README mermaid edge now says follow-up / re-entry reconcile instead of implying automatic background reconciliation. Validation: manual authority audit against AGENTS.md, docs/PLAN_FOR_ROGER_REVIEWER.md, docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md, and the root-level diagram contract, plus live command-surface verification via cargo run -q -p roger-cli -- --help | rg -n "status|findings|sessions|search|return|extension". No automated tests added because this is a docs-only public-story reconciliation bead; deterministic unit or integration coverage would be untruthful for README prose and diagram wording rather than executable behavior. Broader root-diagram synchronization remains separately tracked by rr-05gu/rr-gs7e.

title: Translate PJ-01 through PJ-03 into owned flow families, invariants, and validating suites
description: Map the first three persona journeys into explicit flow ownership instead of leaving them spread across multiple docs. This bead covers the main review happy path, cross-surface continuity, and TUI-first triage so each journey names its owning beads, invariants, validation lane, and current truthful support posture.
acceptance_criteria: PJ-01 through PJ-03 each map to an owning flow family, invariant set, proof lane, and truthful support statement. Any gap where a journey depends on an unowned implementation slice results in a new follow-on bead instead of silent TODO prose. Validation docs and matrices point back to the same owning beads rather than maintaining a separate persona-only truth.
notes:
Primary sources: PERSONA_JOURNEYS_AND_CHAOS_RECOVERY.md, REVIEW_FLOW_MATRIX.md, VALIDATION_INVARIANT_MATRIX.md, and RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md.

Execution answer: answer the persona questions in system terms, not in document-only terms. If a journey reveals that the current product slices do not actually defend the promised behavior, create the missing follow-on bead and tie it back to the journey. Prefer cheaper truthful unit/integration proof where it defends the claim fully; do not spend E2E budget unless the journey really needs it.
